{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_015VDwTKpULkzXmxEMHxA3NR","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Product Requirements Document\n     2→\n     3→## Project Name\n     4→\n     5→Bookmate\n     6→\n     7→## Overview\n     8→\n     9→Bookmate is a web service that helps fiction readers find companions for reading and discussing books together. Users post listings for books they want to read, others join, and once a group forms, they connect via Telegram to coordinate their reading sessions. Think of it as matchmaking for book clubs.\n    10→\n    11→**Target audience:** English-speaking fiction readers who want structured reading companionship but struggle to find like-minded partners.\n    12→\n    13→## Goals\n    14→\n    15→- Make it effortless to find people who want to read the same book at the same pace\n    16→- Remove the friction of organizing a reading group — from discovery to first meeting\n    17→- Keep the platform lightweight: match readers, hand off to Telegram for discussion\n    18→\n    19→## P0 — Must Have (Core, launch blocker)\n    20→\n    21→### User Authentication\n    22→\n    23→Email + password registration and login. Required to post or join any listing.\n    24→\n    25→### Create a Reading Listing\n    26→\n    27→A user creates a listing with:\n    28→- **Book** — selected via Open Library API (title, author, cover art auto-populated)\n    29→- **Preferred language** — language for discussion\n    30→- **Reading pace** — e.g., \"1 chapter per week\", \"50 pages per day\"\n    31→- **Start date** — when the group begins reading\n    32→- **Meeting format** — voice calls, text chat, or mixed\n    33→- **Max group size** — author sets the cap (e.g., 3–6 people)\n    34→\n    35→### Browse Listings\n    36→\n    37→Simple chronological list of open listings. No search or filters at this stage — just a scrollable feed of available reading groups to join.\n    38→\n    39→### Join a Listing\n    40→\n    41→Any authenticated user can join an open listing. First-come-first-served, no approval step. A user can be part of multiple reading groups simultaneously.\n    42→\n    43→### Group Formation\n    44→\n    45→When a listing reaches its max group size:\n    46→- The listing becomes invisible to non-participants (effectively closed)\n    47→- The author is prompted to share a Telegram group link with participants\n    48→- The author manually creates the Telegram group and pastes the invite link\n    49→\n    50→### Notifications\n    51→\n    52→Email + in-app notifications for:\n    53→- **Each new sign-up** — listing author is notified when someone joins\n    54→- **Group full** — all participants are notified when the group reaches its cap\n    55→\n    56→### User Profile (Basic)\n    57→\n    58→- Display name\n    59→- Short bio\n    60→\n    61→## P1 — Should Have (Important, not launch blocker)\n    62→\n    63→### Search and Filters\n    64→\n    65→- Search listings by book title or author\n    66→- Filter by reading pace, start date, meeting format\n    67→- Browsable feed with sorting options\n    68→\n    69→### Telegram Group Creation Assistance\n    70→\n    71→Provide guidance or tooling to help the listing author create and share the Telegram group link more easily (e.g., step-by-step instructions, link validation).\n    72→\n    73→### Extended User Profile\n    74→\n    75→- Reading history (past groups participated in)\n    76→- \"Currently reading\" list\n    77→- Favorite genres\n    78→\n    79→## P2 — Nice to Have (Bonus, if time permits)\n    80→\n    81→### Automatic Telegram Group Creation\n    82→\n    83→System auto-creates a Telegram group via API and shares the invite link with all participants — no manual step needed.\n    84→\n    85→### Applicant Approval System\n    86→\n    87→Listing author can review and approve/reject users who want to join, instead of first-come-first-served.\n    88→\n    89→### Rating and Reputation System\n    90→\n    91→Users can rate their reading group experience. Build a reputation score based on participation history and peer ratings.\n    92→\n    93→### Discord Support\n    94→\n    95→Allow group creation via Discord as an alternative to Telegram.\n    96→\n    97→## Non-Goals\n    98→\n    99→- No payment or monetization features\n   100→- No mobile app (web only)\n   101→- No real-time chat within the platform (groups communicate via Telegram)\n   102→- No complex third-party integrations beyond Open Library API\n   103→- No AI-powered recommendations or matching (AI is allowed where it genuinely improves UX)\n   104→- No content hosting (e-books, PDFs, etc.)\n   105→\n   106→## Tech Stack\n   107→\n   108→- **Frontend + Backend:** Next.js (full-stack)\n   109→- **Database:** SQLite (local, persistent across container restarts)\n   110→- **Analytics:** SQLite-based (no third-party analytics)\n   111→- **Deployment:** Docker container with mounted volume for SQLite persistence\n   112→- **Book data:** Open Library API (free, no API key required)\n   113→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"2461f1b3-284a-46b0-accd-df8549bf2929","uuid":"53da9773-7680-4538-95d4-bf9d9c0e5706","timestamp":"2026-03-24T12:00:08.782Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/PRD.md","content":"# Product Requirements Document\n\n## Project Name\n\nBookmate\n\n## Overview\n\nBookmate is a web service that helps fiction readers find companions for reading and discussing books together. Users post listings for books they want to read, others join, and once a group forms, they connect via Telegram to coordinate their reading sessions. Think of it as matchmaking for book clubs.\n\n**Target audience:** English-speaking fiction readers who want structured reading companionship but struggle to find like-minded partners.\n\n## Goals\n\n- Make it effortless to find people who want to read the same book at the same pace\n- Remove the friction of organizing a reading group — from discovery to first meeting\n- Keep the platform lightweight: match readers, hand off to Telegram for discussion\n\n## P0 — Must Have (Core, launch blocker)\n\n### User Authentication\n\nEmail + password registration and login. Required to post or join any listing.\n\n### Create a Reading Listing\n\nA user creates a listing with:\n- **Book** — selected via Open Library API (title, author, cover art auto-populated)\n- **Preferred language** — language for discussion\n- **Reading pace** — e.g., \"1 chapter per week\", \"50 pages per day\"\n- **Start date** — when the group begins reading\n- **Meeting format** — voice calls, text chat, or mixed\n- **Max group size** — author sets the cap (e.g., 3–6 people)\n\n### Browse Listings\n\nSimple chronological list of open listings. No search or filters at this stage — just a scrollable feed of available reading groups to join.\n\n### Join a Listing\n\nAny authenticated user can join an open listing. First-come-first-served, no approval step. A user can be part of multiple reading groups simultaneously.\n\n### Group Formation\n\nWhen a listing reaches its max group size:\n- The listing becomes invisible to non-participants (effectively closed)\n- The author is prompted to share a Telegram group link with participants\n- The author manually creates the Telegram group and pastes the invite link\n\n### Notifications\n\nEmail + in-app notifications for:\n- **Each new sign-up** — listing author is notified when someone joins\n- **Group full** — all participants are notified when the group reaches its cap\n\n### User Profile (Basic)\n\n- Display name\n- Short bio\n\n## P1 — Should Have (Important, not launch blocker)\n\n### Search and Filters\n\n- Search listings by book title or author\n- Filter by reading pace, start date, meeting format\n- Browsable feed with sorting options\n\n### Telegram Group Creation Assistance\n\nProvide guidance or tooling to help the listing author create and share the Telegram group link more easily (e.g., step-by-step instructions, link validation).\n\n### Extended User Profile\n\n- Reading history (past groups participated in)\n- \"Currently reading\" list\n- Favorite genres\n\n## P2 — Nice to Have (Bonus, if time permits)\n\n### Automatic Telegram Group Creation\n\nSystem auto-creates a Telegram group via API and shares the invite link with all participants — no manual step needed.\n\n### Applicant Approval System\n\nListing author can review and approve/reject users who want to join, instead of first-come-first-served.\n\n### Rating and Reputation System\n\nUsers can rate their reading group experience. Build a reputation score based on participation history and peer ratings.\n\n### Discord Support\n\nAllow group creation via Discord as an alternative to Telegram.\n\n## Non-Goals\n\n- No payment or monetization features\n- No mobile app (web only)\n- No real-time chat within the platform (groups communicate via Telegram)\n- No complex third-party integrations beyond Open Library API\n- No AI-powered recommendations or matching (AI is allowed where it genuinely improves UX)\n- No content hosting (e-books, PDFs, etc.)\n\n## Tech Stack\n\n- **Frontend + Backend:** Next.js (full-stack)\n- **Database:** SQLite (local, persistent across container restarts)\n- **Analytics:** SQLite-based (no third-party analytics)\n- **Deployment:** Docker container with mounted volume for SQLite persistence\n- **Book data:** Open Library API (free, no API key required)\n","numLines":113,"startLine":1,"totalLines":113}}}

lint clean (no warnings)\n    15→- Security hardening merged (PR #32, #36)\n    16→- **Production deployed** via PM2 on port 3000 — http://89.167.127.85:3000\n    17→- Health endpoint: `/api/health` routes (root, listing detail, profile, notifications)\n    21→- Error boundaries on all routes with retry functionality\n    22→- Focus-visible styles for keyboard accessibility\n    23→- pattern)\n    26→  - autocomplete attributes on auth forms\n    27→  - role=\"alert\" on error messages\n    28→  - Skip-to-content link\n    29→  - role=\"tablist\"/tab/tabpanel on profile tabs\n    30→  - role=\"radiogroup\"/radio on format/platform buttons\n    31→  - role=\"checkbox\" with keyboard support on approval toggle\n    32→\n    33→## Production\n    34→- **URL**: http://89.167.127.85:3000\n    35→- **Process manager**: PM2 (via npx)\n    36→- **Config**: `app/ecosystem.config.js`\n    37→- **Deploy script**: `scripts/deploy.sh`\n    38→- **Logs**: `.claude-beat/logs/pm2-{out,error}.log`\n    39→- **No reverse proxy** (nginx requires root — not available)\n    40→- **No HTTPS** (no domain configured, no certbot)\n    41→- PM2 process saved: `npx pm2 save` (restores on `pm2 resurrect`)\n    42→\n    43→## Open PRs\n    44→- None\n    45→\n    46→## Closed Recently\n    47→- #96: Code review round 11 — stale transaction data in approve, unmount cleanup, duplicate notifications — merged + deployed\n    48→- #95: Code review round 10 — FK violations, stale notifications, input validation, error UX — merged + deployed\n    49→- #93: Code review round 9 — client-side race conditions, date bugs, error handling, ARIA — merged + deployed\n    50→- #91: Code review round 8 — timing-safe secrets, type validation, stale transaction data, email HTML escaping — merged + deployed\n    51→- #87: Code review round 6 — logout fix, is_full reset, email subjects, webhook validation, fetch abort — merged + deployed\n    52→\n    53→## Open Issues\n    54→- #21: Telegram bot token (needs-human) — multiple follow-up reminders sent, still waiting\n    55→- #54: **PAT expires ~March 27** (needs-human) — GH_TOKEN renewal required, ~3 days left\n    56→\n    57→## Important Notes\n    58→- GH_TOKEN loaded from `.claude-beat/.env`\n    59→- Remote URL includes PAT: reset with `git remote set-url` if needed\n    60→- Next.js 15 used (not 16) due to Turbopack /root permission issue\n    61→- agent-browser WORKS if: export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp/runtime-$(id -u) && mkdir -p \"$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR\"\n    62→- agent-browser crashes after extended use (memory pressure) — keep recording sessions short, restart between scenes\n    63→- ffmpeg installed as static binary in ~/bin — add to PATH: export PATH=\"$HOME/bin:$PATH\"\n    64→- ELEVENLABS_API_KEY not available — demo video has no voiceover\n    65→- Demo video at .claude-beat/logs/demos/demo_2026-03-20.mp4 (5 scenes, ~1:51)\n    66→- SQLite DB stored in `app/data/` (gitignored)\n    67→- DB tables: users, listings, listing_members, listing_applications, user_genres, notifications, ratings, telegram_chats, pending_telegram_groups\n    68→- Telegram bot integration requires TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN env var (see issue #21)\n    69→- Discord bot integration requires DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN + DISCORD_CLIENT_ID + DISCORD_WEBHOOK_SECRET env vars\n    70→- Listings have `platform_preference` field (telegram/discord)\n    71→- Email notifications require SMTP env vars (SMTP_HOST, SMTP_PORT, SMTP_FROM, etc.)\n    72→- nodemailer added as dependency for email sending\n    73→- Session secret: lazy runtime check (not module-level) to avoid build failure\n    74→- ESLint: use eslint v9 + @eslint/js v9 (not v10) to avoid peer dep conflicts\n    75→- npm ci works cleanly (no --legacy-peer-deps needed)\n    76→- No root/sudo access on server — can't install nginx, Docker, or system packages\n    77→- PM2 available via npx (not globally installed)\n    78→\n    79→## Test Suite\n    80→- 198 tests across 22 test files, all passing\n    81→- Tested: auth (login, register), health, listings (browse, detail, create, join, edit, delete, telegram, discord, auto-telegram, auto-discord), notifications, profile, profile/genres, profile/reading, profile/reputation, ratings, rate-limit, telegram/setup, telegram/webhook, discord/setup, discord/webhook\n    82→- All API routes now have test coverage\n    83→- @vitest/coverage-v8 installed for coverage reporting\n    84→\n    85→## Components\n    86→- `BookCover` — reusable book cover component with error fallback, uses `unoptimized` to bypass Next.js image proxy for Open Library covers that redirect through archive.org\n    87→\n    88→## Next Session Priority\n    89→1. **URGENT**: PAT expires ~March 27 (#54) — ~3 days remaining, still needs-human\n    90→2. Agent Memory\n\n## Project\n- **Name**: Bookmate\n- **Repo**: https://github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate\n- **Project Board**: Bookmate (project #1 on TommyBrennan)\n- **Tech**: Next.js 15, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS v4, SQLite, iron-session\n- **Owner**: XanderZhu (human), TommyBrennan (GH account)\n\n## Current State\n- All PRD features implemented and merged (P0, P1, P2)\n- CI pipeline live: GitHub Actions runs lint + type check + build on push/PR to main\n- ESLint flat config with typescript-eslint, react-hooks, @next/eslint-plugin-next\n- Build passes clean, lint clean (no warnings)\n- Security hardening merged (PR #32, #36)\n- **Production deployed** via PM2 on port 3000 — http://89.167.127.85:3000\n- Health endpoint: `/api/health` (DB check, uptime, stats)\n- Custom 404 page (branded, with CTA)\n- OG + Twitter Card meta tags on listing pages (book cover, title, description)\n- Loading skeletons on all routes (root, listing detail, profile, notifications)\n- Error boundaries on all routes with retry functionality\n- Focus-visible styles for keyboard accessibility\n- **Accessibility improvements** (PR #49, merged + deployed):\n  - ARIA labels, roles, expanded states on all interactive elements\n  - Keyboard navigation on BookSearch dropdown (combobox pattern)\n  - autocomplete attributes on auth forms\n  - role=\"alert\" on error messages\n  - Skip-to-content link\n  - role=\"tablist\"/tab/tabpanel on profile tabs\n  - role=\"radiogroup\"/radio on format/platform buttons\n  - role=\"checkbox\" with keyboard support on approval toggle\n\n## Production\n- **URL**: http://89.167.127.85:3000\n- **Process manager**: PM2 (via npx)\n- **Config**: `app/ecosystem.config.js`\n- **Deploy script**: `scripts/deploy.sh`\n- **Logs**: `.claude-beat/logs/pm2-{out,error}.log`\n- **No reverse proxy** (nginx requires root — not available)\n- **No HTTPS** (no domain configured, no certbot)\n- PM2 process saved: `npx pm2 save` (restores on `pm2 resurrect`)\n\n## Open PRs\n- None\n\n## Closed Recently\n- #96: Code review round 11 — stale transaction data in approve, unmount cleanup, duplicate notifications — merged + deployed\n- #95: Code review round 10 — FK violations, stale notifications, input validation, error UX — merged + deployed\n- #93: Code review round 9 — client-side race conditions, date bugs, error handling, ARIA — merged + deployed\n- #91: Code review round 8 — timing-safe secrets, type validation, stale transaction data, email HTML escaping — merged + deployed\n- #87: Code review round 6 — logout fix, is_full reset, email subjects, webhook validation, fetch abort — merged + deployed\n\n## Open Issues\n- #21: Telegram bot token (needs-human) — multiple follow-up reminders sent, still waiting\n- #54: **PAT expires ~March 27** (needs-human) — GH_TOKEN renewal required, ~3 days left\n\n## Important Notes\n- GH_TOKEN loaded from `.claude-beat/.env`\n- Remote URL includes PAT: reset with `git remote set-url` if needed\n- Next.js 15 used (not 16) due to Turbopack /root permission issue\n- agent-browser WORKS if: export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp/runtime-$(id -u) && mkdir -p \"$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR\"\n- agent-browser crashes after extended use (memory pressure) — keep recording sessions short, restart between scenes\n- ffmpeg installed as static binary in ~/bin — add to PATH: export PATH=\"$HOME/bin:$PATH\"\n- ELEVENLABS_API_KEY not available — demo video has no voiceover\n- Demo video at .claude-beat/logs/demos/demo_2026-03-20.mp4 (5 scenes, ~1:51)\n- SQLite DB stored in `app/data/` (gitignored)\n- DB tables: users, listings, listing_members, listing_applications, user_genres, notifications, ratings, telegram_chats, pending_telegram_groups\n- Telegram bot integration requires TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN env var (see issue #21)\n- Discord bot integration requires DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN + DISCORD_CLIENT_ID + DISCORD_WEBHOOK_SECRET env vars\n- Listings have `platform_preference` field (telegram/discord)\n- Email notifications require SMTP env vars (SMTP_HOST, SMTP_PORT, SMTP_FROM, etc.)\n- nodemailer added as dependency for email sending\n- Session secret: lazy runtime check (not module-level) to avoid build failure\n- ESLint: use eslint v9 + @eslint/js v9 (not v10) to avoid peer dep conflicts\n- npm ci works cleanly (no --legacy-peer-deps needed)\n- No root/sudo access on server — can't install nginx, Docker, or system packages\n- PM2 available via npx (not globally installed)\n\n## Test Suite\n- 198 tests across 22 test files, all passing\n- Tested: auth (login, register), health, listings (browse, detail, create, join, edit, delete, telegram, discord, auto-telegram, auto-discord), notifications, profile, profile/genres, profile/reading, profile/reputation, ratings, rate-limit, telegram/setup, telegram/webhook, discord/setup, discord/webhook\n- All API routes now have test coverage\n- @vitest/coverage-v8 installed for coverage reporting\n\n## Components\n- `BookCover` — reusable book cover component with error fallback, uses `unoptimized` to bypass Next.js image proxy for Open Library covers that redirect through archive.org\n\n## Next Session Priority\n1. **URGENT**: PAT expires ~March 27 (#54) — ~3 days remaining, still needs-human\n2. Check issue #21 for Telegram token response\n3. Check production health\n4. All routes tested, 11 rounds of code review done — consider E2E testing, performance optimization, or new feature proposals\n","numLines":93,"startLine":1,"totalLines":93}}}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_016VaS9kZxawADJVuwyphuGN","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Learned Patterns\n     2→\n     3→## GitHub Authentication\n     4→- Always export GH_TOKEN from .env before running gh commands:\n     5→  ```bash\n     6→  export GH_TOKEN=$(grep GH_TOKEN /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/.env | cut -d= -f2)\n     7→  ```\n     8→- Cannot `--add-reviewer` when token owner is the PR author (HTTP 422)\n     9→- Repo created under TommyBrennan (not XanderZhu — can't create for other users)\n    10→\n    11→## Session Flow\n    12→- First session bootstraps repo, creates issues from PRD as `approved` + `prd`\n    13→- PRD features are pre-approved — no proposal step needed\n    14→- Focus on housekeeping when blocked: organize project board, update memory\n    15→\n    16→## Environment\n    17→- Find agent-browser with: `which agent-browser`\n    18→- agent-browser requires `XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp` prefix due to socket dir permissions\n    19→- Playwright chromium needs manual install: `npx playwright install chromium`\n    20→- Git remote uses PAT in URL: `https://ghp_...@github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate.git`\n    21→- Git remote URL may lose PAT between sessions — re-set with `git remote set-url`\n    22→- Must set `git config user.email` and `user.name` each session if not set globally\n    23→\n    24→## Build\n    25→- Next.js 16 Turbopack fails in this environment (can't read /root dir)\n    26→- Use Next.js 15 which defaults to webpack\n    27→- Build from app/ directory: `cd app && npx next build`\n    28→- `useRef<T>()` needs explicit `undefined` arg in React 19: `useRef<T>(undefined)`\n    29→- better-sqlite3 `.all()` returns `unknown[]` — cast explicitly for TS\n    30→\n    31→## Browser Testing\n    32→- agent-browser WORKS with: `export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp/runtime-$(id -u) && mkdir -p \"$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR\"`\n    33→- Crashes after extended use (~5+ min) due to Chromium memory pressure\n    34→- Restart between recording scenes: `agent-browser close && rm -rf $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/agent-browser/ && sleep 2`\n    35→- For snapshot timeouts on pages with many elements (e.g., book search dropdown), use `agent-browser screenshot` or JS eval instead\n    36→- Book search results can't use `find text` (multiple matches) — use JS: `document.querySelector(\"button[type=button].w-full\").click()`\n    37→- Reading pace input ref (@e17) times out — use `agent-browser eval` with nativeInputValueSetter to set values\n    38→- For API testing: `curl -s URL | python3 -m json.tool`\n    39→- ffmpeg: static binary at ~/bin/ffmpeg — `export PATH=\"$HOME/bin:$PATH\"` needed each session\n    40→- When recording demos: login user BEFORE starting recording, then navigate to target page\n    41→\n    42→## Deployment\n    43→- No root/sudo access — can't apt-get install, create systemd services, or install nginx\n    44→- PM2 available via npx (not global): `npx pm2 start/stop/restart/list/logs`\n    45→- Deploy script: `./scripts/deploy.sh` (pull, install, build, restart PM2)\n    46→- Production runs on port 3000 directly (no reverse proxy)\n    47→- Public IP: 89.167.127.85 (IPv4), 2a01:4f9:c013:600f::1 (IPv6)\n    48→- Kill stale dev servers before deploy: check `ss -tlnp | grep 3000`\n    49→- PM2 ecosystem config at `app/ecosystem.config.js`\n    50→- PM2 logs at `.claude-beat/logs/pm2-{out,error}.log`\n    51→\n    52→## React + SQLite\n    53→- SQLite stores booleans as integers (0/1). In JSX, `{0 && <Component />}` renders \"0\" as text. Always use `!!value` or `Boolean(value)` for numeric fields in `&&` conditionals.\n    54→- Git `add` with bracket directories like `[id]` needs escaping or use from project root\n    55→\n    56→## Security\n    57→- Telegram webhook commands (/link, /link_ID) must verify the sender is the listing author via telegram_user_links table\n    58→- Always validate external URLs before storing (e.g., book_cover_url must start with https://covers.openlibrary.org/)\n    59→- Notification failures should never mask successful DB transactions — wrap in separate try/catch\n    60→- Move related DB writes inside the same transaction for atomicity (e.g., rejection of pending apps when group fills)\n    61→- Query string parameters from URL are always strings — parseInt() before DB queries on integer columns\n    62→\n    63→## Working Directory\n    64→- Project root: /root/Projects/bookmate\n    65→- App directory: /root/Projects/bookmate/app\n    66→- Always use full paths for .claude-beat/.env references\n    67→\n    68→## Security\n    69→- Use `crypto.timingSafeEqual` for secret comparisons (not `!==`) to prevent timing oracle attacks\n    70→- Always `typeof` check JSON body fields before string operations — `parseInt(obj)` silently coerces objects via `.toString()`\n    71→- HTML-escape user-controlled content before embedding in email HTML body\n    72→- Re-read mutable DB values inside transactions to prevent stale data from concurrent writes\n    73→- `lib/crypto-utils.ts` provides `safeCompare()` and `escapeHtmlForEmail()` utilities\n    74→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"2461f1b3-284a-46b0-accd-df8549bf2929","uuid":"688ac3cd-e4c9-4aa7-a80b-1835a9c4069e","timestamp":"2026-03-24T12:00:16.204Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/memory/patterns.md","content":"# Learned Patterns\n\n## GitHub Authentication\n- Always export GH_TOKEN from .env before running gh commands:\n  ```bash\n  export GH_TOKEN=$(grep GH_TOKEN /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/.env | cut -d= -f2)\n  ```\n- Cannot `--add-reviewer` when token owner is the PR author (HTTP 422)\n- Repo created under TommyBrennan (not XanderZhu — can't create for other users)\n\n## Session Flow\n- First session bootstraps repo, creates issues from PRD as `approved` + `prd`\n- PRD features are pre-approved — no proposal step needed\n- Focus on housekeeping when blocked: organize project board, update memory\n\n## Environment\n- Find agent-browser with: `which agent-browser`\n- agent-browser requires `XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp` prefix due to socket dir permissions\n- Playwright chromium needs manual install: `npx playwright install chromium`\n- Git remote uses PAT in URL: `https://ghp_...@github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate.git`\n- Git remote URL may lose PAT between sessions — re-set with `git remote set-url`\n- Must set `git config user.email` and `user.name` each session if not set globally\n\n## Build\n- Next.js 16 Turbopack fails in this environment (can't read /root dir)\n- Use Next.js 15 which defaults to webpack\n- Build from app/ directory: `cd app && npx next build`\n- `useRef<T>()` needs explicit `undefined` arg in React 19: `useRef<T>(undefined)`\n- better-sqlite3 `.all()` returns `unknown[]` — cast explicitly for TS\n\n## Browser Testing\n- agent-browser WORKS with: `export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp/runtime-$(id -u) && mkdir -p \"$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR\"`\n- Crashes after extended use (~5+ min) due to Chromium memory pressure\n- Restart between recording scenes: `agent-browser close && rm -rf $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/agent-browser/ && sleep 2`\n- For snapshot timeouts on pages with many elements (e.g., book search dropdown), use `agent-browser screenshot` or JS eval instead\n- Book search results can't use `find text` (multiple matches) — use JS: `document.querySelector(\"button[type=button].w-full\").click()`\n- Reading pace input ref (@e17) times out — use `agent-browser eval` with nativeInputValueSetter to set values\n- For API testing: `curl -s URL | python3 -m json.tool`\n- ffmpeg: static binary at ~/bin/ffmpeg — `export PATH=\"$HOME/bin:$PATH\"` needed each session\n- When recording demos: login user BEFORE starting recording, then navigate to target page\n\n## Deployment\n- No root/sudo access — can't apt-get install, create systemd services, or install nginx\n- PM2 available via npx (not global): `npx pm2 start/stop/restart/list/logs`\n- Deploy script: `./scripts/deploy.sh` (pull, install, build, restart PM2)\n- Production runs on port 3000 directly (no reverse proxy)\n- Public IP: 89.167.127.85 (IPv4), 2a01:4f9:c013:600f::1 (IPv6)\n- Kill stale dev servers before deploy: check `ss -tlnp | grep 3000`\n- PM2 ecosystem config at `app/ecosystem.config.js`\n- PM2 logs at `.claude-beat/logs/pm2-{out,error}.log`\n\n## React + SQLite\n- SQLite stores booleans as integers (0/1). In JSX, `{0 && <Component />}` renders \"0\" as text. Always use `!!value` or `Boolean(value)` for numeric fields in `&&` conditionals.\n- Git `add` with bracket directories like `[id]` needs escaping or use from project root\n\n## Security\n- Telegram webhook commands (/link, /link_ID) must verify the sender is the listing author via telegram_user_links table\n- Always validate external URLs before storing (e.g., book_cover_url must start with https://covers.openlibrary.org/)\n- Notification failures should never mask successful DB transactions — wrap in separate try/catch\n- Move related DB writes inside the same transaction for atomicity (e.g., rejection of pending apps when group fills)\n- Query string parameters from URL are always strings — parseInt() before DB queries on integer columns\n\n## Working Directory\n- Project root: /root/Projects/bookmate\n- App directory: /root/Projects/bookmate/app\n- Always use full paths for .claude-beat/.env references\n\n## Security\n- Use `crypto.timingSafeEqual` for secret comparisons (not `!==`) to prevent timing oracle attacks\n- Always `typeof` check JSON body fields before string operations — `parseInt(obj)` silently coerces objects via `.toString()`\n- HTML-escape user-controlled content before embedding in email HTML body\n- Re-read mutable DB values inside transactions to prevent stale data from concurrent writes\n- `lib/crypto-utils.ts` provides `safeCompare()` and `escapeHtmlForEmail()` utilities\n","numLines":74,"startLine":1,"totalLines":74}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01XPbanFuk6S8xQGJhyxeE2o","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Session Log — 2026-03-24 11:00 UTC\n     2→\n     3→## Summary\n     4→Code review round 14: found and fixed 7 bugs across env config, session auth, transaction safety, and input validation. Created issue #102, implemented fixes in PR #103, CI passed, merged to main, deployed to production.\n     5→\n     6→## Review Findings\n     7→- **Production**: Healthy — HTTP 200, PM2 online, 201 tests pass\n     8→- **CI**: Green on main\n     9→- **Open PRs**: None\n    10→- **Open issues**: #54 (needs-human, PAT expiry), #21 (needs-human, Telegram token)\n    11→- **PRD sync**: All PRD features have corresponding closed issues\n    12→\n    13→## Work Completed\n    14→\n    15→### Issue #102: Code review round 14 (PR #103 — merged + deployed)\n    16→\n    17→7 fixes:\n    18→\n    19→1. **BOT_TOKEN/CLIENT_ID frozen at module load time** (CRITICAL) — `telegram.ts`, `discord.ts` captured env vars into module-level constants at import time. If vars weren't set at that moment, `isBotConfigured()` permanently returned false and API calls used empty tokens. Fixed by reading `process.env` live inside helper functions.\n    20→\n    21→2. **`isConfigured` frozen at module load in email.ts** (IMPORTANT) — Same pattern as above for SMTP config. Fixed with `checkConfigured()` function that reads `process.env` on each call.\n    22→\n    23→3. **`requireAuth` never verified user existence** (CRITICAL) — Only checked `session.userId` was truthy in the cookie. A deleted user retained full access. Fixed by adding a DB existence check and auto-syncing stale `displayName` from the cookie.\n    24→\n    25→4. **`notifyGroupFull` re-queried `platform_preference` outside transaction** (IMPORTANT) — Between join transaction commit and this query, a concurrent PATCH could change the platform. Fixed by passing `bookTitle` and `platformPreference` as parameters from the caller (captured before/inside the transaction).\n    26→\n    27→5. **PATCH listing TOCTOU on platform link guard** (IMPORTANT) — The check preventing edits after a Telegram/Discord link is shared was outside the transaction. A concurrent POST could set the link in between. Fixed by re-checking inside the `updateTransaction`.\n    28→\n    29→6. **Profile PATCH missing type validation** (IMPORTANT) — `displayName` and `bio` were `.trim()`-ed without `typeof` check, causing unhandled TypeError on non-string input. Fixed with type guards.\n    30→\n    31→7. **Test updated** — `notifyGroupFull` mock expectation updated for new 3-param signature.\n    32→\n    33→All 201 tests pass, ESLint clean, build clean, CI green.\n    34→\n    35→## Also Noted (not fixed this round)\n    36→- No `ON DELETE CASCADE` on FK constraints (requires schema migration)\n    37→- Discord webhook allows any listing to be linked to any guild by secret-holder (architectural, not a pure bug)\n    38→\n    39→## Issues Monitored\n    40→- **#54 (PAT expiry)**: Critical — needs-human\n    41→- **#21 (Telegram token)**: Still needs-human\n    42→\n    43→## Next Session Priority\n    44→1. Check production health\n    45→2. Monitor PAT expiry (#54) — critical\n    46→3. Consider ON DELETE CASCADE migration or new feature proposals\n    47→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"2461f1b3-284a-46b0-accd-df8549bf2929","uuid":"b750e6cc-8ecd-458b-88c7-df9e01d1b45f","timestamp":"2026-03-24T12:00:17.128Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/logs/session-2026-03-24-1100.md","content":"# Session Log — 2026-03-24 11:00 UTC\n\n## Summary\nCode review round 14: found and fixed 7 bugs across env config, session auth, transaction safety, and input validation. Created issue #102, implemented fixes in PR #103, CI passed, merged to main, deployed to production.\n\n## Review Findings\n- **Production**: Healthy — HTTP 200, PM2 online, 201 tests pass\n- **CI**: Green on main\n- **Open PRs**: None\n- **Open issues**: #54 (needs-human, PAT expiry), #21 (needs-human, Telegram token)\n- **PRD sync**: All PRD features have corresponding closed issues\n\n## Work Completed\n\n### Issue #102: Code review round 14 (PR #103 — merged + deployed)\n\n7 fixes:\n\n1. **BOT_TOKEN/CLIENT_ID frozen at module load time** (CRITICAL) — `telegram.ts`, `discord.ts` captured env vars into module-level constants at import time. If vars weren't set at that moment, `isBotConfigured()` permanently returned false and API calls used empty tokens. Fixed by reading `process.env` live inside helper functions.\n\n2. **`isConfigured` frozen at module load in email.ts** (IMPORTANT) — Same pattern as above for SMTP config. Fixed with `checkConfigured()` function that reads `process.env` on each call.\n\n3. **`requireAuth` never verified user existence** (CRITICAL) — Only checked `session.userId` was truthy in the cookie. A deleted user retained full access. Fixed by adding a DB existence check and auto-syncing stale `displayName` from the cookie.\n\n4. **`notifyGroupFull` re-queried `platform_preference` outside transaction** (IMPORTANT) — Between join transaction commit and this query, a concurrent PATCH could change the platform. Fixed by passing `bookTitle` and `platformPreference` as parameters from the caller (captured before/inside the transaction).\n\n5. **PATCH listing TOCTOU on platform link guard** (IMPORTANT) — The check preventing edits after a Telegram/Discord link is shared was outside the transaction. A concurrent POST could set the link in between. Fixed by re-checking inside the `updateTransaction`.\n\n6. **Profile PATCH missing type validation** (IMPORTANT) — `displayName` and `bio` were `.trim()`-ed without `typeof` check, causing unhandled TypeError on non-string input. Fixed with type guards.\n\n7. **Test updated** — `notifyGroupFull` mock expectation updated for new 3-param signature.\n\nAll 201 tests pass, ESLint clean, build clean, CI green.\n\n## Also Noted (not fixed this round)\n- No `ON DELETE CASCADE` on FK constraints (requires schema migration)\n- Discord webhook allows any listing to be linked to any guild by secret-holder (architectural, not a pure bug)\n\n## Issues Monitored\n- **#54 (PAT expiry)**: Critical — needs-human\n- **#21 (Telegram token)**: Still needs-human\n\n## Next Session Priority\n1. Check production health\n2. Monitor PAT expiry (#54) — critical\n3. Consider ON DELETE CASCADE migration or new feature proposals\n","numLines":47,"startLine":1,"totalLines":47}}}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_0148Tt9dG61a2eX7BTFfuazY","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Session Log — 2026-03-24 10:00 UTC\n     2→\n     3→## Summary\n     4→Code review round 13: found and fixed 3 bugs in PATCH endpoint and application lifecycle. Created issue #100, implemented fixes in PR #101, CI passed, merged to main, deployed to production.\n     5→\n     6→## Review Findings\n     7→- **Production**: Healthy — HTTP 200, PM2 online, 201 tests pass\n     8→- **CI**: Green on main\n     9→- **Open PRs**: None\n    10→- **Open issues**: #54 (needs-human, PAT expiry), #21 (needs-human, Telegram token)\n    11→- **PRD sync**: All PRD features have corresponding closed issues\n    12→\n    13→## Work Completed\n    14→\n    15→### Issue #100: Code review round 13 (PR #101 — merged + deployed)\n    16→\n    17→3 fixes:\n    18→\n    19→1. **`is_full` never set to 1 when `maxGroupSize` reduced to equal member count** (CRITICAL) — The PATCH handler only checked `< currentMemberCount` (reject) and `> currentMemberCount && is_full` (reopen), but missed the `=== currentMemberCount` case. When an author reduced group size to exactly the current count, `is_full` stayed 0, leaving the listing visible in browse and appearing joinable. Added the missing equality branch.\n    20→\n    21→2. **Transaction mutates outer `updates`/`values` arrays** (CRITICAL) — The `updates` and `values` arrays were declared outside the `db.transaction()` closure but mutated inside it via `.push()`. Structurally unsafe — any retry or double-call would corrupt the SQL with duplicate bindings. Fixed by creating local copies (`txUpdates`/`txValues`) inside the transaction.\n    22→\n    23→3. **Users permanently locked out when `requires_approval` toggled off** (IMPORTANT) — When an author toggled `requires_approval` from true to false, users with pending applications became stuck: invisible to the author (gated by `requires_approval` check), blocked from direct join (pending app check), and blocked from re-apply (flag check). Fixed by auto-rejecting pending applications in the PATCH transaction when `requires_approval` is toggled off, and showing pending applicants to the author regardless of the flag.\n    24→\n    25→All 201 tests pass (3 new), ESLint clean, build clean, CI green.\n    26→\n    27→## Also Noted (not fixed this round)\n    28→- `notifyGroupFull` reads `platform_preference` outside the join transaction\n    29→- `BOT_TOKEN` / `CLIENT_ID` captured at module load time\n    30→- No `ON DELETE CASCADE` on FK constraints\n    31→- `requireAuth` does not re-validate user existence in DB (low risk — no admin deletion UI)\n    32→\n    33→## Issues Monitored\n    34→- **#54 (PAT expiry)**: Critical — needs-human\n    35→- **#21 (Telegram token)**: Still needs-human\n    36→\n    37→## Next Session Priority\n    38→1. Check production health\n    39→2. Monitor PAT expiry (#54) — critical\n    40→3. Consider E2E testing, performance improvements, or new feature proposals\n    41→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"2461f1b3-284a-46b0-accd-df8549bf2929","uuid":"98d4a860-1797-4735-b3e0-6e1c7f9c9e6a","timestamp":"2026-03-24T12:00:17.126Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/logs/session-2026-03-24-1000.md","content":"# Session Log — 2026-03-24 10:00 UTC\n\n## Summary\nCode review round 13: found and fixed 3 bugs in PATCH endpoint and application lifecycle. Created issue #100, implemented fixes in PR #101, CI passed, merged to main, deployed to production.\n\n## Review Findings\n- **Production**: Healthy — HTTP 200, PM2 online, 201 tests pass\n- **CI**: Green on main\n- **Open PRs**: None\n- **Open issues**: #54 (needs-human, PAT expiry), #21 (needs-human, Telegram token)\n- **PRD sync**: All PRD features have corresponding closed issues\n\n## Work Completed\n\n### Issue #100: Code review round 13 (PR #101 — merged + deployed)\n\n3 fixes:\n\n1. **`is_full` never set to 1 when `maxGroupSize` reduced to equal member count** (CRITICAL) — The PATCH handler only checked `< currentMemberCount` (reject) and `> currentMemberCount && is_full` (reopen), but missed the `=== currentMemberCount` case. When an author reduced group size to exactly the current count, `is_full` stayed 0, leaving the listing visible in browse and appearing joinable. Added the missing equality branch.\n\n2. **Transaction mutates outer `updates`/`values` arrays** (CRITICAL) — The `updates` and `values` arrays were declared outside the `db.transaction()` closure but mutated inside it via `.push()`. Structurally unsafe — any retry or double-call would corrupt the SQL with duplicate bindings. Fixed by creating local copies (`txUpdates`/`txValues`) inside the transaction.\n\n3. **Users permanently locked out when `requires_approval` toggled off** (IMPORTANT) — When an author toggled `requires_approval` from true to false, users with pending applications became stuck: invisible to the author (gated by `requires_approval` check), blocked from direct join (pending app check), and blocked from re-apply (flag check). Fixed by auto-rejecting pending applications in the PATCH transaction when `requires_approval` is toggled off, and showing pending applicants to the author regardless of the flag.\n\nAll 201 tests pass (3 new), ESLint clean, build clean, CI green.\n\n## Also Noted (not fixed this round)\n- `notifyGroupFull` reads `platform_preference` outside the join transaction\n- `BOT_TOKEN` / `CLIENT_ID` captured at module load time\n- No `ON DELETE CASCADE` on FK constraints\n- `requireAuth` does not re-validate user existence in DB (low risk — no admin deletion UI)\n\n## Issues Monitored\n- **#54 (PAT expiry)**: Critical — needs-human\n- **#21 (Telegram token)**: Still needs-human\n\n## Next Session Priority\n1. Check production health\n2. Monitor PAT expiry (#54) — critical\n3. Consider E2E testing, performance improvements, or new feature proposals\n","numLines":41,"startLine":1,"totalLines":41}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01NuyxJwnhfCAEpN5EtikqEo","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import Database from \"better-sqlite3\";\n     2→import path from \"path\";\n     3→import fs from \"fs\";\n     4→\n     5→const DB_DIR = process.env.DB_DIR || path.join(process.cwd(), \"data\");\n     6→const DB_PATH = path.join(DB_DIR, \"bookmate.db\");\n     7→\n     8→// Ensure data directory exists\n     9→if (!fs.existsSync(DB_DIR)) {\n    10→  fs.mkdirSync(DB_DIR, { recursive: true });\n    11→}\n    12→\n    13→const db = new Database(DB_PATH);\n    14→\n    15→// Enable WAL mode for better concurrent performance\n    16→db.pragma(\"journal_mode = WAL\");\n    17→db.pragma(\"foreign_keys = ON\");\n    18→\n    19→// Initialize schema\n    20→db.exec(`\n    21→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (\n    22→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    23→    email TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,\n    24→    password_hash TEXT NOT NULL,\n    25→    display_name TEXT NOT NULL,\n    26→    bio TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    27→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))\n    28→  );\n    29→\n    30→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listings (\n    31→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    32→    author_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    33→    book_title TEXT NOT NULL,\n    34→    book_author TEXT NOT NULL,\n    35→    book_cover_url TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    36→    book_olid TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    37→    language TEXT DEFAULT 'English',\n    38→    reading_pace TEXT NOT NULL,\n    39→    start_date TEXT NOT NULL,\n    40→    meeting_format TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(meeting_format IN ('voice', 'text', 'mixed')),\n    41→    max_group_size INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK(max_group_size >= 2 AND max_group_size <= 20),\n    42→    telegram_link TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    43→    is_full INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\n    44→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    45→    FOREIGN KEY (author_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    46→  );\n    47→\n    48→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listing_members (\n    49→    listing_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    50→    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    51→    joined_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    52→    PRIMARY KEY (listing_id, user_id),\n    53→    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id),\n    54→    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    55→  );\n    56→\n    57→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS user_genres (\n    58→    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    59→    genre TEXT NOT NULL,\n    60→    PRIMARY KEY (user_id, genre),\n    61→    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    62→  );\n    63→\n    64→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS notifications (\n    65→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    66→    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    67→    listing_id INTEGER,\n    68→    type TEXT NOT NULL,\n    69→    message TEXT NOT NULL,\n    70→    is_read INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\n    71→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    72→    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id),\n    73→    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id)\n    74→  );\n    75→\n    76→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listing_applications (\n    77→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    78→    listing_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    79→    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    80→    status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending'\n    81→      CHECK(status IN ('pending', 'approved', 'rejected')),\n    82→    message TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    83→    applied_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    84→    decided_at TEXT,\n    85→    UNIQUE(listing_id, user_id),\n    86→    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id),\n    87→    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    88→  );\n    89→`);\n    90→\n    91→// Idempotent migration: add requires_approval column\n    92→try {\n    93→  db.exec(\"ALTER TABLE listings ADD COLUMN requires_approval INTEGER DEFAULT 0\");\n    94→} catch {\n    95→  // Column already exists — safe to ignore\n    96→}\n    97→\n    98→// Idempotent migration: add platform_preference column\n    99→try {\n   100→  db.exec(\"ALTER TABLE listings ADD COLUMN platform_preference TEXT DEFAULT 'telegram'\");\n   101→} catch {\n   102→  // Column already exists — safe to ignore\n   103→}\n   104→\n   105→// Idempotent migration: add discord_link column\n   106→try {\n   107→  db.exec(\"ALTER TABLE listings ADD COLUMN discord_link TEXT DEFAULT ''\");\n   108→} catch {\n   109→  // Column already exists — safe to ignore\n   110→}\n   111→\n   112→// Idempotent migration: add discord_channel_id column\n   113→try {\n   114→  db.exec(\"ALTER TABLE listings ADD COLUMN discord_channel_id TEXT DEFAULT ''\");\n   115→} catch {\n   116→  // Column already exists — safe to ignore\n   117→}\n   118→\n   119→// Idempotent migration: add telegram_chat_id column for auto-telegram feature\n   120→try {\n   121→  db.exec(\"ALTER TABLE listings ADD COLUMN telegram_chat_id INTEGER\");\n   122→} catch {\n   123→  // Column already exists — safe to ignore\n   124→}\n   125→\n   126→// Tables for Telegram bot integration\n   127→db.exec(`\n   128→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS pending_telegram_groups (\n   129→    listing_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n   130→ Database from \"better-sqlite3\";\nimport path from \"path\";\nimport fs from \"fs\";\n\nconst DB_DIR = process.env.DB_DIR || path.join(process.cwd(), \"data\");\nconst DB_PATH = path.join(DB_DIR, \"bookmate.db\");\n\n// Ensure data directory exists\nif (!fs.existsSync(DB_DIR)) {\n  fs.mkdirSync(DB_DIR, { recursive: true });\n}\n\nconst db = new Database(DB_PATH);\n\n// Enable WAL mode for better concurrent performance\ndb.pragma(\"journal_mode = WAL\");\ndb.pragma(\"foreign_keys = ON\");\n\n// Initialize schema\ndb.exec(`\n  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (\n    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    email TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,\n    password_hash TEXT NOT NULL,\n    display_name TEXT NOT NULL,\n    bio TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))\n  );\n\n  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listings (\n    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    author_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    book_title TEXT NOT NULL,\n    book_author TEXT NOT NULL,\n    book_cover_url TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    book_olid TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    language TEXT DEFAULT 'English',\n    reading_pace TEXT NOT NULL,\n    start_date TEXT NOT NULL,\n    meeting_format TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(meeting_format IN ('voice', 'text', 'mixed')),\n    max_group_size INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK(max_group_size >= 2 AND max_group_size <= 20),\n    telegram_link TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    is_full INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\n    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    FOREIGN KEY (author_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n  );\n\n  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listing_members (\n    listing_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    joined_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    PRIMARY KEY (listing_id, user_id),\n    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id),\n    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n  );\n\n  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS user_genres (\n    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    genre TEXT NOT NULL,\n    PRIMARY KEY (user_id, genre),\n    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n  );\n\n  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS notifications (\n    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    listing_id INTEGER,\n    type TEXT NOT NULL,\n    message TEXT NOT NULL,\n    is_read INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\n    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id),\n    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id)\n  );\n\n  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listing_applications (\n    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    listing_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending'\n      CHECK(status IN ('pending', 'approved', 'rejected')),\n    message TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    applied_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    decided_at TEXT,\n    UNIQUE(listing_id, user_id),\n    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id),\n    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n  );\n`);\n\n// Idempotent migration: add requires_approval column\ntry {\n  db.exec(\"ALTER TABLE listings ADD COLUMN requires_approval INTEGER DEFAULT 0\");\n} catch {\n  // Column already exists — safe to ignore\n}\n\n// Idempotent migration: add platform_preference column\ntry {\n  db.exec(\"ALTER TABLE listings ADD COLUMN platform_preference TEXT DEFAULT 'telegram'\");\n} catch {\n  // Column already exists — safe to ignore\n}\n\n// Idempotent migration: add discord_link column\ntry {\n  db.exec(\"ALTER TABLE listings ADD COLUMN discord_link TEXT DEFAULT ''\");\n} catch {\n  // Column already exists — safe to ignore\n}\n\n// Idempotent migration: add discord_channel_id column\ntry {\n  db.exec(\"ALTER TABLE listings ADD COLUMN discord_channel_id TEXT DEFAULT ''\");\n} catch {\n  // Column already exists — safe to ignore\n}\n\n// Idempotent migration: add telegram_chat_id column for auto-telegram feature\ntry {\n  db.exec(\"ALTER TABLE listings ADD COLUMN telegram_chat_id INTEGER\");\n} catch {\n  // Column already exists — safe to ignore\n}\n\n// Tables for Telegram bot integration\ndb.exec(`\n  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS pending_telegram_groups (\n    listing_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    telegram_chat_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    PRIMARY KEY (listing_id),\n    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id)\n  );\n\n  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS telegram_chats (\n    chat_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,\n    chat_title TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))\n  );\n\n  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS telegram_user_links (\n    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    telegram_user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    PRIMARY KEY (user_id),\n    UNIQUE (telegram_user_id),\n    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n  );\n`);\n\n// Rating and Reputation system\ndb.exec(`\n  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS ratings (\n    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    listing_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    rater_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    rated_user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    score INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK(score >= 1 AND score <= 5),\n    comment TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    UNIQUE(listing_id, rater_id, rated_user_id),\n    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id),\n    FOREIGN KEY (rater_id) REFERENCES users(id),\n    FOREIGN KEY (rated_user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n  );\n`);\n\n// Performance indexes on high-frequency foreign key columns\ndb.exec(`\n  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_listing_members_user_id ON listing_members(user_id);\n  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_notifications_user_id ON notifications(user_id);\n  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_ratings_rated_user_id ON ratings(rated_user_id);\n  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_listings_author_id ON listings(author_id);\n  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_listing_applications_listing_status ON listing_applications(listing_id, status);\n  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_notifications_user_unread ON notifications(user_id, is_read);\n  CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_pending_telegram_chat_id ON pending_telegram_groups(telegram_chat_id);\n`);\n\nexport default db;\n","numLines":181,"startLine":1,"totalLines":181}}}

The FK constraints don't have ON DELETE CASCADE. In SQLite, you can't ALTER TABLE to change FK constraints - you need to recreate the table. This is a significant migration.\n\nHowever, since there's no admin UI for deleting users, and listings are never truly deleted (they just get marked full), the ON DELETE CASCADE issue is more theoretical than practical. Let me think about whether this is really worth doing...\n\nActually, the real risk is: if somehow a user or listing gets deleted (via direct DB manipulation or future feature), orphaned records would remain. But recreating all tables in SQLite is risky with production data.\n \nGiven we've already done 14 rounds of code review and covered all the P1 and P2 features, I think a production walkthrough with the browser agent would be more valuable than another code review pass. Let me check for any actual UX issues in the live application. I'm realizing we've covered a lot of ground across 14 review rounds—race conditions, auth issues, input validation, timing attacks—so let me try a different angle with E2E testing to catch UI-level bugs that static review might miss. {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_014pj6WPC2xcgCkgodaR5Uqq","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_012vFrgiZe8PTQCw3aUsvk83","name":"Agent","input":{"subagent_type":"feature-dev:code-reviewer","description":"Code review round 15","prompt":"Review the Bookmate codebase at /root/Projects/bookmate/app for bugs and issues. \n\nPrevious 14 rounds have already covered: race conditions, TOCTOU, type validation, transaction safety, auth issues, module-load freezing, input validation, FK violations, stale data, error UX, timing attacks, email HTML escaping, stale sessions, orphaned applications, is_full gaps, transaction array mutation.\n\nFocus on NEW angles not previously covered:\n1. **Memory leaks** — event listeners not cleaned up, intervals not cleared\n2. **SQL injection** — any string concatenation in SQL queries\n3. **Resource exhaustion** — unbounded queries, missing LIMIT clauses, large payloads\n4. **Error recovery** — are errors properly caught and reported, or do they silently swallow failures\n5. **Cookie/session edge cases** — session expiry, cookie attributes\n6. **Client-side state bugs** — React state that could get out of sync\n7. **API response inconsistencies** — different error formats, missing fields\n8. **Data integrity** — orphan records, impossible states in the DB\n\nLook at:\n- app/app/api/ (all route handlers)\n- app/app/ (all page components)\n- app/components/\n- app/lib/\n\nOnly report HIGH confidence bugs with clear impact. Do NOT report style issues or minor nits."},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":3368,"cache_read_input_tokens":22887,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":3368},"output_tokens":8,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"2461f1b3-284a-46b0-accd-df8549bf2929","uuid":"5688b5c7-bb94-4c87-a4b0-ff1dea7bcbec"}
{"type":"system","subtype":"task_started","task_id":"ae63ab08b24daf7ae","tool_use_id":"toolu_012vFrgiZe8PTQCw3aUsvk83","description":"Code review round 15","task_type":"local_agent","prompt":"Review the Bookmate codebase at /root/Projects/bookmate/app for bugs and issues. \n\nPrevious 14 rounds have already covered: race conditions, TOCTOU, type validation, transaction safety, auth issues, module-load freezing, input validation, FK violations, stale data, error UX, timing attacks, email HTML escaping, stale sessions, orphaned applications, is_full gaps, transaction array mutation.\n\nFocus on NEW angles not previously covered:\n1. **Memory leaks** — event listeners not cleaned up, intervals not cleared\n2. **SQL injection** — any string concatenation in SQL queries\n3. **Resource exhaustion** — unbounded queries, missing LIMIT clauses, large payloads\n4. **Error recovery** — are errors properly caught and reported, or do they silently swallow failures\n5. **Cookie/session edge cases** — session expiry, cookie attributes\n6. **Client-side state bugs** — React state that could get out of sync\n7. **API response inconsistencies** — different error formats, missing fields\n8. **Data integrity** — orphan records, impossible states in the DB\n\nLook at:\n- app/app/api/ (all route handlers)\n- app/app/ (all page components)\n- app/components/\n- app/lib/\n\nOnly report HIGH confidence bugs with clear impact. Do NOT report style issues or minor nits.","uuid":"361319f2-8913-47a9-95fa-65268e4af6a4","session_id":"2461f1b3-284a-46b0-accd-df8549bf2929"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Review the Bookmate codebase at /root/Projects/bookmate/app for bugs and issues. \n\nPrevious 14 rounds have already covered: race conditions, TOCTOU, type validation, transaction safety, auth issues, module-load freezing, input validation, FK violations, stale data, error UX, timing attacks, email HTML escaping, stale sessions, orphaned applications, is_full gaps, transaction array mutation.\n\nFocus on NEW angles not previously covered:\n1. **Memory leaks** — event listeners not cleaned up, intervals not cleared\n2. **SQL injection** — any string concatenation in SQL queries\n3. **Resource exhaustion** — unbounded queries, missing LIMIT clauses, large payloads\n4. **Error recovery** — are errors properly caught and reported, or do they silently swallow failures\n5. **Cookie/session edge cases** — session expiry, cookie attributes\n6. **Client-side state bugs** — React state that could get out of sync\n7. **API response inconsistencies** — different error formats, missing fields\n8. **Data integrity** — orphan records, impossible states in the DB\n\nLook at:\n- app/app/api/ (all route handlers)\n- app/app/ (all page components)\n- app/components/\n- app/lib/\n\nOnly report HIGH confidence bugs with clear impact. Do NOT report style issues or minor nits."}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_012vFrgiZe8PTQCw3aUsvk83","session_id":"2461f1b3-284a-46b0-accd-df8549bf2929","uuid":"d5427f7f-0fb2-4bfa-b550-4997a48f5239","timestamp":"2026-03-24T12:01:10.532Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01KyfRGWbzuHPdfoEZj9qz2t","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import Database from \"better-sqlite3\";\n     2→import path from \"path\";\n     3→import fs from \"fs\";\n     4→\n     5→const DB_DIR = process.env.DB_DIR || path.join(process.cwd(), \"data\");\n     6→const DB_PATH = path.join(DB_DIR, \"bookmate.db\");\n     7→\n     8→// Ensure data directory exists\n     9→if (!fs.existsSync(DB_DIR)) {\n    10→  fs.mkdirSync(DB_DIR, { recursive: true });\n    11→}\n    12→\n    13→const db = new Database(DB_PATH);\n    14→\n    15→// Enable WAL mode for better concurrent performance\n    16→db.pragma(\"journal_mode = WAL\");\n    17→db.pragma(\"foreign_keys = ON\");\n    18→\n    19→// Initialize schema\n    20→db.exec(`\n    21→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (\n    22→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    23→    email TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,\n    24→    password_hash TEXT NOT NULL,\n    25→    display_name TEXT NOT NULL,\n    26→    bio TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    27→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))\n    28→  );\n    29→\n    30→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listings (\n    31→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    32→    author_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    33→    book_title TEXT NOT NULL,\n    34→    book_author TEXT NOT NULL,\n    35→    book_cover_url TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    36→    book_olid TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    37→    language TEXT DEFAULT 'English',\n    38→    reading_pace TEXT NOT NULL,\n    39→    start_date TEXT NOT NULL,\n    40→    meeting_format TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(meeting_format IN ('voice', 'text', 'mixed')),\n    41→    max_group_size INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK(max_group_size >= 2 AND max_group_size <= 20),\n    42→    telegram_link TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    43→    is_full INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\n    44→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    45→    FOREIGN KEY (author_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    46→  );\n    47→\n    48→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listing_members (\n    49→    listing_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    50→    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    51→    joined_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    52→    PRIMARY KEY (listing_id, user_id),\n    53→    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id),\n    54→    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    55→  );\n    56→\n    57→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS user_genres (\n    58→    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    59→    genre TEXT NOT NULL,\n    60→    PRIMARY KEY (user_id, genre),\n    61→    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    62→  );\n    63→\n    64→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS notifications (\n    65→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    66→    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    67→    listing_id INTEGER,\n    68→    type TEXT NOT NULL,\n    69→    message TEXT NOT NULL,\n    70→    is_read INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\n    71→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    72→    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id),\n    73→    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id)\n    74→  );\n    75→\n    76→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listing_applications (\n    77→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    78→    listing_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    79→    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    80→    status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending'\n    81→      CHECK(status IN ('pending', 'approved', 'rejected')),\n    82→    message TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    83→    applied_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    84→    decided_at TEXT,\n    85→    UNIQUE(listing_id, user_id),\n    86→    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id),\n    87→    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    88→  );\n    89→`);\n    90→\n    91→// Idempotent migration: add requires_approval column\n    92→try {\n    93→  db.exec(\"ALTER TABLE listings ADD COLUMN requires_approval INTEGER DEFAULT 0\");\n    94→} catch {\n    95→  // Column already exists — safe to ignore\n    96→}\n    97→\n    98→// Idempotent migration: add platform_preference column\n    99→try {\n   100→  db.exec(\"ALTER TABLE listings ADD COLUMN platform_preference TEXT DEFAULT 'telegram'\");\n   101→} catch {\n   102→  // Column already exists — safe to ignore\n   103→}\n   104→\n   105→// Idempotent migration: add discord_link column\n   106→try {\n   107→  db.exec(\"ALTER TABLE listings ADD COLUMN discord_link TEXT DEFAULT ''\");\n   108→} catch 125→\n   126→// Tables for Telegram bot integration\n   127→db.exec(`\n   128→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS pending_telegram_groups (\n   129→    listing_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n   130→    telegram_chat_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n   131→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n   132→    PRIMARY KEY (listing_id),\n   133→    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id)\n   134→  );\n   135→\n   136→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS telegram_chats (\n   137→    chat_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,\n   138→    chat_title TEXT DEFAULT '',\n   139→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))\n   140→  );\n   141→\n   142→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS telegram_user_links (\n   143→    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n   144→    telegram_user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n   145→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n   146→    PRIMARY KEY (user_id),\n   147→    UNIQUE (telegram_user_id),\n   148→    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n   149→  );\n   150→`);\n   151→\n   152→// Rating and Reputation system\n   153→db.exec(`\n   154→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS ratings (\n   155→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n   156→    listing_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n   157→    rater_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n   158→    rated_user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n   159→    score INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK(score >= 1 AND score <= 5),\n   160→    comment TEXT DEFAULT '',\n   161→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n   162→    UNIQUE(listing_id, rater_id, rated_user_id),\n   163→    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id),\n   164→    FOREIGN KEY (rater_id) REFERENCES users(id),\n   165→    FOREIGN KEY (rated_user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n   166→  );\n   167→`);\n   168→\n   169→// Performance indexes on high-frequency foreign key columns\n   170→db.exec(`\n   171→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_listing_members_user_id ON listing_members(user_id);\n   172→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_notifications_user_id ON notifications(user_id);\n   173→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_ratings_rated_user_id ON ratings(rated_user_id);\n   174→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_listings_author_id ON listings(author_id);\n   175→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_listing_applications_listing_status ON listing_applications(listing_id, status);\n   176→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_notifications_user_unread ON notifications(user_id, is_read);\n   177→  CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_pending_telegram_chat_id ON pending_telegram_groups(telegram_chat_id);\n   178→`);\n   179→\n   180→export default db;\n {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01KAmbgZRPKEaMbi35UX7xfm","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→/**\n     2→ * Simple in-memory rate limiter for auth endpoints.\n     3→ * Uses a sliding window approach keyed by identifier (IP + route).\n     4→ * Not shared across cluster workers — acceptable for SQLite single-process setup.\n     5→ */\n     6→\n     7→interface RateLimitEntry {\n     8→  timestamps: number[];\n     9→}\n    10→\n    11→const store = new Map<string, RateLimitEntry>();\n    12→\n    13→// Clean up expired entries every 5 minutes\n    14→// Guard against multiple intervals on Next.js hot reload\n    15→const CLEANUP_INTERVAL = 5 * 60 * 1000;\n    16→const globalObj = globalThis as unknown as { __rateLimitCleanup?: ReturnType<typeof setInterval> };\n    17→if (!globalObj.__rateLimitCleanup) {\n    18→  globalObj.__rateLimitCleanup = setInterval(() => {\n    19→    const now = Date.now();\n    20→    for (const [key, entry] of store) {\n    21→      entry.timestamps = entry.timestamps.filter((t) => now - t < 15 * 60 * 1000);\n    22→      if (entry.timestamps.length === 0) {\n    23→        store.delete(key);\n    24→      }\n    25→    }\n    26→  }, CLEANUP_INTERVAL);\n    27→}\n    28→\n    29→/**\n    30→ * Check if a request should be rate limited.\n    31→ * @param key - Unique identifier (e.g., IP + email for login, IP for register)\n    32→ * @param maxAttempts - Maximum attempts allowed in the window\n    33→ * @param windowMs - Time window in milliseconds (default: 15 minutes)\n    34→ * @returns Object with allowed boolean and retryAfter seconds (0 if allowed)\n    35→ */\n    36→export function checkRateLimit(\n    37→  key: string,\n    38→  maxAttempts: number = 5,\n    39→  windowMs: number = 15 * 60 * 1000\n    40→): { allowed: boolean; retryAfter: number } {\n    41→  const now = Date.now();\n    42→  let entry = store.get(key);\n    43→\n    44→  if (!entry) {\n    45→    // Prevent unbounded memory growth under attack\n    46→    if (store.size > 50_000) {\n    47→      return { allowed: false, retryAfter: 60 };\n    48→    }\n    49→    entry = { timestamps: [] };\n    50→    store.set(key, entry);\n    51→  }\n    52→\n    53→  // Remove expired timestamps\n    54→  entry.timestamps = entry.timestamps.filter((t) => now - t < windowMs);\n    55→\n    56→  if (entry.timestamps.length >= maxAttempts) {\n    57→    const oldestInWindow = entry.timestamps[0];\n    58→    const retryAfter = Math.ceil((oldestInWindow + windowMs - now) / 1000);\n    59→    return { allowed: false, retryAfter };\n    60→  }\n    61→\n    62→  entry.timestamps.push(now);\n    63→  return { allowed: true, retryAfter: 0 };\n    64→}\n    65→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_012vFrgiZe8PTQCw3aUsvk83","session_id":"2461f1b3-284a-46b0-accd-df8549bf2929","uuid":"d6001677-36bd-4737-9099-4e0d10865589","timestamp":"2026-03-24T12:01:17.254Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01JefFhV8Ec5UTwT1KW4gnJf","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import db from \"./db\";\n     2→import { sendEmail } from \"./email\";\n     3→import { escapeHtmlForEmail } from \"./crypto-utils\";\n     4→\n     5→function getUserEmail(userId: number): string | null {\n     6→  const user = db\n     7→    .prepare(\"SELECT email FROM users WHERE id = ?\")\n     8→    .get(userId) as { email: string } | undefined;\n     9→  return user?.email || null;\n    10→}\n    11→\n    12→export function createNotification(\n    13→  userId: number,\n    14→  listingId: number | null,\n    15→  type: string,\n    16→  message: string\n    17→) {\n    18→  db.prepare(\n    19→    \"INSERT INTO notifications (user_id, listing_id, type, message) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)\"\n    20→  ).run(userId, listingId, type, message);\n    21→\n    22→  // Also send email notification (fire-and-forget)\n    23→  const email = getUserEmail(userId);\n    24→  if (email) {\n    25→    const subject = getEmailSubject(type);\n    26→    // HTML-escape the message to prevent injection via user-controlled content\n    27→    // (display names, book titles) embedded in notification messages\n    28→    const htmlBody = `<p>${escapeHtmlForEmail(message)}</p>`;\n    29→    sendEmail(email, subject, message, htmlBody).catch(() => {\n    30→      // Email sending is best-effort — don't block on failure\n    31→    });\n    32→  }\n    33→}\n    34→\n    35→function getEmailSubject(type: string): string {\n    36→  switch (type) {\n    37→    case \"new_member\":\n    38→      return \"Bookmate: Someone joined your reading group!\";\n    39→    case \"group_full\":\n    40→      return \"Bookmate: Your reading group is full!\";\n    41→    case \"application_received\":\n    42→      return \"Bookmate: New application for your reading group\";\n    43→    case \"application_approved\":\n    44→      return \"Bookmate: Your application was approved!\";\n    45→    case \"application_rejected\":\n    46→      return \"Bookmate: Application update\";\n    47→    case \"rating_received\":\n    48→      return \"Bookmate: You received a new rating\";\n    49→    case \"listing_deleted\":\n    50→      return \"Bookmate: A reading group has been cancelled\";\n    51→    case \"listing_updated\":\n    52→      return \"Bookmate: A reading group has been updated\";\n    53→    case \"telegram_ready\":\n    54→      return \"Bookmate: Your Telegram reading group is ready!\";\n    55→    case \"discord_ready\":\n    56→      return \"Bookmate: Your Discord channel is ready!\";\n    57→    default:\n    58→      return \"Bookmate: Notification\";\n    59→  }\n    60→}\n    61→\n    62→export function notifyListingAuthor(\n    63→  listingId: number,\n    64→  joinerName: string\n    65→) {\n    66→  const listing = db\n    67→    .prepare(\n    68→      \"SELECT l.author_id, l.book_title FROM listings l WHERE l.id = ?\"\n    69→    )\n    70→    .get(listingId) as { author_id: number; book_title: string } | undefined;\n    71→\n    72→  if (listing) {\n    73→    createNotification(\n    74→      listing.author_id,\n    75→      listingId,\n    76→      \"new_member\",\n    77→      `${joinerName} joined your reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\"`\n    78→    );\n    79→  }\n    80→}\n    81→\n    82→export function notifyApplicationReceived(\n    83→  listingId: number,\n    84→  applicantName: string\n    85→) {\n    86→  const listing = db\n    87→    .prepare(\n    88→      \"SELECT l.author_id, l.book_title FROM listings l WHERE l.id = ?\"\n    89→    )\n    90→    .get(listingId) as { author_id: number; book_title: string } | undefined;\n    91→\n    92→  if (listing) {\n    93→    createNotification(\n    94→      listing.author_id,\n    95→      listingId,\n    96→      \"application_received\",\n    97→      `${applicantName} applied to join your reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\"`\n    98→    );\n    99→  }\n   100→}\n   101→\n   102→export function notifyApplicationDecision(\n   103→  userId: number,\n   104→  listingId: number,\n   105→  decision: \"approved\" | \"rejected\"\n   106→) {\n   107→  const listing = db\n   108→    .prepare(\"SELECT book_title FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n   109→    .get(listingId) as { book_title: string } | undefined;\n   110→\n   111→  if (listing) {\n   112→    const type = decision === \"approved\" ? \"application_approved\" : \"application_rejected\";\n   113→    const message =\n   114→      decision === \"approved\"\n   115→        ? `Your application to join \"${listing.book_title}\" was approved!`\n   116→        : `Your application to join \"${listing.book_title}\" was not accepted.`;\n   117→    createNotification(userId, listingId, type, message);\n   118→  }\n   119→}\n   120→\n   121→/**\n   122→ * Notify all members that the group is full.\n   123→ * Accepts listing data as parameters to avoid re-querying outside the\n   124→ * join transaction (which could return stale platform_preference if\n   125→ * a concurrent PATCH changed it between the transaction commit and this call).\n   126→ */\n   127→export function notifyGroupFull(\n   128→  listingId: number,\n   129→  bookTitle: string,\n   130→  platformPreference: string\n   131→) {\n   132→  // Include all members (including the author) — author needs to know group is full to create the chat\n   133→  const members = db\n   134→    .prepare(\"SELECT user_id FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n   135→    .all(listingId) as { user_id: number }[];\n   136→\n   137→  const platform = platformPreference === \"discord\" ? \"Discord\" : \"Telegram\";\n   138→  const botConfigured = platformPreference === \"discord\"\n   139→    ? !!process.env.DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN\n   140→    : !!process.env.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN;\n   141→  const message = botConfigured\n   142→    ? `The reading group for \"${bookTitle}\" is now full! The ${platform} group will be set up automatically.`\n   143→    : `The reading group for \"${bookTitle}\" is now full! The organizer will share a ${platform} link soon.`;\n   144→\n   145→  for (const member of members) {\n   146→    createNotification(\n   147→      member.user_id,\n   148→      listingId,\n   149→      \"group_full\",\n   150→      message\n   151→    );\n   152→  }\n   153→}\n   154→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_012vFrgiZe8PTQCw3aUsvk83","session_id":"2461f1b3-284a-46b0-accd-df8549bf2929","uuid":"22f99a08-c06b-4608-9994-2fde4196df34","timestamp":"2026-03-24T12:01:17.298Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01U2uVrkzcwMe5bwva5zYjgv","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { getIronSession, type SessionOptions } from \"iron-session\";\n     2→import { cookies } from \"next/headers\";\n     3→import db from \"./db\";\n     4→\n     5→export interface SessionData {\n     6→  userId?: number;\n     7→  email?: string;\n     8→  displayName?: string;\n     9→}\n    10→\n    11→const DEV_SECRET =\n    12→  \"bookmate-development-secret-key-change-in-production-32chars!\";\n    13→\n    14→function getSessionOptions(): SessionOptions {\n    15→  const secret = process.env.SESSION_SECRET;\n    16→  if (!secret && process.env.NODE_ENV === \"production\") {\n    17→    throw new Error(\n    18→      \"SESSION_SECRET environment variable is required in production\"\n    19→    );\n    20→  }\n    21→  return {\n    22→    password: secret || DEV_SECRET,\n    23→    cookieName: \"bookmate_session\",\n    24→    cookieOptions: {\n    25→      httpOnly: true,\n    26→      secure: process.env.NODE_ENV === \"production\" && process.env.HTTPS_ENABLED === \"true\",\n    27→      sameSite: \"lax\",\n    28→      maxAge: 60 * 60 * 24 * 7, // 1 week\n    29→    },\n    30→  };\n    31→}\n    32→\n    33→export async function getSession() {\n    34→  const cookieStore = await cookies();\n    35→  return getIronSession<SessionData>(cookieStore, getSessionOptions());\n    36→}\n    37→\n    38→export async function requireAuth() {\n    39→  const session = await getSession();\n    40→  if (!session.userId) {\n    41→    return null;\n    42→  }\n    43→  // Verify the user still exists in the DB — a deleted user's cookie\n    44→  // would otherwise grant full access until the session expires\n    45→  const user = db\n    46→    .prepare(\"SELECT id, display_name FROM users WHERE id = ?\")\n    47→    .get(session.userId) as { id: number; display_name: string } | undefined;\n    48→  if (!user) {\n    49→    session.destroy();\n    50→    return null;\n    51→  }\n    52→  // Keep session displayName in sync with DB (handles stale cookies)\n    53→  if (user.display_name !== session.displayName) {\n    54→    session.displayName = user.display_name;\n    55→    await session.save();\n    56→  }\n    57→  return session;\n    58→}\n    59→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_012vFrgiZe8PTQCw3aUsvk83","session_id":"2461f1b3-284a-46b0-accd-df8549bf2929","uuid":"94e3be72-598f-4087-97f0-35347097f8f3","timestamp":"2026-03-24T12:01:17.685Z"}
