{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01S2tkQtiaxWnU4nLPZc4M7B","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→\n     5→const AVAILABLE_GENRES = [\n     6→  \"Literary Fiction\",\n     7→  \"Science Fiction\",\n     8→  \"Fantasy\",\n     9→  \"Mystery\",\n    10→  \"Thriller\",\n    11→  \"Romance\",\n    12→  \"Historical Fiction\",\n    13→  \"Horror\",\n    14→  \"Dystopian\",\n    15→  \"Adventure\",\n    16→  \"Contemporary Fiction\",\n    17→  \"Magical Realism\",\n    18→  \"Classics\",\n    19→  \"Short Stories\",\n    20→  \"Young Adult\",\n    21→  \"Graphic Novels\",\n    22→];\n    23→\n    24→interface GenreRow {\n    25→  genre: string;\n    26→}\n    27→\n    28→export async function GET() {\n    29→  const session = await requireAuth();\n    30→  if (!session) {\n    31→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n    32→  }\n    33→\n    34→  const userGenres = db.prepare(\n    35→    \"SELECT genre FROM user_genres WHERE user_id = ? ORDER BY genre\"\n    36→  ).all(session.userId) as GenreRow[];\n    37→\n    38→  return NextResponse.json({\n    39→    genres: userGenres.map((g) => g.genre),\n    40→    available: AVAILABLE_GENRES,\n    41→  });\n    42→}\n    43→\n    44→export async function PUT(req: NextRequest) {\n    45→  const session = await requireAuth();\n    46→  if (!session) {\n    47→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n    48→  }\n    49→\n    50→  const { genres } = await req.json();\n    51→\n    52→  if (!Array.isArray(genres)) {\n    53→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"genres must be an array\" }, { status: 400 });\n    54→  }\n    55→\n    56→  // Validate all genres\n    57→  const validGenres = genres.filter((g: string) => AVAILABLE_GENRES.includes(g));\n    58→\n    59→  // Replace all genres in a transaction\n    60→  const updateGenres = db.transaction(() => {\n    61→    db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM user_genres WHERE user_id = ?\").run(session.userId);\n    62→    const insert = db.prepare(\"INSERT INTO user_genres (user_id, genre) VALUES (?, ?)\");\n    63→    for (const genre of validGenres) {\n    64→      insert.run(session.userId, genre);\n    65→    }\n    66→  });\n    67→\n    68→  updateGenres();\n    69→\n    70→  return NextResponse.json({ genres: validGenres });\n    71→}\n    72→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_012vFrgiZe8PTQCw3aUsvk83","session_id":"2461f1b3-284a-46b0-accd-df8549bf2929","uuid":"ac274460-e297-4b17-a24e-ac703751e3b3","timestamp":"2026-03-24T12:02:01.405Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01YXGtfd2HhJopn1s7gYpJCk","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { getSession } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→\n     5→const startTime = Date.now();\n     6→\n     7→export async function GET() {\n     8→  try {\n     9→    // Check database connectivity\n    10→    const dbCheck = db.prepare(\"SELECT 1 AS ok\").get() as { ok: number } | undefined;\n    11→    const dbOk = dbCheck?.ok === 1;\n    12→\n    13→    const response: Record<string, unknown> = {\n    14→      status: \"healthy\",\n    15→      uptime: Math.floor((Date.now() - startTime) / 1000),\n    16→      database: dbOk ? \"connected\" : \"error\",\n    17→      timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),\n    18→    };\n    19→\n    20→    // Only expose detailed stats to authenticated users\n    21→    try {\n    22→      const session = await getSession();\n    23→      if (session?.userId) {\n    24→        const stats = db.prepare(`\n    25→          SELECT\n    26→            (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users) AS users,\n    27→            (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM listings) AS listings,\n    28→            (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM listings WHERE is_full = 0) AS open_listings\n    29→        `).get() as { users: number; listings: number; open_listings: number };\n    30→\n    31→        response.stats = {\n    32→          users: stats.users,\n    33→          listings: stats.listings,\n    34→          openListings: stats.open_listings,\n    35→        };\n    36→      }\n    37→    } catch {\n    38→      // Session check failed (e.g., no cookies context) — skip stats\n    39→    }\n    40→\n    41→    return NextResponse.json(response);\n    42→  } catch (error) {\n    43→    return NextResponse.json(\n    44→      {\n    45→        status: \"unhealthy\",\n    46→        uptime: Math.floor((Date.now() - startTime) / 1000),\n    47→        database: \"error\",\n    48→        error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : \"Unknown error\",\n    49→        timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),\n    50→      },\n    51→      { status: 503 }\n    52→    );\n    53→  }\n    54→}\n    55→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_012vFrgiZe8PTQCw3aUsvk83","session_id":"2461f1b3-284a-46b0-accd-df8549bf2929","uuid":"41dc8b5c-69be-4266-8112-11420fd56aee","timestamp":"2026-03-24T12:02:01.838Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_0156yPYyFWaRyG7rdhpbFtRJ","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { timingSafeEqual } from \"crypto\";\n     2→\n     3→/**\n     4→ * Constant-time string comparison to prevent timing side-channel attacks.\n     5→ * Use this for comparing secrets (webhook tokens, setup secrets, etc.)\n     6→ */\n     7→export function safeCompare(a: string, b: string): boolean {\n     8→  const bufA = Buffer.from(a);\n     9→  const bufB = Buffer.from(b);\n    10→  if (bufA.length !== bufB.length) {\n    11→    // Do a dummy comparison to add some constant work, though this does not\n    12→    // fully mask length differences (bufA self-compare time varies with length).\n    13→    // In practice, the secrets compared here are fixed-length env vars.\n    14→    timingSafeEqual(bufA, bufA);\n    15→    return false;\n    16→  }\n    17→  return timingSafeEqual(bufA, bufB);\n    18→}\n    19→\n    20→/**\n    21→ * Escape user-controlled content for safe embedding in HTML emails.\n    22→ */\n    23→export function escapeHtmlForEmail(text: string): string {\n    24→  return text\n    25→    .replace(/&/g, \"&amp;\")\n    26→    .replace(/</g, \"&lt;\")\n    27→    .replace(/>/g, \"&gt;\")\n    28→    .replace(/\"/g, \"&quot;\")\n    29→    .replace(/'/g, \"&#039;\");\n    30→}\n    31→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_012vFrgiZe8PTQCw3aUsvk83","session_id":"2461f1b3-284a-46b0-accd-df8549bf2929","uuid":"e1f9937e-0f18-4ef8-88c5-0d9c3d126079","timestamp":"2026-03-24T12:02:02.177Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_012K7JQaP7ebA4sycGowgTo1","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→import { isBotConfigured, generateGroupDeepLink } from \"@/lib/telegram\";\n     5→\n     6→/**\n     7→ * GET /api/listings/[id]/auto-telegram\n     8→ *\n     9→ * Returns the Telegram deep link for one-click group creation.\n    10→ * Only available to the listing author when the group is full\n    11→ * and no Telegram link has been set yet.\n    12→ */\n    13→export async function GET(\n    14→  _req: NextRequest,\n    15→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n    16→) {\n    17→  const session = await requireAuth();\n    18→  if (!session) {\n    19→    return NextResponse.json(\n    20→      { error: \"Authentication required\" },\n    21→      { status: 401 }\n    22→    );\n    23→  }\n    24→\n    25→  if (!isBotConfigured()) {\n    26→    return NextResponse.json(\n    27→      { error: \"Telegram bot not configured\", botConfigured: false },\n    28→      { status: 404 }\n    29→    );\n    30→  }\n    31→\n    32→  const { id } = await params;\n    33→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n    34→  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n    35→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid listing ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n    36→  }\n    37→\n    38→  const listing = db\n    39→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    40→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    41→\n    42→  if (!listing) {\n    43→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n    44→  }\n    45→\n    46→  if (listing.author_id !== session.userId) {\n    47→    return NextResponse.json(\n    48→      { error: \"Only the organizer can create the Telegram group\" },\n    49→      { status: 403 }\n    50→    );\n    51→  }\n    52→\n    53→  if (!listing.is_full) {\n    54→    return NextResponse.json(\n    55→      { error: \"Group must be full before creating a Telegram group\" },\n    56→      { status: 400 }\n    57→    );\n    58→  }\n    59→\n    60→  if (listing.telegram_link) {\n    61→    return NextResponse.json(\n    62→      { error: \"Telegram link already set\", telegramLink: listing.telegram_link },\n    63→      { status: 400 }\n    64→    );\n    65→  }\n    66→\n    67→  const deepLink = await generateGroupDeepLink(listingId);\n    68→  if (!deepLink) {\n    69→    return NextResponse.json(\n    70→      { error: \"Failed to generate deep link. Check bot configuration.\" },\n    71→      { status: 500 }\n    72→    );\n    73→  }\n    74→\n    75→  return NextResponse.json({\n    76→    deepLink,\n    77→    instructions:\n    78→      \"Click the link to open Telegram and create a group. The bot will automatically generate an invite link for your reading group.\",\n    79→  });\n    80→}\n    81→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_012vFrgiZe8PTQCw3aUsvk83","session_id":"2461f1b3-284a-46b0-accd-df8549bf2929","uuid":"312da239-1341-41ef-8a4e-f043e1a2310a","timestamp":"2026-03-24T12:02:05.965Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01KQLfGR4dMvU5mhpzRmPqcq","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { getSession } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→import { isBotConfigured, generateBotInviteUrl } from \"@/lib/discord\";\n     5→\n     6→export async function GET(\n     7→  _req: NextRequest,\n     8→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n     9→) {\n    10→  const session = await getSession();\n    11→  if (!session.userId) {\n    12→    return NextResponse.json(\n    13→      { error: \"Authentication required\" },\n    14→      { status: 401 }\n    15→    );\n    16→  }\n    17→\n    18→  if (!isBotConfigured()) {\n    19→    return NextResponse.json(\n    20→      { error: \"Discord bot is not configured\" },\n    21→      { status: 503 }\n    22→    );\n    23→  }\n    24→\n    25→  const { id } = await params;\n    26→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n    27→  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n    28→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid listing ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n    29→  }\n    30→\n    31→  const listing = db\n    32→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    33→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    34→\n    35→  if (!listing) {\n    36→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n    37→  }\n    38→\n    39→  if (listing.author_id !== session.userId) {\n    40→    return NextResponse.json(\n    41→      { error: \"Only the listing author can set up the Discord group\" },\n    42→      { status: 403 }\n    43→    );\n    44→  }\n    45→\n    46→  if (!listing.is_full) {\n    47→    return NextResponse.json(\n    48→      { error: \"The group is not full yet\" },\n    49→      { status: 400 }\n    50→    );\n    51→  }\n    52→\n    53→  if (listing.discord_link) {\n    54→    return NextResponse.json(\n    55→      { error: \"A Discord link has already been set\" },\n    56→      { status: 400 }\n    57→    );\n    58→  }\n    59→\n    60→  const inviteUrl = generateBotInviteUrl(listingId);\n    61→\n    62→  return NextResponse.json({\n    63→    inviteUrl,\n    64→    instructions:\n    65→      \"Click the link to add the Bookmate bot to your Discord server. \" +\n    66→      \"The bot will create a reading group channel and generate an invite link for your members.\",\n    67→  });\n    68→}\n    69→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_012vFrgiZe8PTQCw3aUsvk83","session_id":"2461f1b3-284a-46b0-accd-df8549bf2929","uuid":"35d734ea-0fbb-48c5-a6b2-ec0360b91f7b","timestamp":"2026-03-24T12:02:06.422Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_013p7M5caFAcntGuNJKBnFYJ","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→/**\n     2→ * Discord Bot API integration for automatic server/channel creation.\n     3→ *\n     4→ * When DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN and DISCORD_CLIENT_ID are set, the system can:\n     5→ * 1. Generate bot invite URLs so authors can add the bot to their server\n     6→ * 2. Create text channels and invite links via Discord REST API\n     7→ * 3. Receive interaction events via webhook\n     8→ *\n     9→ * Flow:\n    10→ * - Author clicks \"Set up Discord\" → opens bot invite URL\n    11→ * - Author adds bot to their server\n    12→ * - Bot receives an interaction or the author triggers channel creation\n    13→ * - Bot creates a text channel + invite link, saves to listing\n    14→ */\n    15→\n    16→const DISCORD_API = \"https://discord.com/api/v10\";\n    17→\n    18→/**\n    19→ * Read bot credentials live from process.env on each call — not at module load time.\n    20→ * Module-level constants would be frozen when the file is first imported,\n    21→ * missing any env vars set or changed after server startup.\n    22→ */\n    23→function getBotToken(): string {\n    24→  return process.env.DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN || \"\";\n    25→}\n    26→\n    27→function getClientId(): string {\n    28→  return process.env.DISCORD_CLIENT_ID || \"\";\n    29→}\n    30→\n    31→export function isBotConfigured(): boolean {\n    32→  return getBotToken().length > 0 && getClientId().length > 0;\n    33→}\n    34→\n    35→/**\n    36→ * Validate a Discord snowflake ID (numeric string, 17-20 digits).\n    37→ * Used to prevent path traversal in Discord API URL construction.\n    38→ */\n    39→export function isValidSnowflake(id: string): boolean {\n    40→  return /^\\d{17,20}$/.test(id);\n    41→}\n    42→\n    43→/**\n    44→ * Call the Discord REST API.\n    45→ */\n    46→async function callApi<T = unknown>(\n    47→  endpoint: string,\n    48→  method: string = \"GET\",\n    49→  body?: Record<string, unknown>\n    50→): Promise<{ ok: boolean; data?: T; error?: string }> {\n    51→  try {\n    52→    const res = await fetch(`${DISCORD_API}${endpoint}`, {\n    53→      method,\n    54→      headers: {\n    55→        Authorization: `Bot ${getBotToken()}`,\n    56→        \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\",\n    57→      },\n    58→      body: body ? JSON.stringify(body) : undefined,\n    59→    });\n    60→\n    61→    if (!res.ok) {\n    62→      const errData = await res.json().catch(() => ({}));\n    63→      return {\n    64→        ok: false,\n    65→        error: (errData as Record<string, string>).message || `HTTP ${res.status}`,\n    66→      };\n    67→    }\n    68→\n    69→    const data = (await res.json()) as T;\n    70→    return { ok: true, data };\n    71→  } catch (err) {\n    72→    return { ok: false, error: String(err) };\n    73→  }\n    74→}\n    75→\n    76→/**\n    77→ * Get bot user info.\n    78→ */\n    79→export async function getBotInfo(): Promise<{\n    80→  id: string;\n    81→  username: string;\n    82→} | null> {\n    83→  const res = await callApi<{ id: string; username: string }>(\"/users/@me\");\n    84→  return res.ok && res.data ? res.data : null;\n    85→}\n    86→\n    87→/**\n    88→ * Generate a bot invite URL that includes the listing ID in the state parameter.\n    89→ * The bot needs permissions: Manage Channels, Create Instant Invite, Send Messages\n    90→ *\n    91→ * Permission bits:\n    92→ * - MANAGE_CHANNELS (0x10) = 16\n    93→ * - CREATE_INSTANT_INVITE (0x1) = 1\n    94→ * - SEND_MESSAGES (0x800) = 2048\n    95→ * - VIEW_CHANNEL (0x400) = 1024\n    96→ * Total: 3089\n    97→ */\n    98→export function generateBotInviteUrl(listingId: number): string {\n    99→  const permissions = 3089;\n   100→  const state = `listing_${listingId}`;\n   101→  return `https://discord.com/oauth2/authorize?client_id=${getClientId()}&permissions=${permissions}&scope=bot&state=${encodeURIComponent(state)}`;\n   102→}\n   103→\n   104→/**\n   105→ * Create a text channel in a guild.\n   106→ */\n   107→export async function createTextChannel(\n   108→  guildId: string,\n   109→  name: string,\n   110→  topic?: string\n   111→): Promise<{ id: string; name: string } | null> {\n   112→  const res = await callApi<{ id: string; name: string }>(\n   113→    `/guilds/${guildId}/channels`,\n   114→    \"POST\",\n   115→    {\n   116→      name: name.toLowerCase().replace(/[^a-z0-9-]/g, \"-\").slice(0, 100),\n   117→      type: 0, // GUILD_TEXT\n   118→      topic: topic || \"Bookmate reading group discussion channel\",\n   119→    }\n   120→  );\n   121→  return res.ok && res.data ? res.data : null;\n   122→}\n   123→\n   124→/**\n   125→ * Create an invite for a channel.\n   126→ */\n   127→export async function createChannelInvite(\n   128→  channelId: string,\n   129→  maxAge: number = 0 // 0 = never expires\n   130→): Promise<string | null> {\n   131→  const res = await callApi<{ code: string }>(\n   132→    `/channels/${channelId}/invites`,\n   133→    \"POST\",\n   134→    {\n   135→      max_age: maxAge,\n   136→      max_uses: 0,\n   137→      unique: true,\n   138→    }\n   139→  );\n   140→  if (res.ok && res.data) {\n   141→    return `https://discord.gg/${res.data.code}`;\n   142→  }\n   143→  return null;\n   144→}\n   145→\n   146→/**\n   147→ * Send a message to a Discord channel.\n   148→ */\n   149→export async function sendChannelMessage(\n   150→  channelId: string,\n   151→  content: string\n   152→): Promise<boolean> {\n   153→  const res = await callApi(\n   154→    `/channels/${channelId}/messages`,\n   155→    \"POST\",\n   156→    { content }\n   157→  );\n   158→  return res.ok;\n   159→}\n   160→\n   161→/**\n   162→ * Get guild (server) info.\n   163→ */\n   164→export async function getGuild(\n   165→  guildId: string\n   166→): Promise<{ id: string; name: string } | null> {\n   167→  const res = await callApi<{ id: string; name: string }>(\n   168→    `/guilds/${guildId}`\n   169→  );\n   170→  return res.ok && res.data ? res.data : null;\n   171→}\n   172→\n   173→/**\n   174→ * List guilds the bot is a member of.\n   175→ */\n   176→export async function getBotGuilds(): Promise<\n   177→  { id: string; name: string }[]\n   178→> {\n   179→  const res = await callApi<{ id: string; name: string }[]>(\n   180→    \"/users/@me/guilds\"\n   181→  );\n   182→  return res.ok && res.data ? res.data : [];\n   183→}\n   184→\n   185→/**\n   186→ * Parse listing ID from a state or payload string.\n   187→ * Expected format: \"listing_123\"\n   188→ */\n   189→export function parseListingIdFromState(state: string): number | null {\n   190→  const match = state.match(/^listing_(\\d+)$/);\n   191→  return match ? parseInt(match[1], 10) : null;\n   192→}\n   193→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_012vFrgiZe8PTQCw3aUsvk83","session_id":"2461f1b3-284a-46b0-accd-df8549bf2929","uuid":"aa1cc6bd-3a00-48a7-8f08-008ec6a3430d","timestamp":"2026-03-24T12:02:06.573Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01D7F4HW1WyBBoEz9qAXo9wU","type":"tool_result","content":"    46→  if (readingPace && readingPace.length <= 200) {\n    47→    conditions.push(\"l.reading_pace LIKE ?\");\n    48→    params.push(`%${readingPace}%`);\n    49→  }\n    50→\n    51→  const whereClause = conditions.join(\" AND \");\n    52→\n    53→  let orderClause = \"l.created_at DESC\";\n    54→  if (sort === \"oldest\") orderClause = \"l.created_at ASC\";\n    55→  else if (sort === \"start_date\") orderClause = \"l.start_date ASC\";\n    56→\n    57→  const offset = (page - 1) * PAGE_SIZE;\n    58→\n    59→  // Get total count for pagination metadata\n    60→  const countResult = db\n    61→    .prepare(\n    62→      `SELECT COUNT(*) as total FROM listings l WHERE ${whereClause}`\n    63→    )\n    64→    .get(...params) as { total: number };\n    65→\n    66→  const listings = db\n    67→    .prepare(\n    68→      `SELECT\n    69→        l.*,\n    70→        u.display_name as author_name,\n    71→        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = l.id) as member_count\n    72→      FROM listings l\n    73→      JOIN users u ON l.author_id = u.id\n    74→      WHERE ${whereClause}\n    75→      ORDER BY ${orderClause}\n    76→      LIMIT ? OFFSET ?`\n    77→    )\n    78→    .all(...params, PAGE_SIZE, offset);\n    79→\n    80→  return NextResponse.json({\n    81→    listings,\n    82→    pagination: {\n    83→      page,\n    84→      pageSize: PAGE_SIZE,\n    85→      total: countResult.total,\n    86→      totalPages: Math.ceil(countResult.total / PAGE_SIZE),\n    87→    },\n    88→  });\n    89→}\n    90→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_012vFrgiZe8PTQCw3aUsvk83","session_id":"2461f1b3-284a-46b0-accd-df8549bf2929","uuid":"ae54c0d8-6648-4e9d-bf5f-32577ea8fc68","timestamp":"2026-03-24T12:02:23.274Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_012vFrgiZe8PTQCw3aUsvk83","type":"tool_result","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Now I have everything I need. Let me compile the findings.\n\n---\n\nReviewing the Bookmate codebase at `/root/Projects/bookmate/app`. The review covers all API routes, lib utilities, and client components against the new angles specified.\n\n---\n\n## Critical Issues\n\n### 1. SQL Injection via Unvalidated `sort` Parameter in ORDER BY Clause\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/route.ts`, lines 53-55 and 75\n\n**Confidence: 95**\n\nThe `sort` query parameter controls `orderClause`, which is interpolated directly into the SQL string template at line 75:\n\n```\nORDER BY ${orderClause}\n```\n\nThe validation at lines 53-55 only assigns three safe string literals for `\"oldest\"` and `\"start_date\"`, and defaults to `\"l.created_at DESC\"` for anything else. That default handling looks correct. However, this is a fragile structure — if a future developer adds a new sort option using user input directly, or if the logic is modified, it becomes injection. More critically, the `whereClause` is also string-interpolated (lines 62 and 74):\n\n```\n`SELECT COUNT(*) as total FROM listings l WHERE ${whereClause}`\n`WHERE ${whereClause}`\n```\n\nThe `whereClause` is built by joining fixed string literals from `conditions[]`, and all user-controlled values go into `params[]` as bound parameters, so the WHERE clause itself is safe. However, `orderClause` is a raw string inserted without parameterization. The current three-value allowlist makes it safe today, but this pattern is one wrong edit away from full SQL injection. The order clause string is set only from three hardcoded literals with no user input reaching it, so this is a code quality risk rather than a live injection, but it warrants being highlighted since the structural pattern is dangerous.\n\n**Fix:** Replace the string interpolation with an allowlist map:\n```typescript\nconst ORDER_MAP: Record<string, string> = {\n  oldest: \"l.created_at ASC\",\n  start_date: \"l.start_date ASC\",\n  newest: \"l.created_at DESC\",\n};\nconst orderClause = ORDER_MAP[sort] ?? \"l.created_at DESC\";\n```\n\n---\n\n## Important Issues\n\n### 2. `email.ts` — Nodemailer Transporter Cached After First Creation, Config Changes Not Reflected\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/email.ts`, lines 16-36\n\n**Confidence: 85**\n\nThe module-level `transporter` variable is created once and cached:\n\n```typescript\nlet transporter: nodemailer.Transporter | null = null;\n\nfunction getTransporter(): nodemailer.Transporter | null {\n  if (!checkConfigured()) return null;\n  if (!transporter) {\n    transporter = nodemailer.createTransport({ ... });\n  }\n  return transporter;\n}\n```\n\nThe `checkConfigured()` call reads from `process.env` each time, which is the intended pattern (consistent with the comment in the file). However, `getTransporter()` only creates the transporter once. If SMTP credentials are set after the first call (e.g., if `SMTP_HOST` is set but `SMTP_PASS` is changed), the transporter is never recreated. The cached transporter will use the old credentials indefinitely until the process restarts.\n\nMore concretely: if `checkConfigured()` returns true on the second call (e.g., `SMTP_FROM` was added after startup), the transporter was already built on the first call without `SMTP_FROM`, and the cached instance will send emails without the `from` address being configured. This is a silent misconfiguration that can result in emails failing at send time rather than at startup, making it hard to diagnose.\n\n**Fix:** Either build the transporter at startup (fail fast) or re-check all SMTP vars when creating it. At minimum, hash the relevant env vars and invalidate the cache when they change.\n\n---\n\n### 3. `auto-discord/route.ts` — Uses `getSession()` Instead of `requireAuth()`, Bypassing DB User-Existence Check\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/auto-discord/route.ts`, lines 10-16\n\n**Confidence: 90**\n\nThis route uses `getSession()` directly and checks `session.userId` manually:\n\n```typescript\nconst session = await getSession();\nif (!session.userId) {\n  return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n}\n```\n\nEvery other authenticated route in the codebase uses `requireAuth()`, which additionally verifies the user still exists in the DB and invalidates sessions for deleted users. This route does not perform that check. A deleted user's valid cookie can reach the author-check at line 39 with a userId that no longer exists in the DB, potentially matching `listing.author_id` if the user was deleted and a new user was created with the same row ID (SQLite AUTOINCREMENT makes this extremely unlikely but not impossible as a concern).\n\nMore practically, this is an inconsistency with the security model — the comment in `requireAuth()` explicitly documents that the DB check is intended to prevent deleted users from accessing the API. This endpoint intentionally or accidentally circumvents that invariant.\n\n**Fix:** Replace `getSession()` + manual check with `requireAuth()` to match every other authenticated endpoint.\n\n---\n\n### 4. `notifications/route.ts` — `GET` Queries Up to 50 Rows But `PATCH` (Mark All Read) Is Unbounded\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/notifications/route.ts`, lines 34-57\n\n**Confidence: 82**\n\nThe GET handler correctly limits results to 50 rows. However, the PATCH handler that marks all notifications as read issues an unbounded `UPDATE`:\n\n```typescript\ndb.prepare(\"UPDATE notifications SET is_read = 1 WHERE user_id = ?\").run(session.userId);\n```\n\nIf a user accumulates thousands of notifications (either legitimately or due to a spam attack that bypasses rate limits), this UPDATE will lock the SQLite WAL for an extended write. Given SQLite's single-writer model, this can block all concurrent reads and writes during that window, degrading service for all users.\n\n**Fix:** Add a `LIMIT` to the mark-all-read UPDATE, or process it in batches. Alternatively, add a periodic cleanup job that deletes old read notifications.\n\n---\n\n### 5. `profile/reading/route.ts` — Unbounded Query Returns All Listings for a User\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/profile/reading/route.ts`, lines 29-50\n\n**Confidence: 82**\n\nThe query fetches all listings a user is a member of without any `LIMIT`:\n\n```typescript\nconst readings = db.prepare(`\n  SELECT ... FROM listing_members lm\n  JOIN listings l ON l.id = lm.listing_id\n  ...\n  WHERE lm.user_id = ?\n  ORDER BY lm.joined_at DESC\n`).all(session.userId) as ReadingRow[];\n```\n\nA power user who has joined many groups, or a test account, could receive a very large payload. The result is then serialized to JSON and sent in a single response. There is no pagination, and no cap on the number of rows returned. While today's dataset is small, as the site scales this endpoint will become a memory and bandwidth concern, and could be used as a denial-of-service vector against authenticated users.\n\n**Fix:** Add a reasonable `LIMIT` (e.g., 200) and consider paginating the reading history, at minimum.\n\n---\n\n### 6. `ListingDetail.tsx` — Client-Side State for `telegramLink` / `discordLink` Can Drift After Successful Save\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/listings/[id]/ListingDetail.tsx`, lines 86-103 and 266-336\n\n**Confidence: 83**\n\nWhen the user saves a Telegram link (`handleTelegramSave`) or Discord link (`handleDiscordSave`), on success the component calls `fetchListing()` to refresh all listing data. This is correct.\n\nHowever, the `telegramLink` and `discordLink` local state variables (lines 60, 66) are initialized from the fetched listing:\n\n```typescript\nsetTelegramLink(data.listing.telegram_link || \"\");\nsetDiscordLink(data.listing.discord_link || \"\");\n```\n\nBut if the API returns a listing where `telegram_link` / `discord_link` is stripped (i.e., the user is a non-member, non-author, where these fields are deleted server-side per the access control logic in `[id]/route.ts` lines 95-99), these local state values will be reset to `\"\"` after `fetchListing()`. This means if the author saves a link, the input resets to empty on the next fetch, which could lead them to believe the save failed or cause them to re-submit.\n\nIn practice the author always satisfies `isAuthor`, so the fields are included in the response. But the logic is subtle and could silently regress if the access control logic changes.\n\n**Fix:** This is a latent issue. The fix is to drive `telegramLink` display state from `listing.telegram_link` directly rather than a parallel state variable, or to explicitly not reset state when `fetchListing()` returns a non-author view.\n\n---\n\n### 7. `listings/route.ts` — `q` and `readingPace` Input Contains LIKE Wildcards That Aren't Escaped\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/route.ts`, lines 36-48\n\n**Confidence: 85**\n\nUser-supplied search strings `q` and `readingPace` are interpolated as LIKE patterns using parameterized queries:\n\n```typescript\nparams.push(`%${q}%`, `%${q}%`);\nparams.push(`%${readingPace}%`);\n```\n\nThe parameterization prevents SQL injection, but `%` and `_` characters inside `q` and `readingPace` are LIKE wildcard metacharacters. A user searching for `%` will match every row (equivalent to no filter). A user searching for `_` will match any single character. While this is not a security vulnerability, it means a search for `%` returns all listings, potentially revealing listings the user isn't supposed to see (the list endpoint already filters `is_full = 0`, so visibility exposure is limited). More importantly, a search for `%%%%` causes SQLite to scan all rows with a maximally permissive pattern, which is a minor resource exhaustion vector.\n\n**Fix:** Escape LIKE metacharacters in search inputs before wrapping with `%`:\n```typescript\nconst escapedQ = q.replace(/[%_\\\\]/g, \"\\\\$&\");\nparams.push(`%${escapedQ}%`, `%${escapedQ}%`);\n```\nand add `ESCAPE '\\\\'` to the LIKE clause.\n\n---\n\n## Summary\n\n| # | Issue | Severity | Confidence |\n|---|-------|----------|------------|\n| 1 | SQL `ORDER BY` via string interpolation (structural injection risk) | Critical pattern | 95 |\n| 2 | Nodemailer transporter cached, credential changes not reflected | Important | 85 |\n| 3 | `auto-discord` uses `getSession()` instead of `requireAuth()` | Important | 90 |\n| 4 | Mark-all-notifications-read UPDATE is unbounded | Important | 82 |\n| 5 | Profile reading history query has no LIMIT | Important | 82 |\n| 6 | Telegram/Discord link client state can drift after save | Important | 83 |\n| 7 | LIKE wildcard metacharacters not escaped in search inputs | Important | 85 |\n\nThe most actionable fixes in priority order are: #3 (one-line fix, security model consistency), #7 (prevents resource exhaustion and unexpected search behavior), #4 and #5 (add LIMIT clauses), and #2 (transporter caching logic). Issue #1 is safe today but the pattern should be hardened against future regressions.\n\n**Relevant files:**\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/route.ts`\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/auto-discord/route.ts`\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/notifications/route.ts`\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/profile/reading/route.ts`\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/listings/[id]/ListingDetail.tsx`\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/email.ts`"},{"type":"text","text":"agentId: ae63ab08b24daf7ae (use SendMessage with to: 'ae63ab08b24daf7ae' to continue this agent)\n<usage>total_tokens: 87999\ntool_uses: 46\nduration_ms: 131960</usage>"}]}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"2461f1b3-284a-46b0-accd-df8549bf2929","uuid":"182d356d-f66f-4588-b9ea-00b3252067be","timestamp":"2026-03-24T12:03:22.494Z","tool_use_result":{"status":"completed","prompt":"Review the Bookmate codebase at /root/Projects/bookmate/app for bugs and issues. \n\nPrevious 14 rounds have already covered: race conditions, TOCTOU, type validation, transaction safety, auth issues, module-load freezing, input validation, FK violations, stale data, error UX, timing attacks, email HTML escaping, stale sessions, orphaned applications, is_full gaps, transaction array mutation.\n\nFocus on NEW angles not previously covered:\n1. **Memory leaks** — event listeners not cleaned up, intervals not cleared\n2. **SQL injection** — any string concatenation in SQL queries\n3. **Resource exhaustion** — unbounded queries, missing LIMIT clauses, large payloads\n4. **Error recovery** — are errors properly caught and reported, or do they silently swallow failures\n5. **Cookie/session edge cases** — session expiry, cookie attributes\n6. **Client-side state bugs** — React state that could get out of sync\n7. **API response inconsistencies** — different error formats, missing fields\n8. **Data integrity** — orphan records, impossible states in the DB\n\nLook at:\n- app/app/api/ (all route handlers)\n- app/app/ (all page components)\n- app/components/\n- app/lib/\n\nOnly report HIGH confidence bugs with clear impact. Do NOT report style issues or minor nits.","agentId":"ae63ab08b24daf7ae","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Now I have everything I need. Let me compile the findings.\n\n---\n\nReviewing the Bookmate codebase at `/root/Projects/bookmate/app`. The review covers all API routes, lib utilities, and client components against the new angles specified.\n\n---\n\n## Critical Issues\n\n### 1. SQL Injection via Unvalidated `sort` Parameter in ORDER BY Clause\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/route.ts`, lines 53-55 and 75\n\n**Confidence: 95**\n\nThe `sort` query parameter controls `orderClause`, which is interpolated directly into the SQL string template at line 75:\n\n```\nORDER BY ${orderClause}\n```\n\nThe validation at lines 53-55 only assigns three safe string literals for `\"oldest\"` and `\"start_date\"`, and defaults to `\"l.created_at DESC\"` for anything else. That default handling looks correct. However, this is a fragile structure — if a future developer adds a new sort option using user input directly, or if the logic is modified, it becomes injection. More critically, the `whereClause` is also string-interpolated (lines 62 and 74):\n\n```\n`SELECT COUNT(*) as total FROM listings l WHERE ${whereClause}`\n`WHERE ${whereClause}`\n```\n\nThe `whereClause` is built by joining fixed string literals from `conditions[]`, and all user-controlled values go into `params[]` as bound parameters, so the WHERE clause itself is safe. However, `orderClause` is a raw string inserted without parameterization. The current three-value allowlist makes it safe today, but this pattern is one wrong edit away from full SQL injection. The order clause string is set only from three hardcoded literals with no user input reaching it, so this is a code quality risk rather than a live injection, but it warrants being highlighted since the structural pattern is dangerous.\n\n**Fix:** Replace the string interpolation with an allowlist map:\n```typescript\nconst ORDER_MAP: Record<string, string> = {\n  oldest: \"l.created_at ASC\",\n  start_date: \"l.start_date ASC\",\n  newest: \"l.created_at DESC\",\n};\nconst orderClause = ORDER_MAP[sort] ?? \"l.created_at DESC\";\n```\n\n---\n\n## Important Issues\n\n### 2. `email.ts` — Nodemailer Transporter Cached After First Creation, Config Changes Not Reflected\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/email.ts`, lines 16-36\n\n**Confidence: 85**\n\nThe module-level `transporter` variable is created once and cached:\n\n```typescript\nlet transporter: nodemailer.Transporter | null = null;\n\nfunction getTransporter(): nodemailer.Transporter | null {\n  if (!checkConfigured()) return null;\n  if (!transporter) {\n    transporter = nodemailer.createTransport({ ... });\n  }\n  return transporter;\n}\n```\n\nThe `checkConfigured()` call reads from `process.env` each time, which is the intended pattern (consistent with the comment in the file). However, `getTransporter()` only creates the transporter once. If SMTP credentials are set after the first call (e.g., if `SMTP_HOST` is set but `SMTP_PASS` is changed), the transporter is never recreated. The cached transporter will use the old credentials indefinitely until the process restarts.\n\nMore concretely: if `checkConfigured()` returns true on the second call (e.g., `SMTP_FROM` was added after startup), the transporter was already built on the first call without `SMTP_FROM`, and the cached instance will send emails without the `from` address being configured. This is a silent misconfiguration that can result in emails failing at send time rather than at startup, making it hard to diagnose.\n\n**Fix:** Either build the transporter at startup (fail fast) or re-check all SMTP vars when creating it. At minimum, hash the relevant env vars and invalidate the cache when they change.\n\n---\n\n### 3. `auto-discord/route.ts` — Uses `getSession()` Instead of `requireAuth()`, Bypassing DB User-Existence Check\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/auto-discord/route.ts`, lines 10-16\n\n**Confidence: 90**\n\nThis route uses `getSession()` directly and checks `session.userId` manually:\n\n```typescript\nconst session = await getSession();\nif (!session.userId) {\n  return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n}\n```\n\nEvery other authenticated route in the codebase uses `requireAuth()`, which additionally verifies the user still exists in the DB and invalidates sessions for deleted users. This route does not perform that check. A deleted user's valid cookie can reach the author-check at line 39 with a userId that no longer exists in the DB, potentially matching `listing.author_id` if the user was deleted and a new user was created with the same row ID (SQLite AUTOINCREMENT makes this extremely unlikely but not impossible as a concern).\n\nMore practically, this is an inconsistency with the security model — the comment in `requireAuth()` explicitly documents that the DB check is intended to prevent deleted users from accessing the API. This endpoint intentionally or accidentally circumvents that invariant.\n\n**Fix:** Replace `getSession()` + manual check with `requireAuth()` to match every other authenticated endpoint.\n\n---\n\n### 4. `notifications/route.ts` — `GET` Queries Up to 50 Rows But `PATCH` (Mark All Read) Is Unbounded\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/notifications/route.ts`, lines 34-57\n\n**Confidence: 82**\n\nThe GET handler correctly limits results to 50 rows. However, the PATCH handler that marks all notifications as read issues an unbounded `UPDATE`:\n\n```typescript\ndb.prepare(\"UPDATE notifications SET is_read = 1 WHERE user_id = ?\").run(session.userId);\n```\n\nIf a user accumulates thousands of notifications (either legitimately or due to a spam attack that bypasses rate limits), this UPDATE will lock the SQLite WAL for an extended write. Given SQLite's single-writer model, this can block all concurrent reads and writes during that window, degrading service for all users.\n\n**Fix:** Add a `LIMIT` to the mark-all-read UPDATE, or process it in batches. Alternatively, add a periodic cleanup job that deletes old read notifications.\n\n---\n\n### 5. `profile/reading/route.ts` — Unbounded Query Returns All Listings for a User\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/profile/reading/route.ts`, lines 29-50\n\n**Confidence: 82**\n\nThe query fetches all listings a user is a member of without any `LIMIT`:\n\n```typescript\nconst readings = db.prepare(`\n  SELECT ... FROM listing_members lm\n  JOIN listings l ON l.id = lm.listing_id\n  ...\n  WHERE lm.user_id = ?\n  ORDER BY lm.joined_at DESC\n`).all(session.userId) as ReadingRow[];\n```\n\nA power user who has joined many groups, or a test account, could receive a very large payload. The result is then serialized to JSON and sent in a single response. There is no pagination, and no cap on the number of rows returned. While today's dataset is small, as the site scales this endpoint will become a memory and bandwidth concern, and could be used as a denial-of-service vector against authenticated users.\n\n**Fix:** Add a reasonable `LIMIT` (e.g., 200) and consider paginating the reading history, at minimum.\n\n---\n\n### 6. `ListingDetail.tsx` — Client-Side State for `telegramLink` / `discordLink` Can Drift After Successful Save\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/listings/[id]/ListingDetail.tsx`, lines 86-103 and 266-336\n\n**Confidence: 83**\n\nWhen the user saves a Telegram link (`handleTelegramSave`) or Discord link (`handleDiscordSave`), on success the component calls `fetchListing()` to refresh all listing data. This is correct.\n\nHowever, the `telegramLink` and `discordLink` local state variables (lines 60, 66) are initialized from the fetched listing:\n\n```typescript\nsetTelegramLink(data.listing.telegram_link || \"\");\nsetDiscordLink(data.listing.discord_link || \"\");\n```\n\nBut if the API returns a listing where `telegram_link` / `discord_link` is stripped (i.e., the user is a non-member, non-author, where these fields are deleted server-side per the access control logic in `[id]/route.ts` lines 95-99), these local state values will be reset to `\"\"` after `fetchListing()`. This means if the author saves a link, the input resets to empty on the next fetch, which could lead them to believe the save failed or cause them to re-submit.\n\nIn practice the author always satisfies `isAuthor`, so the fields are included in the response. But the logic is subtle and could silently regress if the access control logic changes.\n\n**Fix:** This is a latent issue. The fix is to drive `telegramLink` display state from `listing.telegram_link` directly rather than a parallel state variable, or to explicitly not reset state when `fetchListing()` returns a non-author view.\n\n---\n\n### 7. `listings/route.ts` — `q` and `readingPace` Input Contains LIKE Wildcards That Aren't Escaped\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/route.ts`, lines 36-48\n\n**Confidence: 85**\n\nUser-supplied search strings `q` and `readingPace` are interpolated as LIKE patterns using parameterized queries:\n\n```typescript\nparams.push(`%${q}%`, `%${q}%`);\nparams.push(`%${readingPace}%`);\n```\n\nThe parameterization prevents SQL injection, but `%` and `_` characters inside `q` and `readingPace` are LIKE wildcard metacharacters. A user searching for `%` will match every row (equivalent to no filter). A user searching for `_` will match any single character. While this is not a security vulnerability, it means a search for `%` returns all listings, potentially revealing listings the user isn't supposed to see (the list endpoint already filters `is_full = 0`, so visibility exposure is limited). More importantly, a search for `%%%%` causes SQLite to scan all rows with a maximally permissive pattern, which is a minor resource exhaustion vector.\n\n**Fix:** Escape LIKE metacharacters in search inputs before wrapping with `%`:\n```typescript\nconst escapedQ = q.replace(/[%_\\\\]/g, \"\\\\$&\");\nparams.push(`%${escapedQ}%`, `%${escapedQ}%`);\n```\nand add `ESCAPE '\\\\'` to the LIKE clause.\n\n---\n\n## Summary\n\n| # | Issue | Severity | Confidence |\n|---|-------|----------|------------|\n| 1 | SQL `ORDER BY` via string interpolation (structural injection risk) | Critical pattern | 95 |\n| 2 | Nodemailer transporter cached, credential changes not reflected | Important | 85 |\n| 3 | `auto-discord` uses `getSession()` instead of `requireAuth()` | Important | 90 |\n| 4 | Mark-all-notifications-read UPDATE is unbounded | Important | 82 |\n| 5 | Profile reading history query has no LIMIT | Important | 82 |\n| 6 | Telegram/Discord link client state can drift after save | Important | 83 |\n| 7 | LIKE wildcard metacharacters not escaped in search inputs | Important | 85 |\n\nThe most actionable fixes in priority order are: #3 (one-line fix, security model consistency), #7 (prevents resource exhaustion and unexpected search behavior), #4 and #5 (add LIMIT clauses), and #2 (transporter caching logic). Issue #1 is safe today but the pattern should be hardened against future regressions.\n\n**Relevant files:**\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/route.ts`\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/auto-discord/route.ts`\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/notifications/route.ts`\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/profile/reading/route.ts`\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/listings/[id]/ListingDetail.tsx`\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/email.ts`"}],"totalDurationMs":131960,"totalTokens":87999,"totalToolUseCount":46,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":686,"cache_read_input_tokens":84249,"output_tokens":3063,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":686},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"}}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01Nn3VckvSYyQ4cvww7jLXf5","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"Good findings from both agents. Let me now plan what to implement this session:\n\nFrom the code review (round 15), the actionable fixes are:\n1. `auto-discord` uses `getSession()` instead of `requireAuth()` - easy fix, security consistency\n2. LIKE wildcard metacharacters not escaped in search inputs\n3. Mark-all-notifications-read UPDATE is unbounded\n4. Profile reading history query has no LIMIT\n5. Nodemailer transporter cached, credential changes not reflected\n6. ORDER BY string interpolation hardening\n7. Client-side state drift (lower priority, latent)\n\n\n8. ON DELETE CASCADE would help with listing-related tables but risks notification preservation, and the current manual deletion code already handles this correctly anyway.\n\nI'll focus on implementing the six concrete fixes from the code review since they're actual bugs, while skipping the CASCADE migration—there's no user deletion in the app yet, listing deletion already manages child records manually, and recreating tables in production is too risky for a mostly theoretical benefit.","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":3,"cache_creation_input_tokens":5941,"cache_read_input_tokens":26255,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":5941},"output_tokens":9,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"2461f1b3-284a-46b0-accd-df8549bf2929","uuid":"ce3effdb-155a-44ed-a3ff-17e7f0720e28"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_011ZbYAE6HUPUimYttBZzwCw","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→\n     4→const PAGE_SIZE = 20;\n     5→\n     6→export async function GET(request: NextRequest) {\n     7→  const { searchParams } = request.nextUrl;\n     8→\n     9→  const q = searchParams.get(\"q\")?.trim() || \"\";\n    10→  const meetingFormat = searchParams.get(\"meeting_format\") || \"\";\n    11→  const readingPace = searchParams.get(\"reading_pace\") || \"\";\n    12→  const startDateFrom = searchParams.get(\"start_date_from\") || \"\";\n    13→  const sort = searchParams.get(\"sort\") || \"newest\";\n    14→  const includePast = searchParams.get(\"include_past\") === \"true\";\n    15→  const pageParam = parseInt(searchParams.get(\"page\") || \"1\", 10);\n    16→  const page = isNaN(pageParam) || pageParam < 1 ? 1 : Math.min(pageParam, 1000);\n    17→\n    18→  const conditions: string[] = [\"l.is_full = 0\"];\n    19→  const params: (string | number)[] = [];\n    20→\n    21→  // Apply date floor: use the more restrictive of today or startDateFrom\n    22→  const today = new Date().toISOString().split(\"T\")[0];\n    23→  const hasStartDateFilter = startDateFrom && /^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2}$/.test(startDateFrom);\n    24→\n    25→  if (!includePast) {\n    26→    // Use whichever is later: today or the explicit start_date_from filter\n    27→    const effectiveDate = hasStartDateFilter && startDateFrom > today ? startDateFrom : today;\n    28→    conditions.push(\"l.start_date >= ?\");\n    29→    params.push(effectiveDate);\n    30→  } else if (hasStartDateFilter) {\n    31→    // includePast is true, but user wants a specific start date floor\n    32→    conditions.push(\"l.start_date >= ?\");\n    33→    params.push(startDateFrom);\n    34→  }\n    35→\n    36→  if (q && q.length <= 300) {\n    37→    conditions.push(\"(l.book_title LIKE ? OR l.book_author LIKE ?)\");\n    38→    params.push(`%${q}%`, `%${q}%`);\n    39→  }\n    40→\n    41→  if (meetingFormat && [\"voice\", \"text\", \"mixed\"].includes(meetingFormat)) {\n    42→    conditions.push(\"l.meeting_format = ?\");\n    43→    params.push(meetingFormat);\n    44→  }\n    45→\n    46→  if (readingPace && readingPace.length <= 200) {\n    47→    conditions.push(\"l.reading_pace LIKE ?\");\n    48→    params.push(`%${readingPace}%`);\n    49→  }\n    50→\n    51→  const whereClause = conditions.join(\" AND \");\n    52→\n    53→  let orderClause = \"l.created_at DESC\";\n    54→  if (sort === \"oldest\") orderClause = \"l.created_at ASC\";\n    55→  else if (sort === \"start_date\") orderClause = \"l.start_date ASC\";\n    56→\n    57→  const offset = (page - 1) * PAGE_SIZE;\n    58→\n    59→  // Get total count for pagination metadata\n    60→  const countResult = db\n    61→    .prepare(\n    62→      `SELECT COUNT(*) as total FROM listings l WHERE ${whereClause}`\n    63→    )\n    64→    .get(...params) as { total: number };\n    65→\n    66→  const listings = db\n    67→    .prepare(\n    68→      `SELECT\n    69→        l.*,\n    70→        u.display_name as author_name,\n    71→        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = l.id) as member_count\n    72→      FROM listings l\n    73→      JOIN users u ON l.author_id = u.id\n    74→      WHERE ${whereClause}\n    75→      ORDER BY ${orderClause}\n    76→      LIMIT ? OFFSET ?`\n    77→    )\n    78→    .all(...params, PAGE_SIZE, offset);\n    79→\n    80→  return NextResponse.json({\n    81→    listings,\n    82→    pagination: {\n    83→      page,\n    84→      pageSize: PAGE_SIZE,\n    85→      total: countResult.total,\n    86→      totalPages: Math.ceil(countResult.total / PAGE_SIZE),\n    87→    },\n    88→  });\n    89→}\n    90→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"2461f1b3-284a-46b0-accd-df8549bf2929","uuid":"3c663d07-775c-4f31-adf7-96054ab92f16","timestamp":"2026-03-24T12:03:39.650Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/route.ts","content":"import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\nimport db from \"@/lib/db\";\n\nconst PAGE_SIZE = 20;\n\nexport async function GET(request: NextRequest) {\n  const { searchParams } = request.nextUrl;\n\n  const q = searchParams.get(\"q\")?.trim() || \"\";\n  const meetingFormat = searchParams.get(\"meeting_format\") || \"\";\n  const readingPace = searchParams.get(\"reading_pace\") || \"\";\n  const startDateFrom = searchParams.get(\"start_date_from\") || \"\";\n  const sort = searchParams.get(\"sort\") || \"newest\";\n  const includePast = searchParams.get(\"include_past\") === \"true\";\n  const pageParam = parseInt(searchParams.get(\"page\") || \"1\", 10);\n  const page = isNaN(pageParam) || pageParam < 1 ? 1 : Math.min(pageParam, 1000);\n\n  const conditions: string[] = [\"l.is_full = 0\"];\n  const params: (string | number)[] = [];\n\n  // Apply date floor: use the more restrictive of today or startDateFrom\n  const today = new Date().toISOString().split(\"T\")[0];\n  const hasStartDateFilter = startDateFrom && /^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2}$/.test(startDateFrom);\n\n  if (!includePast) {\n    // Use whichever is later: today or the explicit start_date_from filter\n    const effectiveDate = hasStartDateFilter && startDateFrom > today ? startDateFrom : today;\n    conditions.push(\"l.start_date >= ?\");\n    params.push(effectiveDate);\n  } else if (hasStartDateFilter) {\n    // includePast is true, but user wants a specific start date floor\n    conditions.push(\"l.start_date >= ?\");\n    params.push(startDateFrom);\n  }\n\n  if (q && q.length <= 300) {\n    conditions.push(\"(l.book_title LIKE ? OR l.book_author LIKE ?)\");\n    params.push(`%${q}%`, `%${q}%`);\n  }\n\n  if (meetingFormat && [\"voice\", \"text\", \"mixed\"].includes(meetingFormat)) {\n    conditions.push(\"l.meeting_format = ?\");\n    params.push(meetingFormat);\n  }\n\n  if (readingPace && readingPace.length <= 200) {\n    conditions.push(\"l.reading_pace LIKE ?\");\n    params.push(`%${readingPace}%`);\n  }\n\n  const whereClause = conditions.join(\" AND \");\n\n  let orderClause = \"l.created_at DESC\";\n  if (sort === \"oldest\") orderClause = \"l.created_at ASC\";\n  else if (sort === \"start_date\") orderClause = \"l.start_date ASC\";\n\n  const offset = (page - 1) * PAGE_SIZE;\n\n  // Get total count for pagination metadata\n  const countResult = db\n    .prepare(\n      `SELECT COUNT(*) as total FROM listings l WHERE ${whereClause}`\n    )\n    .get(...params) as { total: number };\n\n  const listings = db\n    .prepare(\n      `SELECT\n        l.*,\n        u.display_name as author_name,\n        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = l.id) as member_count\n      FROM listings l\n      JOIN users u ON l.author_id = u.id\n      WHERE ${whereClause}\n      ORDER BY ${orderClause}\n      LIMIT ? OFFSET ?`\n    )\n    .all(...params, PAGE_SIZE, offset);\n\n  return NextResponse.json({\n    listings,\n    pagination: {\n      page,\n      pageSize: PAGE_SIZE,\n      total: countResult.total,\n      totalPages: Math.ceil(countResult.total / PAGE_SIZE),\n    },\n  });\n}\n","numLines":90,"startLine":1,"totalLines":90}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01PSTtBVTyzkr4ZAwB1hXYUv","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { getSession } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→import { isBotConfigured, generateBotInviteUrl } from \"@/lib/discord\";\n     5→\n     6→export async function GET(\n     7→  _req: NextRequest,\n     8→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n     9→) {\n    10→  const session = await getSession();\n    11→  if (!session.userId) {\n    12→    return NextResponse.json(\n    13→      { error: \"Authentication required\" },\n    14→      { status: 401 }\n    15→    );\n    16→  }\n    17→\n    18→  if (!isBotConfigured()) {\n    19→    return NextResponse.json(\n    20→      { error: \"Discord bot is not configured\" },\n    21→      { status: 503 }\n    22→    );\n    23→  }\n    24→\n    25→  const { id } = await params;\n    26→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n    27→  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n    28→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid listing ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n    29→  }\n    30→\n    31→  const listing = db\n    32→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    33→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    34→\n    35→  if (!listing) {\n    36→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n    37→  }\n    38→\n    39→  if (listing.author_id !== session.userId) {\n    40→    return NextResponse.json(\n    41→      { error: \"Only the listing author can set up the Discord group\" },\n    42→      { status: 403 }\n    43→    );\n    44→  }\n    45→\n    46→  if (!listing.is_full) {\n    47→    return NextResponse.json(\n    48→      { error: \"The group is not full yet\" },\n    49→      { status: 400 }\n    50→    );\n    51→  }\n    52→\n    53→  if (listing.discord_link) {\n    54→    return NextResponse.json(\n    55→      { error: \"A Discord link has already been set\" },\n    56→      { status: 400 }\n    57→    );\n    58→  }\n    59→\n    60→  const inviteUrl = generateBotInviteUrl(listingId);\n    61→\n    62→  return NextResponse.json({\n    63→    inviteUrl,\n    64→    instructions:\n    65→      \"Click the link to add the Bookmate bot to your Discord server. \" +\n    66→      \"The bot will create a reading group channel and generate an invite link for your members.\",\n    67→  });\n    68→}\n    69→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"2461f1b3-284a-46b0-accd-df8549bf2929","uuid":"0c90d614-688d-45ad-be01-0b6a82aad905","timestamp":"2026-03-24T12:03:39.990Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/auto-discord/route.ts","content":"import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\nimport db from \"@/lib/db\";\nimport { getSession } from \"@/lib/session\";\nimport { isBotConfigured, generateBotInviteUrl } from \"@/lib/discord\";\n\nexport async function GET(\n  _req: NextRequest,\n  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n) {\n  const session = await getSession();\n  if (!session.userId) {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"Authentication required\" },\n      { status: 401 }\n    );\n  }\n\n  if (!isBotConfigured()) {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"Discord bot is not configured\" },\n      { status: 503 }\n    );\n  }\n\n  const { id } = await params;\n  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid listing ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n  }\n\n  const listing = db\n    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n\n  if (!listing) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n  }\n\n  if (listing.author_id !== session.userId) {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"Only the listing author can set up the Discord group\" },\n      { status: 403 }\n    );\n  }\n\n  if (!listing.is_full) {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"The group is not full yet\" },\n      { status: 400 }\n    );\n  }\n\n  if (listing.discord_link) {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"A Discord link has already been set\" },\n      { status: 400 }\n    );\n  }\n\n  const inviteUrl = generateBotInviteUrl(listingId);\n\n  return NextResponse.json({\n    inviteUrl,\n    instructions:\n      \"Click the link to add the Bookmate bot to your Discord server. \" +\n      \"The bot will create a reading group channel and generate an invite link for your members.\",\n  });\n}\n","numLines":69,"startLine":1,"totalLines":69}}}

{"type":"stream_event","event":{"type":"content_block_start","index":5,"content_block":{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01L71N9ftKPsghmeTEcsMdxg","name":"Read","input":{},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}},"session_id":"2461f1b3-284a-46b0-accd-df8549bf2929","parent_tool_use_id":null,"uuid":"e485ab27-81de-4d60-8cad-da54614efdf3"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_017i1BW1n2HqTXaqUjYXxpjV","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→\n     5→export async function GET() {\n     6→  const session = await requireAuth();\n     7→  if (!session) {\n     8→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n     9→  }\n    10→\n    11→  const notifications = db\n    12→    .prepare(\n    13→      `SELECT n.*, l.book_title\n    14→       FROM notifications n\n    15→       LEFT JOIN listings l ON n.listing_id = l.id\n    16→       WHERE n.user_id = ?\n    17→       ORDER BY n.created_at DESC\n    18→       LIMIT 50`\n    19→    )\n    20→    .all(session.userId);\n    21→\n    22→  const unreadCount = db\n    23→    .prepare(\n    24→      \"SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM notifications WHERE user_id = ? AND is_read = 0\"\n    25→    )\n    26→    .get(session.userId) as { count: number };\n    27→\n    28→  return NextResponse.json({\n    29→    notifications,\n    30→    unreadCount: unreadCount.count,\n    31→  });\n    32→}\n    33→\n    34→export async function PATCH(req: NextRequest) {\n    35→  const session = await requireAuth();\n    36→  if (!session) {\n    37→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n    38→  }\n    39→\n    40→  const { notificationId } = await req.json();\n    41→\n    42→  if (notificationId) {\n    43→    // Validate notificationId is a positive integer\n    44→    if (typeof notificationId !== \"number\" || !Number.isInteger(notificationId) || notificationId < 1) {\n    45→      return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid notification ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n    46→    }\n    47→    db.prepare(\n    48→      \"UPDATE notifications SET is_read = 1 WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?\"\n    49→    ).run(notificationId, session.userId);\n    50→  } else {\n    51→    // Mark all as read\n    52→    db.prepare(\n    53→      \"UPDATE notifications SET is_read = 1 WHERE user_id = ?\"\n    54→    ).run(session.userId);\n    55→  }\n    56→\n    57→  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n    58→}\n    59→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"2461f1b3-284a-46b0-accd-df8549bf2929","uuid":"6a358fb8-9bbf-4ff9-a96a-7ba96a0c5021","timestamp":"2026-03-24T12:03:40.153Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/notifications/route.ts","content":"import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\nimport db from \"@/lib/db\";\nimport { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n\nexport async function GET() {\n  const session = await requireAuth();\n  if (!session) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n  }\n\n  const notifications = db\n    .prepare(\n      `SELECT n.*, l.book_title\n       FROM notifications n\n       LEFT JOIN listings l ON n.listing_id = l.id\n       WHERE n.user_id = ?\n       ORDER BY n.created_at DESC\n       LIMIT 50`\n    )\n    .all(session.userId);\n\n  const unreadCount = db\n    .prepare(\n      \"SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM notifications WHERE user_id = ? AND is_read = 0\"\n    )\n    .get(session.userId) as { count: number };\n\n  return NextResponse.json({\n    notifications,\n    unreadCount: unreadCount.count,\n  });\n}\n\nexport async function PATCH(req: NextRequest) {\n  const session = await requireAuth();\n  if (!session) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n  }\n\n  const { notificationId } = await req.json();\n\n  if (notificationId) {\n    // Validate notificationId is a positive integer\n    if (typeof notificationId !== \"number\" || !Number.isInteger(notificationId) || notificationId < 1) {\n      return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid notification ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n    }\n    db.prepare(\n      \"UPDATE notifications SET is_read = 1 WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?\"\n    ).run(notificationId, session.userId);\n  } else {\n    // Mark all as read\n    db.prepare(\n      \"UPDATE notifications SET is_read = 1 WHERE user_id = ?\"\n    ).run(session.userId);\n  }\n\n  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n}\n","numLines":59,"startLine":1,"totalLines":59}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01L71N9ftKPsghmeTEcsMdxg","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→\n     5→interface ReadingRow {\n     6→  id: number;\n     7→  book_title: string;\n     8→  book_author: string;\n     9→  book_cover_url: string;\n    10→  reading_pace: string;\n    11→  start_date: string;\n    12→  meeting_format: string;\n    13→  is_full: number;\n    14→  telegram_link: string;\n    15→  discord_link: string;\n    16→  member_count: number;\n    17→  max_group_size: number;\n    18→  author_name: string;\n    19→  joined_at: string;\n    20→}\n    21→\n    22→export async function GET() {\n    23→  const session = await requireAuth();\n    24→  if (!session) {\n    25→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n    26→  }\n    27→\n    28→  // Get all listings the user is a member of (including ones they authored)\n    29→  const readings = db.prepare(`\n    30→    SELECT\n    31→      l.id,\n    32→      l.book_title,\n    33→      l.book_author,\n    34→      l.book_cover_url,\n    35→      l.reading_pace,\n    36→      l.start_date,\n    37→      l.meeting_format,\n    38→      l.is_full,\n    39→      l.telegram_link,\n    40→      l.discord_link,\n    41→      l.max_group_size,\n    42→      u.display_name as author_name,\n    43→      lm.joined_at,\n    44→      (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = l.id) as member_count\n    45→    FROM listing_members lm\n    46→    JOIN listings l ON l.id = lm.listing_id\n    47→    JOIN users u ON u.id = l.author_id\n    48→    WHERE lm.user_id = ?\n    49→    ORDER BY lm.joined_at DESC\n    50→  `).all(session.userId) as ReadingRow[];\n    51→\n    52→  const today = new Date().toISOString().split(\"T\")[0];\n    53→\n    54→  // Reading history: completed groups (full + start_date in the past)\n    55→  // Upcoming: not yet started and not full\n    56→  // Active: everything else that has started or is full (but not completed)\n    57→  const isHistory = (r: ReadingRow) => r.is_full === 1 && r.start_date < today;\n    58→  const isUpcoming = (r: ReadingRow) => r.is_full === 0 && r.start_date > today;\n    59→\n    60→  const history = readings.filter(isHistory);\n    61→  const upcoming = readings.filter(isUpcoming);\n    62→  const active = readings.filter(\n    63→    (r) => !isHistory(r) && !isUpcoming(r) && (r.is_full === 1 || r.start_date <= today)\n    64→  );\n    65→\n    66→  return NextResponse.json({\n    67→    active,\n    68→    history,\n    69→    upcoming,\n    70→    total: readings.length,\n    71→  });\n    72→}\n    73→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"2461f1b3-284a-46b0-accd-df8549bf2929","uuid":"5b0d39af-7aa8-46c2-a16b-2b98cfbcd848","timestamp":"2026-03-24T12:03:40.356Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/profile/reading/route.ts","content":"import { NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\nimport db from \"@/lib/db\";\nimport { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n\ninterface ReadingRow {\n  id: number;\n  book_title: string;\n  book_author: string;\n  book_cover_url: string;\n  reading_pace: string;\n  start_date: string;\n  meeting_format: string;\n  is_full: number;\n  telegram_link: string;\n  discord_link: string;\n  member_count: number;\n  max_group_size: number;\n  author_name: string;\n  joined_at: string;\n}\n\nexport async function GET() {\n  const session = await requireAuth();\n  if (!session) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n  }\n\n  // Get all listings the user is a member of (including ones they authored)\n  const readings = db.prepare(`\n    SELECT\n      l.id,\n      l.book_title,\n      l.book_author,\n      l.book_cover_url,\n      l.reading_pace,\n      l.start_date,\n      l.meeting_format,\n      l.is_full,\n      l.telegram_link,\n      l.discord_link,\n      l.max_group_size,\n      u.display_name as author_name,\n      lm.joined_at,\n      (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = l.id) as member_count\n    FROM listing_members lm\n    JOIN listings l ON l.id = lm.listing_id\n    JOIN users u ON u.id = l.author_id\n    WHERE lm.user_id = ?\n    ORDER BY lm.joined_at DESC\n  `).all(session.userId) as ReadingRow[];\n\n  const today = new Date().toISOString().split(\"T\")[0];\n\n  // Reading history: completed groups (full + start_date in the past)\n  // Upcoming: not yet started and not full\n  // Active: everything else that has started or is full (but not completed)\n  const isHistory = (r: ReadingRow) => r.is_full === 1 && r.start_date < today;\n  const isUpcoming = (r: ReadingRow) => r.is_full === 0 && r.start_date > today;\n\n  const history = readings.filter(isHistory);\n  const upcoming = readings.filter(isUpcoming);\n  const active = readings.filter(\n    (r) => !isHistory(r) && !isUpcoming(r) && (r.is_full === 1 || r.start_date <= today)\n  );\n\n  return NextResponse.json({\n    active,\n    history,\n    upcoming,\n    total: readings.length,\n  });\n}\n","numLines":73,"startLine":1,"totalLines":73}}}

   4→ * Check SMTP config live from process.env on each call — not at module load time.\n     5→ * Module-level constants would be frozen when the file is first imported,\n     6→ * permanently returning false if SMTP vars aren't set at that exact moment.\n     7→ */\n     8→function checkConfigured(): boolean {\n     9→  return (\n    10→    !!process.env.SMTP_HOST &&\n    11→    !!process.env.SMTP_PORT &&\n    12→    !!process.env.SMTP_FROM\n    13→  );\n    14→}\n    15→\n    16→let transporter: nodemailer.Transporter | null = null;\n    17→\n    18→function getTransporter(): nodemailer.Transporter | null {\n    19→  if (!checkConfigured()) return null;\n    20→  if (!transporter) {\n    21→    transporter = nodemailer.createTransport({\n    22→      host: process.env.SMTP_HOST,\n    23→      port: parseInt(process.env.SMTP_PORT || \"587\", 10),\n    24→      secure: process.env.SMTP_SECURE === \"true\",\n    25→      ...(process.env.SMTP_USER && process.env.SMTP_PASS\n    26→        ? {\n    27→            auth: {\n    28→              user: process.env.SMTP_USER,\n    29→              pass: process.env.SMTP_PASS,\n    30→            },\n    31→          }\n    32→        : {}),\n    33→    });\n    34→  }\n    35→  return transporter;\n    36→}\n    37→\n    38→export async function sendEmail(\n    39→  to: string,\n    40→  subject: string,\n    41→  text: string,\n    42→  html?: string\n    43→): Promise<boolean> {\n    44→  const t = getTransporter();\n    45→  if (!t) return false;\n    46→\n    47→  try {\n    48→    await t.sendMail({\n    49→      from: process.env.SMTP_FROM,\n    50→      to,\n    51→      subject,\n    52→      text,\n    53→      html: html || text,\n    54→    });\n    55→    return true;\n    56→  } catch (err) {\n    57→    console.error(\"Failed to send email:\", err);\n    58→    return false;\n    59→  }\n    60→}\n    61→\n    62→export function isEmailConfigured(): boolean {\n    63→ 65→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"2461f1b3-284a-46b0-accd-df8549bf2929","uuid":"5d7f26a3-21e4-4338-858c-87035910865f","timestamp":"2026-03-24T12:03:40.439Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/email.ts","content":"import nodemailer from \"nodemailer\";\n\n/**\n * Check SMTP config live from process.env on each call — not at module load time.\n * Module-level constants would be frozen when the file is first imported,\n * permanently returning false if SMTP vars aren't set at that exact moment.\n */\nfunction checkConfigured(): boolean {\n  return (\n    !!process.env.SMTP_HOST &&\n    !!process.env.SMTP_PORT &&\n    !!process.env.SMTP_FROM\n  );\n}\n\nlet transporter: nodemailer.Transporter | null = null;\n\nfunction getTransporter(): nodemailer.Transporter | null {\n  if (!checkConfigured()) return null;\n  if (!transporter) {\n    transporter = nodemailer.createTransport({\n      host: process.env.SMTP_HOST,\n      port: parseInt(process.env.SMTP_PORT || \"587\", 10),\n      secure: process.env.SMTP_SECURE === \"true\",\n      ...(process.env.SMTP_USER && process.env.SMTP_PASS\n        ? {\n            auth: {\n              user: process.env.SMTP_USER,\n              pass: process.env.SMTP_PASS,\n            },\n          }\n        : {}),\n    });\n  }\n  return transporter;\n}\n\nexport async function sendEmail(\n  to: string,\n  subject: string,\n  text: string,\n  html?: string\n): Promise<boolean> {\n  const t = getTransporter();\n  if (!t) return false;\n\n  try {\n    await t.sendMail({\n      from: process.env.SMTP_FROM,\n      to,\n      subject,\n      text,\n      html: html || text,\n    });\n    return true;\n  } catch (err) {\n    console.error(\"Failed to send email:\", err);\n    return false;\n  }\n}\n\nexport function isEmailConfigured(): boolean {\n  return checkConfigured();\n}\n","numLines":65,"startLine":1,"totalLines":65}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_015ALhYKisUKGNt9g8nj36Hk","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→import { isBotConfigured, generateGroupDeepLink } from \"@/lib/telegram\";\n     5→\n     6→/**\n     7→ * GET /api/listings/[id]/auto-telegram\n     8→ *\n     9→ * Returns the Telegram deep link for one-click group creation.\n    10→ * Only available to the listing author when the group is full\n    11→ * and no Telegram link has been set yet.\n    12→ */\n    13→export async function GET(\n    14→  _req: NextRequest,\n    15→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n    16→) {\n    17→  const session = await requireAuth();\n    18→  if (!session) {\n    19→    return NextResponse.json(\n    20→      { error: \"Authentication required\" },"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"2461f1b3-284a-46b0-accd-df8549bf2929","uuid":"4734e1dd-7174-472c-a86e-c0b3204282f6","timestamp":"2026-03-24T12:03:49.326Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/auto-telegram/route.ts","content":"import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\nimport db from \"@/lib/db\";\nimport { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\nimport { isBotConfigured, generateGroupDeepLink } from \"@/lib/telegram\";\n\n/**\n * GET /api/listings/[id]/auto-telegram\n *\n * Returns the Telegram deep link for one-click group creation.\n * Only available to the listing author when the group is full\n * and no Telegram link has been set yet.\n */\nexport async function GET(\n  _req: NextRequest,\n  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n) {\n  const session = await requireAuth();\n  if (!session) {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"Authentication required\" },","numLines":20,"startLine":1,"totalLines":81}}}

{"type":"stream_event","event":{"type":"content_block_delta","index":1,"delta":{"type":"input_json_delta","partial_json":"\"}"}},"session_id":"2461f1b3-284a-46b0-accd-df8549bf2929","parent_tool_use_id":null,"uuid":"107216ef-2229-4a89-a387-c30f83d592ec"}
{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01DAnL7sbKLbyimJGUCxkTg1","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01DXouup9eQ7Qj1qG7ZchtLP","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"export GH_TOKEN=$(grep GH_TOKEN /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/.env | cut -d= -f2) && gh issue create \\\n  --title \"Code review round 15: LIKE injection, auth bypass, unbounded queries, transporter cache\" \\\n  --label \"approved,bug\" \\\n  --body \"$(cat <<'EOF'\n## Bugs Found\n\n1. **`auto-discord` uses `getSession()` instead of `requireAuth()`** — bypasses DB user-existence check, inconsistent with security model\n2. **LIKE wildcard metacharacters not escaped in search inputs** — `%` and `_` in search terms act as wildcards, `%` matches everything\n3. **Mark-all-notifications-read UPDATE is unbounded** — no LIMIT on bulk UPDATE, could lock SQLite WAL with thousands of rows\n4. **Profile reading history query has no LIMIT** — unbounded result set, potential memory/bandwidth issue\n5. **ORDER BY via string interpolation** — safe today (3-value allowlist) but fragile pattern, harden with explicit map\n6. **Nodemailer transporter cached permanently** — credential changes after first creation never reflected\n\n## Fix Plan\n- Fix 1: Replace `getSession()` with `requireAuth()` in auto-discord\n- Fix 2: Escape LIKE metacharacters (`%`, `_`, `\\`) before search\n- Fix 3: Add LIMIT to mark-all-read UPDATE (batch of 1000)\n- Fix 4: Add LIMIT 200 to reading history query\n- Fix 5: Use explicit ORDER_MAP for sort parameter\n- Fix 6: Invalidate transporter cache when env vars change\nEOF\n)\"","description":"Create issue for code review round 15"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":362,"cache_read_input_tokens":37523,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":362},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"2461f1b3-284a-46b0-accd-df8549bf2929","uuid":"5c43332b-56b1-4025-92b8-bbde0562fd5e"}

\"@/lib/discord\";\n\nexport async function GET(\n  _req: NextRequest,\n  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n) {\n  const session = await getSession();\n  if (!session.userId) {\n
