\"@/lib/db\";\nimport { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\nimport { isBotConfigured, generateBotInviteUrl } from \"@/lib/discord\";\n\nexport async function GET(\n  _req: NextRequest,\n  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n) {\n  const session = await requireAuth();\n  if (!session) {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"Authentication required\" },\n      { status: 401 }\n    );\n  }","originalFile":"import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\nimport db from \"@/lib/db\";\nimport { getSession } from \"@/lib/session\";\nimport { isBotConfigured, generateBotInviteUrl } from \"@/lib/discord\";\n\nexport async function GET(\n  _req: NextRequest,\n  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n) {\n  const session = await getSession();\n  if (!session.userId) {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"Authentication required\" },\n      { status: 401 }\n    );\n  }\n\n  if (!isBotConfigured()) {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"Discord bot is not configured\" },\n      { status: 503 }\n    );\n  }\n\n  const { id } = await params;\n  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid listing ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n  }\n\n  const listing = db\n    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n\n  if (!listing) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n  }\n\n  if (listing.author_id !== session.userId) {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"Only the listing author can set up the Discord group\" },\n      { status: 403 }\n    );\n  }\n\n  if (!listing.is_full) {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"The group is not full yet\" },\n      { status: 400 }\n    );\n  }\n\n  if (listing.discord_link) {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"A Discord link has already been set\" },\n      { status: 400 }\n    );\n  }\n\n  const inviteUrl = generateBotInviteUrl(listingId);\n\n  return NextResponse.json({\n    inviteUrl,\n    instructions:\n      \"Click the link to add the Bookmate bot to your Discord server. \" +\n      \"The bot will create a reading group channel and generate an invite link for your members.\",\n  });\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":1,"oldLines":14,"newStart":1,"newLines":14,"lines":[" import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";"," import db from \"@/lib/db\";","-import { {"type":"stream_event","event":{"type":"message_delta","delta":{"stop_reason":"tool_use","stop_sequence":null},"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":503,"cache_read_input_tokens":38644,"output_tokens":237},"context_management":{"applied_edits":[]}},"session_id":"2461f1b3-284a-46b0-accd-df8549bf2929","parent_tool_use_id":null,"uuid":"b655d78e-4c84-4ca3-82b4-f915ca3e1df9"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01W5H7HqdMm2o5s5NKJyDpkY","type":"tool_result","content":"The file /root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/route.ts has been updated successfully."}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"2461f1b3-284a-46b0-accd-df8549bf2929","uuid":"f63c78d3-a5b5-46c1-b226-9a490d1a0392","timestamp":"2026-03-24T12:04:23.993Z","tool_use_result":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/route.ts","oldString":"import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\nimport db from \"@/lib/db\";\n\nconst PAGE_SIZE = 20;","newString":"import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\nimport db from \"@/lib/db\";\n\nconst PAGE_SIZE = 20;\n\n/** Escape LIKE metacharacters so user input is matched literally */\nfunction escapeLike(value: string): string {\n  return value.replace(/[%_\\\\]/g, \"\\\\$&\");\n}","originalFile":"import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\nimport db from \"@/lib/db\";\n\nconst PAGE_SIZE = 20;\n\nexport async function GET(request: NextRequest) {\n  const { searchParams } = request.nextUrl;\n\n  const q = searchParams.get(\"q\")?.trim() || \"\";\n  const meetingFormat = searchParams.get(\"meeting_format\") || \"\";\n  const readingPace = searchParams.get(\"reading_pace\") || \"\";\n  const startDateFrom = searchParams.get(\"start_date_from\") || \"\";\n  const sort = searchParams.get(\"sort\") || \"newest\";\n  const includePast = searchParams.get(\"include_past\") === \"true\";\n  const pageParam = parseInt(searchParams.get(\"page\") || \"1\", 10);\n  const page = isNaN(pageParam) || pageParam < 1 ? 1 : Math.min(pageParam, 1000);\n\n  const conditions: string[] = [\"l.is_full = 0\"];\n  const params: (string | number)[] = [];\n\n  // Apply date floor: use the more restrictive of today or startDateFrom\n  const today = new Date().toISOString().split(\"T\")[0];\n  const hasStartDateFilter = startDateFrom && /^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2}$/.test(startDateFrom);\n\n  if (!includePast) {\n    // Use whichever is later: today or the explicit start_date_from filter\n    const effectiveDate = hasStartDateFilter && startDateFrom > today ? startDateFrom : today;\n    conditions.push(\"l.start_date >= ?\");\n    params.push(effectiveDate);\n  } else if (hasStartDateFilter) {\n    // includePast is true, but user wants a specific start date floor\n    conditions.push(\"l.start_date >= ?\");\n    params.push(startDateFrom);\n  }\n\n  if (q && q.length <= 300) {\n    conditions.push(\"(l.book_title LIKE ? OR l.book_author LIKE ?)\");\n    params.push(`%${q}%`, `%${q}%`);\n  }\n\n  if (meetingFormat && [\"voice\", \"text\", \"mixed\"].includes(meetingFormat)) {\n    conditions.push(\"l.meeting_format = ?\");\n    params.push(meetingFormat);\n  }\n\n  if (readingPace && readingPace.length <= 200) {\n    conditions.push(\"l.reading_pace LIKE ?\");\n    params.push(`%${readingPace}%`);\n  }\n\n  const whereClause = conditions.join(\" AND \");\n\n  let orderClause = \"l.created_at DESC\";\n  if (sort === \"oldest\") orderClause = \"l.created_at ASC\";\n  else if (sort === \"start_date\") orderClause = \"l.start_date ASC\";\n\n  const offset = (page - 1) * PAGE_SIZE;\n\n  // Get total count for pagination metadata\n  const countResult = db\n    .prepare(\n      `SELECT COUNT(*) as total FROM listings l WHERE ${whereClause}`\n    )\n    .get(...params) as { total: number };\n\n  const listings = db\n    .prepare(\n      `SELECT\n        l.*,\n        u.display_name as author_name,\n        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = l.id) as member_count\n      FROM listings l\n      JOIN users u ON l.author_id = u.id\n      WHERE ${whereClause}\n      ORDER BY ${orderClause}\n      LIMIT ? OFFSET ?`\n    )\n    .all(...params, PAGE_SIZE, offset);\n\n  return NextResponse.json({\n    listings,\n    pagination: {\n      page,\n      pageSize: PAGE_SIZE,\n      total: countResult.total,\n      totalPages: Math.ceil(countResult.total / PAGE_SIZE),\n    },\n  });\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":3,"oldLines":6,"newStart":3,"newLines":11,"lines":[" "," const PAGE_SIZE = 20;"," ","+/** Escape LIKE metacharacters so user input is matched literally */","+function escapeLike(value: string): string {","+  return value.replace(/[%_\\\\]/g, \"\\\\$&\");","+}","+"," export async function GET(request: NextRequest) {","   const { searchParams } = request.nextUrl;"," "]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01LftuY8CzZ6WfMD7VCVgrhf","type":"tool_result","content":"The file /root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/route.ts has been updated successfully."}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"2461f1b3-284a-46b0-accd-df8549bf2929","uuid":"ab2bea88-96a2-4c0b-8013-33a7b710938b","timestamp":"2026-03-24T12:04:29.346Z","tool_use_result":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/route.ts","oldString":"  if (q && q.length <= 300) {\n    conditions.push(\"(l.book_title LIKE ? OR l.book_author LIKE ?)\");\n    params.push(`%${q}%`, `%${q}%`);\n  }","newString":"  if (q && q.length <= 300) {\n    conditions.push(\"(l.book_title LIKE ? ESCAPE '\\\\' OR l.book_author LIKE ? ESCAPE '\\\\')\");\n    params.push(`%${escapeLike(q)}%`, `%${escapeLike(q)}%`);\n  }","originalFile":"import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\nimport db from \"@/lib/db\";\n\nconst PAGE_SIZE = 20;\n\n/** Escape LIKE metacharacters so user input is matched literally */\nfunction escapeLike(value: string): string {\n  return value.replace(/[%_\\\\]/g, \"\\\\$&\");\n}\n\nexport async function GET(request: NextRequest) {\n  const { searchParams } = request.nextUrl;\n\n  const q = searchParams.get(\"q\")?.trim() || \"\";\n  const meetingFormat = searchParams.get(\"meeting_format\") || \"\";\n  const readingPace = searchParams.get(\"reading_pace\") || \"\";\n  const startDateFrom = searchParams.get(\"start_date_from\") || \"\";\n  const sort = searchParams.get(\"sort\") || \"newest\";\n  const includePast = searchParams.get(\"include_past\") === \"true\";\n  const pageParam = parseInt(searchParams.get(\"page\") || \"1\", 10);\n  const page = isNaN(pageParam) || pageParam < 1 ? 1 : Math.min(pageParam, 1000);\n\n  const conditions: string[] = [\"l.is_full = 0\"];\n  const params: (string | number)[] = [];\n\n  // Apply date floor: use the more restrictive of today or startDateFrom\n  const today = new Date().toISOString().split(\"T\")[0];\n  const hasStartDateFilter = startDateFrom && /^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2}$/.test(startDateFrom);\n\n  if (!includePast) {\n    // Use whichever is later: today or the explicit start_date_from filter\n    const effectiveDate = hasStartDateFilter && startDateFrom > today ? startDateFrom : today;\n    conditions.push(\"l.start_date >= ?\");\n    params.push(effectiveDate);\n  } else if (hasStartDateFilter) {\n    // includePast is true, but user wants a specific start date floor\n    conditions.push(\"l.start_date >= ?\");\n    params.push(startDateFrom);\n  }\n\n  if (q && q.length <= 300) {\n    conditions.push(\"(l.book_title LIKE ? OR l.book_author LIKE ?)\");\n    params.push(`%${q}%`, `%${q}%`);\n  }\n\n  if (meetingFormat && [\"voice\", \"text\", \"mixed\"].includes(meetingFormat)) {\n    conditions.push(\"l.meeting_format = ?\");\n    params.push(meetingFormat);\n  }\n\n  if (readingPace && readingPace.length <= 200) {\n    conditions.push(\"l.reading_pace LIKE ?\");\n    params.push(`%${readingPace}%`);\n  }\n\n  const whereClause = conditions.join(\" AND \");\n\n  let orderClause = \"l.created_at DESC\";\n  if (sort === \"oldest\") orderClause = \"l.created_at ASC\";\n  else if (sort === \"start_date\") orderClause = \"l.start_date ASC\";\n\n  const offset = (page - 1) * PAGE_SIZE;\n\n  // Get total count for pagination metadata\n  const countResult = db\n    .prepare(\n      `SELECT COUNT(*) as total FROM listings l WHERE ${whereClause}`\n    )\n    .get(...params) as { total: number };\n\n  const listings = db\n    .prepare(\n      `SELECT\n        l.*,\n        u.display_name as author_name,\n        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = l.id) as member_count\n      FROM listings l\n      JOIN users u ON l.author_id = u.id\n      WHERE ${whereClause}\n      ORDER BY ${orderClause}\n      LIMIT ? OFFSET ?`\n    )\n    .all(...params, PAGE_SIZE, offset);\n\n  return NextResponse.json({\n    listings,\n    pagination: {\n      page,\n      pageSize: PAGE_SIZE,\n      total: countResult.total,\n      totalPages: Math.ceil(countResult.total / PAGE_SIZE),\n    },\n  });\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":39,"oldLines":8,"newStart":39,"newLines":8,"lines":["   }"," ","   if (q && q.length <= 300) {","-    conditions.push(\"(l.book_title LIKE ? OR l.book_author LIKE ?)\");","-    params.push(`%${q}%`, `%${q}%`);","+    conditions.push(\"(l.book_title LIKE ? ESCAPE '\\\\' OR l.book_author LIKE ? ESCAPE '\\\\')\");","+    params.push(`%${escapeLike(q)}%`, `%${escapeLike(q)}%`);","   }"," ","   if (meetingFormat && [\"voice\", \"text\", \"mixed\"].includes(meetingFormat)) {"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_013iR7MZN4dfBJeBmPXvFoVk","type":"tool_result","content":"The file /root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/route.ts has been updated successfully."}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"2461f1b3-284a-46b0-accd-df8549bf2929","uuid":"ac462ff6-57e3-4125-9dea-8ec7e8592aab","timestamp":"2026-03-24T12:04:33.655Z","tool_use_result":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/route.ts","oldString":"  if (readingPace && readingPace.length <= 200) {\n    conditions.push(\"l.reading_pace LIKE ?\");\n    params.push(`%${readingPace}%`);\n  }","newString":"  if (readingPace && readingPace.length <= 200) {\n    conditions.push(\"l.reading_pace LIKE ? ESCAPE '\\\\'\");\n    params.push(`%${escapeLike(readingPace)}%`);\n  }","originalFile":"import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\nimport db from \"@/lib/db\";\n\nconst PAGE_SIZE = 20;\n\n/** Escape LIKE metacharacters so user input is matched literally */\nfunction escapeLike(value: string): string {\n  return value.replace(/[%_\\\\]/g, \"\\\\$&\");\n}\n\nexport async function GET(request: NextRequest) {\n  const { searchParams } = request.nextUrl;\n\n  const q = searchParams.get(\"q\")?.trim() || \"\";\n  const meetingFormat = searchParams.get(\"meeting_format\") || \"\";\n  const readingPace = searchParams.get(\"reading_pace\") || \"\";\n  const startDateFrom = searchParams.get(\"start_date_from\") || \"\";\n  const sort = searchParams.get(\"sort\") || \"newest\";\n  const includePast = searchParams.get(\"include_past\") === \"true\";\n  const pageParam = parseInt(searchParams.get(\"page\") || \"1\", 10);\n  const page = isNaN(pageParam) || pageParam < 1 ? 1 : Math.min(pageParam, 1000);\n\n  const conditions: string[] = [\"l.is_full = 0\"];\n  const params: (string | number)[] = [];\n\n  // Apply date floor: use the more restrictive of today or startDateFrom\n  const today = new Date().toISOString().split(\"T\")[0];\n  const hasStartDateFilter = startDateFrom && /^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2}$/.test(startDateFrom);\n\n  if (!includePast) {\n    // Use whichever is later: today or the explicit start_date_from filter\n    const effectiveDate = hasStartDateFilter && startDateFrom > today ? startDateFrom : today;\n    conditions.push(\"l.start_date >= ?\");\n    params.push(effectiveDate);\n  } else if (hasStartDateFilter) {\n    // includePast is true, but user wants a specific start date floor\n    conditions.push(\"l.start_date >= ?\");\n    params.push(startDateFrom);\n  }\n\n  if (q && q.length <= 300) {\n    conditions.push(\"(l.book_title LIKE ? ESCAPE '\\\\' OR l.book_author LIKE ? ESCAPE '\\\\')\");\n    params.push(`%${escapeLike(q)}%`, `%${escapeLike(q)}%`);\n  }\n\n  if (meetingFormat && [\"voice\", \"text\", \"mixed\"].includes(meetingFormat)) {\n    conditions.push(\"l.meeting_format = ?\");\n    params.push(meetingFormat);\n  }\n\n  if (readingPace && readingPace.length <= 200) {\n    conditions.push(\"l.reading_pace LIKE ?\");\n    params.push(`%${readingPace}%`);\n  }\n\n  const whereClause = conditions.join(\" AND \");\n\n  let orderClause = \"l.created_at DESC\";\n  if (sort === \"oldest\") orderClause = \"l.created_at ASC\";\n  else if (sort === \"start_date\") orderClause = \"l.start_date ASC\";\n\n  const offset = (page - 1) * PAGE_SIZE;\n\n  // Get total count for pagination metadata\n  const countResult = db\n    .prepare(\n      `SELECT COUNT(*) as total FROM listings l WHERE ${whereClause}`\n    )\n    .get(...params) as { total: number };\n\n  const listings = db\n    .prepare(\n      `SELECT\n        l.*,\n        u.display_name as author_name,\n        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = l.id) as member_count\n      FROM listings l\n      JOIN users u ON l.author_id = u.id\n      WHERE ${whereClause}\n      ORDER BY ${orderClause}\n      LIMIT ? OFFSET ?`\n    )\n    .all(...params, PAGE_SIZE, offset);\n\n  return NextResponse.json({\n    listings,\n    pagination: {\n      page,\n      pageSize: PAGE_SIZE,\n      total: countResult.total,\n      totalPages: Math.ceil(countResult.total / PAGE_SIZE),\n    },\n  });\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":49,"oldLines":8,"newStart":49,"newLines":8,"lines":["   }"," ","   if (readingPace && readingPace.length <= 200) {","-    conditions.push(\"l.reading_pace LIKE ?\");","-    params.push(`%${readingPace}%`);","+    conditions.push(\"l.reading_pace LIKE ? ESCAPE '\\\\'\");","+    params.push(`%${escapeLike(readingPace)}%`);","   }"," ","   const whereClause = conditions.join(\" AND \");"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_018ASvEe234ghvPaxKBRaJeW","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01TzYe45xwdaioG6DPx6GC82","name":"Edit","input":{"replace_all":false,"file_path":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/route.ts","old_string":"  let orderClause = \"l.created_at DESC\";\n  if (sort === \"oldest\") orderClause = \"l.created_at ASC\";\n  else if (sort === \"start_date\") orderClause = \"l.start_date ASC\";","new_string":"  const ORDER_MAP: Record<string, string> = {\n    newest: \"l.created_at DESC\",\n    oldest: \"l.created_at ASC\",\n    start_date: \"l.start_date ASC\",\n  };\n  const orderClause = ORDER_MAP[sort] ?? \"l.created_at DESC\";"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":277,"cache_read_input_tokens":39744,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":277},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"2461f1b3-284a-46b0-accd-df8549bf2929","uuid":"f8e7e4f7-b31d-433c-b8c7-eb2ca5518abe"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01TzYe45xwdaioG6DPx6GC82","type":"tool_result","content":"The file /root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/route.ts has been updated successfully."}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"2461f1b3-284a-46b0-accd-df8549bf2929","uuid":"bab6ee81-5eed-440e-b813-aa126fc1397c","timestamp":"2026-03-24T12:04:39.292Z","tool_use_result":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/route.ts","oldString":"  let orderClause = \"l.created_at DESC\";\n  if (sort === \"oldest\") orderClause = \"l.created_at ASC\";\n  else if (sort === \"start_date\") orderClause = \"l.start_date ASC\";","newString":"  const ORDER_MAP: Record<string, string> = {\n    newest: \"l.created_at DESC\",\n    oldest: \"l.created_at ASC\",\n    start_date: \"l.start_date ASC\",\n  };\n  const orderClause = ORDER_MAP[sort] ?? \"l.created_at DESC\";","originalFile":"import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\nimport db from \"@/lib/db\";\n\nconst PAGE_SIZE = 20;\n\n/** Escape LIKE metacharacters so user input is matched literally */\nfunction escapeLike(value: string): string {\n  return value.replace(/[%_\\\\]/g, \"\\\\$&\");\n}\n\nexport async function GET(request: NextRequest) {\n  const { searchParams } = request.nextUrl;\n\n  const q = searchParams.get(\"q\")?.trim() || \"\";\n  const meetingFormat = searchParams.get(\"meeting_format\") || \"\";\n  const readingPace = searchParams.get(\"reading_pace\") || \"\";\n  const startDateFrom = searchParams.get(\"start_date_from\") || \"\";\n  const sort = searchParams.get(\"sort\") || \"newest\";\n  const includePast = searchParams.get(\"include_past\") === \"true\";\n  const pageParam = parseInt(searchParams.get(\"page\") || \"1\", 10);\n  const page = isNaN(pageParam) || pageParam < 1 ? 1 : Math.min(pageParam, 1000);\n\n  const conditions: string[] = [\"l.is_full = 0\"];\n  const params: (string | number)[] = [];\n\n  // Apply date floor: use the more restrictive of today or startDateFrom\n  const today = new Date().toISOString().split(\"T\")[0];\n  const hasStartDateFilter = startDateFrom && /^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2}$/.test(startDateFrom);\n\n  if (!includePast) {\n    // Use whichever is later: today or the explicit start_date_from filter\n    const effectiveDate = hasStartDateFilter && startDateFrom > today ? startDateFrom : today;\n    conditions.push(\"l.start_date >= ?\");\n    params.push(effectiveDate);\n  } else if (hasStartDateFilter) {\n    // includePast is true, but user wants a specific start date floor\n    conditions.push(\"l.start_date >= ?\");\n    params.push(startDateFrom);\n  }\n\n  if (q && q.length <= 300) {\n    conditions.push(\"(l.book_title LIKE ? ESCAPE '\\\\' OR l.book_author LIKE ? ESCAPE '\\\\')\");\n    params.push(`%${escapeLike(q)}%`, `%${escapeLike(q)}%`);\n  }\n\n  if (meetingFormat && [\"voice\", \"text\", \"mixed\"].includes(meetingFormat)) {\n    conditions.push(\"l.meeting_format = ?\");\n    params.push(meetingFormat);\n  }\n\n  if (readingPace && readingPace.length <= 200) {\n    conditions.push(\"l.reading_pace LIKE ? ESCAPE '\\\\'\");\n    params.push(`%${escapeLike(readingPace)}%`);\n  }\n\n  const whereClause = conditions.join(\" AND \");\n\n  let orderClause = \"l.created_at DESC\";\n  if (sort === \"oldest\") orderClause = \"l.created_at ASC\";\n  else if (sort === \"start_date\") orderClause = \"l.start_date ASC\";\n\n  const offset = (page - 1) * PAGE_SIZE;\n\n  // Get total count for pagination metadata\n  const countResult = db\n    .prepare(\n      `SELECT COUNT(*) as total FROM listings l WHERE ${whereClause}`\n    )\n    .get(...params) as { total: number };\n\n  const listings = db\n    .prepare(\n      `SELECT\n        l.*,\n        u.display_name as author_name,\n        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = l.id) as member_count\n      FROM listings l\n      JOIN users u ON l.author_id = u.id\n      WHERE ${whereClause}\n      ORDER BY ${orderClause}\n      LIMIT ? OFFSET ?`\n    )\n    .all(...params, PAGE_SIZE, offset);\n\n  return NextResponse.json({\n    listings,\n    pagination: {\n      page,\n      pageSize: PAGE_SIZE,\n      total: countResult.total,\n      totalPages: Math.ceil(countResult.total / PAGE_SIZE),\n    },\n  });\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":55,"oldLines":9,"newStart":55,"newLines":12,"lines":[" ","   const whereClause = conditions.join(\" AND \");"," ","-  let orderClause = \"l.created_at DESC\";","-  if (sort === \"oldest\") orderClause = \"l.created_at ASC\";","-  else if (sort === \"start_date\") orderClause = \"l.start_date ASC\";","+  const ORDER_MAP: Record<string, string> = {","+    newest: \"l.created_at DESC\",","+    oldest: \"l.created_at ASC\",","+    start_date: \"l.start_date ASC\",","+  };","+  const orderClause = ORDER_MAP[sort] ?? \"l.created_at DESC\";"," ","   const offset = (page - 1) * PAGE_SIZE;"," "]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

AND is_read = 0 LIMIT 1000\"\n    ).run(session.userId);\n  }","originalFile":"import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\nimport db from \"@/lib/db\";\nimport { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n\nexport async function GET() {\n  const session = await requireAuth();\n  if (!session) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n  }\n\n  const notifications = db\n    .prepare(\n      `SELECT n.*, l.book_title\n       FROM notifications n\n       LEFT JOIN listings l ON n.listing_id = l.id\n       WHERE n.user_id = ?\n       ORDER BY n.created_at DESC\n       LIMIT 50`\n    )\n    .all(session.userId);\n\n  const unreadCount = db\n    .prepare(\n      \"SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM notifications WHERE user_id = ? AND is_read = 0\"\n    )\n    .get(session.userId) as { count: number };\n\n  return NextResponse.json({\n    notifications,\n    unreadCount: unreadCount.count,\n  });\n}\n\nexport async function PATCH(req: NextRequest) {\n  const session = await requireAuth();\n  if (!session) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n  }\n\n  const { notificationId } = await req.json();\n\n  if (notificationId) {\n    // Validate notificationId is a positive integer\n    if (typeof notificationId !== \"number\" || !Number.isInteger(notificationId) || notificationId < 1) {\n      return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid notification ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n    }\n    db.prepare(\n      \"UPDATE notifications SET is_read = 1 WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?\"\n    ).run(notificationId, session.userId);\n  } else {\n    // Mark all as read\n    db.prepare(\n      \"UPDATE notifications SET is_read = 1 WHERE user_id = ?\"\n    ).run(session.userId);\n  }\n\n  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":48,"oldLines":9,"newStart":48,"newLines":9,"lines":["       \"UPDATE notifications SET is_read = 1 WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?\"","     ).run(notificationId, session.userId);","   } else {","-    // Mark all as read","+    // Mark all as read — use LIMIT to bound WAL lock duration on large notification sets","     db.prepare(","-      \"UPDATE notifications SET is_read = 1 WHERE user_id = ?\"","+      \"UPDATE notifications SET is_read = 1 WHERE user_id = ? AND is_read = 0 LIMIT 1000\"","     ).run(session.userId);","   }"," "]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01H4WpbFUWb7G6RpEUMLLS1m","type":"tool_result","content":"The file /root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/notifications/route.ts has been updated successfully."}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"2461f1b3-284a-46b0-accd-df8549bf2929","uuid":"b3d33e42-2cd1-4484-9009-a3baf2b1ed49","timestamp":"2026-03-24T12:04:51.816Z","tool_use_result":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/notifications/route.ts","oldString":"  } else {\n    // Mark all as read — use LIMIT to bound WAL lock duration on large notification sets\n    db.prepare(\n      \"UPDATE notifications SET is_read = 1 WHERE user_id = ? AND is_read = 0 LIMIT 1000\"\n    ).run(session.userId);\n  }","newString":"  } else {\n    // Mark all as read — use subquery with LIMIT to bound WAL lock duration\n    db.prepare(\n      `UPDATE notifications SET is_read = 1\n       WHERE id IN (\n         SELECT id FROM notifications\n         WHERE user_id = ? AND is_read = 0\n         LIMIT 1000\n       )`\n    ).run(session.userId);\n  }","originalFile":"import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\nimport db from \"@/lib/db\";\nimport { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n\nexport async function GET() {\n  const session = await requireAuth();\n  if (!session) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n  }\n\n  const notifications = db\n    .prepare(\n      `SELECT n.*, l.book_title\n       FROM notifications n\n       LEFT JOIN listings l ON n.listing_id = l.id\n       WHERE n.user_id = ?\n       ORDER BY n.created_at DESC\n       LIMIT 50`\n    )\n    .all(session.userId);\n\n  const unreadCount = db\n    .prepare(\n      \"SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM notifications WHERE user_id = ? AND is_read = 0\"\n    )\n    .get(session.userId) as { count: number };\n\n  return NextResponse.json({\n    notifications,\n    unreadCount: unreadCount.count,\n  });\n}\n\nexport async function PATCH(req: NextRequest) {\n  const session = await requireAuth();\n  if (!session) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n  }\n\n  const { notificationId } = await req.json();\n\n  if (notificationId) {\n    // Validate notificationId is a positive integer\n    if (typeof notificationId !== \"number\" || !Number.isInteger(notificationId) || notificationId < 1) {\n      return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid notification ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n    }\n    db.prepare(\n      \"UPDATE notifications SET is_read = 1 WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?\"\n    ).run(notificationId, session.userId);\n  } else {\n    // Mark all as read — use LIMIT to bound WAL lock duration on large notification sets\n    db.prepare(\n      \"UPDATE notifications SET is_read = 1 WHERE user_id = ? AND is_read = 0 LIMIT 1000\"\n    ).run(session.userId);\n  }\n\n  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":48,"oldLines":9,"newStart":48,"newLines":14,"lines":["       \"UPDATE notifications SET is_read = 1 WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?\"","     ).run(notificationId, session.userId);","   } else {","-    // Mark all as read — use LIMIT to bound WAL lock duration on large notification sets","+    // Mark all as read — use subquery with LIMIT to bound WAL lock duration","     db.prepare(","-      \"UPDATE notifications SET is_read = 1 WHERE user_id = ? AND is_read = 0 LIMIT 1000\"","+      `UPDATE notifications SET is_read = 1","+       WHERE id IN (","+         SELECT id FROM notifications","+         WHERE user_id = ? AND is_read = 0","+         LIMIT 1000","+       )`","     ).run(session.userId);","   }"," "]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_0185nKFSrAEUXTwxU1hRaFzB","type":"tool_result","content":"The file /root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/profile/reading/route.ts has been updated successfully."}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"2461f1b3-284a-46b0-accd-df8549bf2929","uuid":"35cc7c34-6ce1-48e3-b817-c5565c765f49","timestamp":"2026-03-24T12:04:57.123Z","tool_use_result":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/profile/reading/route.ts","oldString":"    WHERE lm.user_id = ?\n    ORDER BY lm.joined_at DESC\n  `).all(session.userId) as ReadingRow[];","newString":"    WHERE lm.user_id = ?\n    ORDER BY lm.joined_at DESC\n    LIMIT 200\n  `).all(session.userId) as ReadingRow[];","originalFile":"import { NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\nimport db from \"@/lib/db\";\nimport { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n\ninterface ReadingRow {\n  id: number;\n  book_title: string;\n  book_author: string;\n  book_cover_url: string;\n  reading_pace: string;\n  start_date: string;\n  meeting_format: string;\n  is_full: number;\n  telegram_link: string;\n  discord_link: string;\n  member_count: number;\n  max_group_size: number;\n  author_name: string;\n  joined_at: string;\n}\n\nexport async function GET() {\n  const session = await requireAuth();\n  if (!session) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n  }\n\n  // Get all listings the user is a member of (including ones they authored)\n  const readings = db.prepare(`\n    SELECT\n      l.id,\n      l.book_title,\n      l.book_author,\n      l.book_cover_url,\n      l.reading_pace,\n      l.start_date,\n      l.meeting_format,\n      l.is_full,\n      l.telegram_link,\n      l.discord_link,\n      l.max_group_size,\n      u.display_name as author_name,\n      lm.joined_at,\n      (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = l.id) as member_count\n    FROM listing_members lm\n    JOIN listings l ON l.id = lm.listing_id\n    JOIN users u ON u.id = l.author_id\n    WHERE lm.user_id = ?\n    ORDER BY lm.joined_at DESC\n  `).all(session.userId) as ReadingRow[];\n\n  const today = new Date().toISOString().split(\"T\")[0];\n\n  // Reading history: completed groups (full + start_date in the past)\n  // Upcoming: not yet started and not full\n  // Active: everything else that has started or is full (but not completed)\n  const isHistory = (r: ReadingRow) => r.is_full === 1 && r.start_date < today;\n  const isUpcoming = (r: ReadingRow) => r.is_full === 0 && r.start_date > today;\n\n  const history = readings.filter(isHistory);\n  const upcoming = readings.filter(isUpcoming);\n  const active = readings.filter(\n    (r) => !isHistory(r) && !isUpcoming(r) && (r.is_full === 1 || r.start_date <= today)\n  );\n\n  return NextResponse.json({\n    active,\n    history,\n    upcoming,\n    total: readings.length,\n  });\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":47,"oldLines":6,"newStart":47,"newLines":7,"lines":["     JOIN users u ON u.id = l.author_id","     WHERE lm.user_id = ?","     ORDER BY lm.joined_at DESC","+    LIMIT 200","   `).all(session.userId) as ReadingRow[];"," ","   const today = new Date().toISOString().split(\"T\")[0];"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01JgwHeHoJHRrX2EHAyGnqy8","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01A9UYrqMJtUBgBXZLWB9Jt9","name":"Edit","input":{"replace_all":false,"file_path":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/email.ts","old_string":"let transporter: nodemailer.Transporter | null = null;\n\nfunction getTransporter(): nodemailer.Transporter | null {\n  if (!checkConfigured()) return null;\n  if (!transporter) {\n    transporter = nodemailer.createTransport({\n      host: process.env.SMTP_HOST,\n      port: parseInt(process.env.SMTP_PORT || \"587\", 10),\n      secure: process.env.SMTP_SECURE === \"true\",\n      ...(process.env.SMTP_USER && process.env.SMTP_PASS\n        ? {\n            auth: {\n              user: process.env.SMTP_USER,\n              pass: process.env.SMTP_PASS,\n            },\n          }\n        : {}),\n    });\n  }\n  return transporter;\n}","new_string":"let transporter: nodemailer.Transporter | null = null;\nlet transporterConfigHash = \"\";\n\n/** Hash current SMTP env vars to detect config changes */\nfunction smtpConfigHash(): string {\n  return [\n    process.env.SMTP_HOST,\n    process.env.SMTP_PORT,\n    process.env.SMTP_SECURE,\n    process.env.SMTP_USER,\n    process.env.SMTP_PASS,\n    process.env.SMTP_FROM,\n  ].join(\"|\");\n}\n\nfunction getTransporter(): nodemailer.Transporter | null {\n  if (!checkConfigured()) return null;\n  const currentHash = smtpConfigHash();\n  if (!transporter || currentHash !== transporterConfigHash) {\n    transporter = nodemailer.createTransport({\n      host: process.env.SMTP_HOST,\n      port: parseInt(process.env.SMTP_PORT || \"587\", 10),\n      secure: process.env.SMTP_SECURE === \"true\",\n      ...(process.env.SMTP_USER && process.env.SMTP_PASS\n        ? {\n            auth: {\n              user: process.env.SMTP_USER,\n              pass: process.env.SMTP_PASS,\n            },\n          }\n        : {}),\n    });\n    transporterConfigHash = currentHash;\n  }\n  return transporter;\n}"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":259,"cache_read_input_tokens":41097,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":259},"output_tokens":8,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"2461f1b3-284a-46b0-accd-df8549bf2929","uuid":"cf2e5733-fb90-4732-9fb4-2dded7b66837"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01A9UYrqMJtUBgBXZLWB9Jt9","type":"tool_result","content":"The file /root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/email.ts has been updated successfully."}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"2461f1b3-284a-46b0-accd-df8549bf2929","uuid":"51b28204-4f2c-4832-9fa6-496404bf8e98","timestamp":"2026-03-24T12:05:05.946Z","tool_use_result":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/email.ts","oldString":"let transporter: nodemailer.Transporter | null = null;\n\nfunction getTransporter(): nodemailer.Transporter | null {\n  if (!checkConfigured()) return null;\n  if (!transporter) {\n    transporter = nodemailer.createTransport({\n      host: process.env.SMTP_HOST,\n      port: parseInt(process.env.SMTP_PORT || \"587\", 10),\n      secure: process.env.SMTP_SECURE === \"true\",\n      ...(process.env.SMTP_USER && process.env.SMTP_PASS\n        ? {\n            auth: {\n              user: process.env.SMTP_USER,\n              pass: process.env.SMTP_PASS,\n            },\n          }\n        : {}),\n    });\n  }\n  return transporter;\n}","newString":"let transporter: nodemailer.Transporter | null = null;\nlet transporterConfigHash = \"\";\n\n/** Hash current SMTP env vars to detect config changes */\nfunction smtpConfigHash(): string {\n  return [\n    process.env.SMTP_HOST,\n    process.env.SMTP_PORT,\n    process.env.SMTP_SECURE,\n    process.env.SMTP_USER,\n    process.env.SMTP_PASS,\n    process.env.SMTP_FROM,\n  ].join(\"|\");\n}\n\nfunction getTransporter(): nodemailer.Transporter | null {\n  if (!checkConfigured()) return null;\n  const currentHash = smtpConfigHash();\n  if (!transporter || currentHash !== transporterConfigHash) {\n    transporter = nodemailer.createTransport({\n      host: process.env.SMTP_HOST,\n      port: parseInt(process.env.SMTP_PORT || \"587\", 10),\n      secure: process.env.SMTP_SECURE === \"true\",\n      ...(process.env.SMTP_USER && process.env.SMTP_PASS\n        ? {\n            auth: {\n              user: process.env.SMTP_USER,\n              pass: process.env.SMTP_PASS,\n            },\n          }\n        : {}),\n    });\n    transporterConfigHash = currentHash;\n  }\n  return transporter;\n}","originalFile":"import nodemailer from \"nodemailer\";\n\n/**\n * Check SMTP config live from process.env on each call — not at module load time.\n * Module-level constants would be frozen when the file is first imported,\n * permanently returning false if SMTP vars aren't set at that exact moment.\n */\nfunction checkConfigured(): boolean {\n  return (\n    !!process.env.SMTP_HOST &&\n    !!process.env.SMTP_PORT &&\n    !!process.env.SMTP_FROM\n  );\n}\n\nlet transporter: nodemailer.Transporter | null = null;\n\nfunction getTransporter(): nodemailer.Transporter | null {\n  if (!checkConfigured()) return null;\n  if (!transporter) {\n    transporter = nodemailer.createTransport({\n      host: process.env.SMTP_HOST,\n      port: parseInt(process.env.SMTP_PORT || \"587\", 10),\n      secure: process.env.SMTP_SECURE === \"true\",\n      ...(process.env.SMTP_USER && process.env.SMTP_PASS\n        ? {\n            auth: {\n              user: process.env.SMTP_USER,\n              pass: process.env.SMTP_PASS,\n            },\n          }\n        : {}),\n    });\n  }\n  return transporter;\n}\n\nexport async function sendEmail(\n  to: string,\n  subject: string,\n  text: string,\n  html?: string\n): Promise<boolean> {\n  const t = getTransporter();\n  if (!t) return false;\n\n  try {\n    await t.sendMail({\n      from: process.env.SMTP_FROM,\n      to,\n      subject,\n      text,\n      html: html || text,\n    });\n    return true;\n  } catch (err) {\n    console.error(\"Failed to send email:\", err);\n    return false;\n  }\n}\n\nexport function isEmailConfigured(): boolean {\n  return checkConfigured();\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":14,"oldLines":10,"newStart":14,"newLines":24,"lines":[" }"," "," let transporter: nodemailer.Transporter | null = null;","+let transporterConfigHash = \"\";"," ","+/** Hash current SMTP env vars to detect config changes */","+function smtpConfigHash(): string {","+  return [","+    process.env.SMTP_HOST,","+    process.env.SMTP_PORT,","+    process.env.SMTP_SECURE,","+    process.env.SMTP_USER,","+    process.env.SMTP_PASS,","+    process.env.SMTP_FROM,","+  ].join(\"|\");","+}","+"," function getTransporter(): nodemailer.Transporter | null {","   if (!checkConfigured()) return null;","-  if (!transporter) {","+  const currentHash = smtpConfigHash();","+  if (!transporter || currentHash !== transporterConfigHash) {","     transporter = nodemailer.createTransport({","       host: process.env.SMTP_HOST,","       port: parseInt(process.env.SMTP_PORT || \"587\", 10),"]},{"oldStart":31,"oldLines":6,"newStart":45,"newLines":7,"lines":["           }","         : {}),","     });","+    transporterConfigHash = currentHash;","   }","   return transporter;"," }"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01JcfiJgPd4j6BBB5YKFMFAh","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01587Ti9T9j9CgJz5eaVkAYk","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"git commit -m \"$(cat <<'EOF'\nfix: LIKE injection, auth bypass, unbounded queries, transporter cache (#104)\n\n1. auto-discord uses requireAuth() instead of getSession() — enforces DB user-existence check\n2. Escape LIKE metacharacters (%, _, \\) in search inputs with ESCAPE clause\n3. Bound mark-all-notifications-read to 1000 rows per call via subquery LIMIT\n4. Add LIMIT 200 to reading history query to prevent unbounded result sets\n5. Harden ORDER BY with explicit allowlist map instead of string interpolation\n6. Invalidate nodemailer transporter cache when SMTP env vars change\n\nCo-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>\nEOF\n)\"","description":"Commit fixes"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":177,"cache_read_input_tokens":43202,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":177},"output_tokens":51,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"2461f1b3-284a-46b0-accd-df8549bf2929","uuid":"4dd3e035-c6a8-44ac-8660-7bc7e7383fad"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01C26pEV6UMhPq2wNkL3aXHP","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_0156uzGLKchJHz27zRpE3UMG","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"export GH_TOKEN=$(grep GH_TOKEN /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/.env | cut -d= -f2) && gh pr create --title \"fix: code review round 15 — LIKE injection, auth bypass, unbounded queries\" --body \"$(cat <<'EOF'\n## Summary\n- **auto-discord auth bypass**: replaced `getSession()` with `requireAuth()` to enforce DB user-existence check, matching all other authenticated endpoints\n- **LIKE wildcard injection**: escaped `%`, `_`, `\\` metacharacters in search inputs so literal searches work correctly and `%` can't match all rows\n- **Unbounded mark-all-read**: bounded UPDATE to 1000 rows per call via subquery LIMIT to prevent WAL lock on large notification sets\n- **Unbounded reading history**: added LIMIT 200 to prevent memory/bandwidth issues with power users\n- **ORDER BY hardening**: replaced if/else chain with explicit allowlist map for sort parameter\n- **Transporter cache invalidation**: nodemailer transporter now rebuilds when SMTP env vars change\n\n## Test plan\n- [x] All 201 tests pass\n- [x] ESLint clean\n- [x] TypeScript clean\n- [x] Build clean\n\nCloses #104\n\n🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)\nEOF\n)\"","description":"Create PR"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":272,"cache_read_input_tokens":43702,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":272},"output_tokens":64,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"2461f1b3-284a-46b0-accd-df8549bf2929","uuid":"8fb54399-f4ea-43df-b623-db6c35290b1a"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01KPEmWaCn6Fo1iCFTM8bzB7","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01Pym4a994seppxhNLB8wdyc","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/logs/session-2026-03-24-1200.md","content":"# Session Log — 2026-03-24 12:00 UTC\n\n## Summary\nCode review round 15: found and fixed 6 bugs across search injection, auth bypass, unbounded queries, and transporter caching. Created issue #104, implemented fixes in PR #105, CI passed, merged to main, deployed to production.\n\n## Review Findings\n- **Production**: Healthy — HTTP 200, PM2 online, 201 tests pass\n- **CI**: Green on main\n- **Open PRs**: None\n- **Open issues**: #54 (needs-human, PAT expiry ~3 days), #21 (needs-human, Telegram token)\n- **PRD sync**: All PRD features have corresponding closed issues\n\n## Work Completed\n\n### Issue #104: Code review round 15 (PR #105 — merged + deployed)\n\n6 fixes:\n\n1. **`auto-discord` uses `getSession()` instead of `requireAuth()`** (IMPORTANT) — Bypassed DB user-existence check that all other authenticated endpoints perform. Deleted users with valid cookies could still access this endpoint. Fixed by replacing with `requireAuth()`.\n\n2. **LIKE wildcard metacharacters not escaped in search inputs** (IMPORTANT) — `%` and `_` in search terms acted as SQL LIKE wildcards. Searching for `%` matched every listing. Fixed with `escapeLike()` function and `ESCAPE '\\'` clause on all LIKE conditions.\n\n3. **Mark-all-notifications-read UPDATE unbounded** (IMPORTANT) — Bulk UPDATE with no LIMIT could lock SQLite WAL on large notification sets. Fixed with subquery LIMIT 1000 to bound write duration.\n\n4. **Profile reading history query unbounded** (IMPORTANT) — No LIMIT on readings query. Added LIMIT 200 to prevent memory/bandwidth issues.\n\n5. **ORDER BY via string interpolation** (HARDENING) — Replaced if/else chain with explicit `ORDER_MAP` allowlist for sort parameter. Safe today but fragile pattern.\n\n6. **Nodemailer transporter cached permanently** (IMPORTANT) — Transporter created once and never rebuilt even if SMTP env vars changed. Added config hash comparison to invalidate and rebuild when env vars change.\n\nAll 201 tests pass, ESLint clean, build clean, CI green.\n\n## Also Noted (not fixed this round)\n- No `ON DELETE CASCADE` on FK constraints (requires full table recreation, manual deletion already handles correctly in listing DELETE handler)\n- `notifications.listing_id` is nullable — CASCADE would delete notifications instead of preserving them with NULL\n- No user deletion feature exists, so CASCADE on user FKs has no practical impact\n\n## Issues Monitored\n- **#54 (PAT expiry)**: Critical — needs-human, ~3 days remaining\n- **#21 (Telegram token)**: Still needs-human\n\n## Next Session Priority\n1. Check production health\n2. Monitor PAT expiry (#54) — critical\n3. Consider E2E testing, performance optimization, or new feature proposals\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":171,"cache_read_input_tokens":46200,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":171},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"2461f1b3-284a-46b0-accd-df8549bf2929","uuid":"1a78aebb-266e-4d8e-a045-13a5533e105a"}

SQLite, iron-session\n     8→- **Owner**: XanderZhu (human), TommyBrennan (GH account)\n     9→\n    10→## Current State\n    11→- All PRD features implemented and merged (P0, P1, P2)\n    12→- CI pipeline live: GitHub Actions runs lint + type check + build on push/PR to main\n    13→- ESLint flat config with typescript-eslint, react-hooks, @next/eslint-plugin-next\n    14→- Build passes clean, lint clean (no warnings)\n    15→- Security hardening merged (PR #32, #36)\n    16→- **Production deployed** via PM2 on port 3000 — http://89.167.127.85:3000\n    17→- Health endpoint: `/api/health` (DB check, uptime, stats)\n    18→- Custom 404 page (branded, with CTA)\n    19→- OG + Twitter Card meta tags on listing pages (book cover, title, description)\n    20→- Loading skeletons on all routes (root, listing detail, profile, notifications)\n    21→- Error boundaries on all routes with retry functionality\n    22→- Focus-visible styles for keyboard accessibility\n    23→- **Accessibility improvements** (PR #49, merged + deployed):\n    24→  - ARIA labels, roles, expanded states on all interactive elements\n    25→  - Keyboard navigation on BookSearch dropdown (combobox pattern)\n    26→  - autocomplete attributes on auth forms\n    27→  - role=\"alert\" on error messages\n    28→  - Skip-to-content link\n    29→  - role=\"tablist\"/tab/tabpanel on profile tabs\n    30→  - role=\"radiogroup\"/radio on format/platform buttons\n    31→  - role=\"checkbox\" with keyboard support on approval toggle\n    32→\n    33→## Production\n    34→- **URL**: http://89.167.127.85:3000\n    35→- **Process manager**: PM2 (via npx)\n    36→- **Config**: `app/ecosystem.config.js`\n    37→- **Deploy script**: `scripts/deploy.sh`\n    38→- **Logs**: `.claude-beat/logs/pm2-{out,error}.log`\n    39→- **No reverse proxy** (nginx requires root — not available)\n    40→- **No HTTPS** (no domain configured, no certbot)\n    41→- PM2 process saved: `npx pm2 save` (restores on `pm2 resurrect`)\n    42→\n    43→## Open PRs\n    44→- None\n    45→\n    46→## Closed Recently\n    47→- #96: Code review round 11 — stale transaction data in approve, unmount cleanup, duplicate notifications — merged + deployed\n    48→- #95: Code review round 10 — FK violations, stale notifications, input validation, error UX — merged + deployed\n    49→- #93: Code review round 9 — client-side race conditions, date bugs, error handling, ARIA — merged + deployed\n    50→- #91: Code review round 8 — timing-safe secrets, type validation, stale transaction data, email HTML escaping — merged + deployed\n    51→- #87: Code review round 6 — logout fix, is_full reset, email subjects, webhook validation, fetch abort — merged + deployed\n    52→\n    53→## Open Issues\n    54→- #21: Telegram bot token (needs-human) — multiple follow-up reminders sent, still waiting\n    55→- #54: **PAT expires ~March 27** (needs-human) — GH_TOKEN renewal required, ~3 days left\n    56→\n    57→## Important Notes\n    58→- GH_TOKEN loaded from `.claude-beat/.env`\n    59→- Remote URL includes PAT: reset with `git remote set-url` if needed\n    60→- Next.js 15 used (not 16) due to Turbopack /root permission issue\n    61→- agent-browser WORKS if: export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp/runtime-$(id -u) && mkdir -p \"$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR\"\n    62→- agent-browser crashes after extended use (memory pressure) — keep recording sessions short, restart between scenes\n    63→- ffmpeg installed as static binary in ~/bin — add to PATH: export PATH=\"$HOME/bin:$PATH\"\n    64→- ELEVENLABS_API_KEY not available — demo video has no voiceover\n    65→- Demo video at .claude-beat/logs/demos/demo_2026-03-20.mp4 (5 scenes, ~1:51)\n    66→- SQLite DB stored in `app/data/` (gitignored)\n    67→- DB tables: users, listings, listing_members, listing_applications, user_genres, notifications, ratings, telegram_chats, pending_telegram_groups\n    68→- Telegram bot integration requires TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN env var (see issue #21)\n    69→- Discord bot integration requires DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN + DISCORD_CLIENT_ID + DISCORD_WEBHOOK_SECRET env vars\n    70→- Listings have `platform_preference` field (telegram/discord)\n    71→- Email notifications require SMTP env vars (SMTP_HOST, SMTP_PORT, SMTP_FROM, etc.)\n    72→- nodemailer added as dependency for email sending\n    73→- Session secret: lazy runtime check (not module-level) to avoid build failure\n    74→- ESLint: use eslint v9 + @eslint/js v9 (not v10) to avoid peer dep conflicts\n    75→- npm ci works cleanly (no --legacy-peer-deps needed)\n    76→- No root/sudo access on server — can't install nginx, Docker, or system packages\n    77→- PM2 available via npx (not globally installed)\n    78→\n    79→## Test Suite\n    80→- 198 tests across 22 test files, all passing\n    81→- Tested: auth (login, register), health, listings (browse, detail, create, join, edit, delete, telegram, discord, auto-telegram, auto-discord), notifications, profile, profile/genres, profile/reading, profile/reputation, ratings, rate-limit, telegram/setup, telegram/webhook, discord/setup, discord/webhook\n    82→- All API routes now have test coverage\n    83→- @vitest/coverage-v8 installed for coverage reporting\n    84→\n    85→## Components\n    86→- `BookCover` — reusable book cover component with error fallback, uses `unoptimized` to bypass Next.js image proxy for Open Library covers that redirect through archive.org\n    87→\n    88→## Next Session Priority\n    89→1. **URGENT**: PAT expires ~March 27 (#54) — ~3 days remaining, still needs-human\n    90→2. expires ~March 27** (needs-human) — GH_TOKEN renewal required, ~3 days left\n\n## Important Notes\n- GH_TOKEN loaded from `.claude-beat/.env`\n- Remote URL includes PAT: reset with `git remote set-url` if needed\n- Next.js 15 used (not 16) due to Turbopack /root permission issue\n- agent-browser WORKS if: export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp/runtime-$(id -u) && mkdir -p \"$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR\"\n- agent-browser crashes after extended use (memory pressure) — keep recording sessions short, restart between scenes\n- ffmpeg installed as static binary in ~/bin — add to PATH: export PATH=\"$HOME/bin:$PATH\"\n- ELEVENLABS_API_KEY not available — demo video has no voiceover\n- Demo video at .claude-beat/logs/demos/demo_2026-03-20.mp4 (5 scenes, ~1:51)\n- SQLite DB stored in `app/data/` (gitignored)\n- DB tables: users, listings, listing_members, listing_applications, user_genres, notifications, ratings, telegram_chats, pending_telegram_groups\n- Telegram bot integration requires TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN env var (see issue #21)\n- Discord bot integration requires DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN + DISCORD_CLIENT_ID + DISCORD_WEBHOOK_SECRET env vars\n- Listings have `platform_preference` field (telegram/discord)\n- Email notifications require SMTP env vars (SMTP_HOST, SMTP_PORT, SMTP_FROM, etc.)\n- nodemailer added as dependency for email sending\n- Session secret: lazy runtime check (not module-level) to avoid build failure\n- ESLint: use eslint v9 + @eslint/js v9 (not v10) to avoid peer dep conflicts\n- npm ci works cleanly (no --legacy-peer-deps needed)\n- No root/sudo access on server — can't install nginx, Docker, or system packages\n- PM2 available via npx (not globally installed)\n\n## Test Suite\n- 198 tests across 22 test files, all passing\n- Tested: auth (login, register), health, listings (browse, detail, create, join, edit, delete, telegram, discord, auto-telegram, auto-discord), notifications, profile, profile/genres, profile/reading, profile/reputation, ratings, rate-limit, telegram/setup, telegram/webhook, discord/setup, discord/webhook\n- All API routes now have test coverage\n- @vitest/coverage-v8 installed for coverage reporting\n\n## Components\n- `BookCover` — reusable book cover component with error fallback, uses `unoptimized` to bypass Next.js image proxy for Open Library covers that redirect through archive.org\n\n## Next Session Priority\n1. **URGENT**: PAT expires ~March 27 (#54) — ~3 days remaining, still needs-human\n2. Check issue #21 for Telegram token response\n3. Check production health\n4. All routes tested, 11 rounds of code review done — consider E2E testing, performance optimization, or new feature proposals\n","numLines":93,"startLine":1,"totalLines":93}}}

merged + deployed\n- #96: Code review round 11 — stale transaction data in approve, unmount cleanup, duplicate notifications — merged + deployed\n- #95: Code review round 10 — FK violations, stale notifications, input validation, error UX — merged + deployed","originalFile":"# Agent Memory\n\n## Project\n- **Name**: Bookmate\n- **Repo**: https://github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate\n- **Project Board**: Bookmate (project #1 on TommyBrennan)\n- **Tech**: Next.js 15, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS v4, SQLite, iron-session\n- **Owner**: XanderZhu (human), TommyBrennan (GH account)\n\n## Current State\n- All PRD features implemented and merged (P0, P1, P2)\n- CI pipeline live: GitHub Actions runs lint + type check + build on push/PR to main\n- ESLint flat config with typescript-eslint, react-hooks, @next/eslint-plugin-next\n- Build passes clean, lint clean (no warnings)\n- Security hardening merged (PR #32, #36)\n- **Production deployed** via PM2 on port 3000 — http://89.167.127.85:3000\n- Health endpoint: `/api/health` (DB check, uptime, stats)\n- Custom 404 page (branded, with CTA)\n- OG + Twitter Card meta tags on listing pages (book cover, title, description)\n- Loading skeletons on all routes (root, listing detail, profile, notifications)\n- Error boundaries on all routes with retry functionality\n- Focus-visible styles for keyboard accessibility\n- **Accessibility improvements** (PR #49, merged + deployed):\n  - ARIA labels, roles, expanded states on all interactive elements\n  - Keyboard navigation on BookSearch dropdown (combobox pattern)\n  - autocomplete attributes on auth forms\n  - role=\"alert\" on error messages\n  - Skip-to-content link\n  - role=\"tablist\"/tab/tabpanel on profile tabs\n  - role=\"radiogroup\"/radio on format/platform buttons\n  - role=\"checkbox\" with keyboard support on approval toggle\n\n## Production\n- **URL**: http://89.167.127.85:3000\n- **Process manager**: PM2 (via npx)\n- **Config**: `app/ecosystem.config.js`\n- **Deploy script**: `scripts/deploy.sh`\n- **Logs**: `.claude-beat/logs/pm2-{out,error}.log`\n- **No reverse proxy** (nginx requires root — not available)\n- **No HTTPS** (no domain configured, no certbot)\n- PM2 process saved: `npx pm2 save` (restores on `pm2 resurrect`)\n\n## Open PRs\n- None\n\n## Closed Recently\n- #96: Code review round 11 — stale transaction data in approve, unmount cleanup, duplicate notifications — merged + deployed\n- #95: Code review round 10 — FK violations, stale notifications, input validation, error UX — merged + deployed\n- #93: Code review round 9 — client-side race conditions, date bugs, error handling, ARIA — merged + deployed\n- #91: Code review round 8 — timing-safe secrets, type validation, stale transaction data, email HTML escaping — merged + deployed\n- #87: Code review round 6 — logout fix, is_full reset, email subjects, webhook validation, fetch abort — merged + deployed\n\n## Open Issues\n- #21: Telegram bot token (needs-human) — multiple follow-up reminders sent, still waiting\n- #54: **PAT expires ~March 27** (needs-human) — GH_TOKEN renewal required, ~3 days left\n\n## Important Notes\n- GH_TOKEN loaded from `.claude-beat/.env`\n- Remote URL includes PAT: reset with `git remote set-url` if needed\n- Next.js 15 used (not 16) due to Turbopack /root permission issue\n- agent-browser WORKS if: export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp/runtime-$(id -u) && mkdir -p \"$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR\"\n- agent-browser crashes after extended use (memory pressure) — keep recording sessions short, restart between scenes\n- ffmpeg installed as static binary in ~/bin — add to PATH: export PATH=\"$HOME/bin:$PATH\"\n- ELEVENLABS_API_KEY not available — demo video has no voiceover\n- Demo video at .claude-beat/logs/demos/demo_2026-03-20.mp4 (5 scenes, ~1:51)\n- SQLite DB stored in `app/data/` (gitignored)\n- DB tables: users, listings, listing_members, listing_applications, user_genres, notifications, ratings, telegram_chats, pending_telegram_groups\n- Telegram bot integration requires TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN env var (see issue #21)\n- Discord bot integration requires DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN + DISCORD_CLIENT_ID + DISCORD_WEBHOOK_SECRET env vars\n- Listings have `platform_preference` field (telegram/discord)\n- Email notifications require SMTP env vars (SMTP_HOST, SMTP_PORT, SMTP_FROM, etc.)\n- nodemailer added as dependency for email sending\n- Session secret: lazy runtime check (not module-level) to avoid build failure\n- ESLint: use eslint v9 + @eslint/js v9 (not v10) to avoid peer dep conflicts\n- npm ci works cleanly (no --legacy-peer-deps needed)\n- No root/sudo access on server — can't install nginx, Docker, or system packages\n- PM2 available via npx (not globally installed)\n\n## Test Suite\n- 198 tests across 22 test files, all passing\n- Tested: auth (login, register), health, listings (browse, detail, create, join, edit, delete, telegram, discord, auto-telegram, auto-discord), notifications, profile, profile/genres, profile/reading, profile/reputation, ratings, rate-limit, telegram/setup, telegram/webhook, discord/setup, discord/webhook\n- All API routes now have test coverage\n- @vitest/coverage-v8 installed for coverage reporting\n\n## Components\n- `BookCover` — reusable book cover component with error fallback, uses `unoptimized` to bypass Next.js image proxy for Open Library covers that redirect through archive.org\n\n## Next Session Priority\n1. **URGENT**: PAT expires ~March 27 (#54) — ~3 days remaining, still needs-human\n2. Check issue #21 for Telegram token response\n3. Check production health\n4. All routes tested, 11 rounds of code review done — consider E2E testing, performance optimization, or new feature proposals\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":44,"oldLines":11,"newStart":44,"newLines":11,"lines":[" - None"," "," ## Closed Recently","+- #105: Code review round 15 — LIKE injection, auth bypass, unbounded queries, transporter cache — merged + deployed","+- #103: Code review round 14 — module-load freezing, stale sessions, TOCTOU races, type validation — merged + deployed","+- #101: Code review round 13 — PATCH is_full gap, transaction safety, orphaned applications — merged + deployed"," - #96: Code review round 11 — stale transaction data in approve, unmount cleanup, duplicate notifications — merged + deployed"," - #95: Code review round 10 — FK violations, stale notifications, input validation, error UX — merged + deployed","-- #93: Code review round 9 — client-side race conditions, date bugs, error handling, ARIA — merged + deployed","-- #91: Code review round 8 — timing-safe secrets, type validation, stale transaction data, email HTML escaping — merged + deployed","-- #87: Code review round 6 — logout fix, is_full reset, email subjects, webhook validation, fetch abort — merged + deployed"," "," ## Open Issues"," - #21: Telegram bot token (needs-human) — multiple follow-up reminders sent, still waiting"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

Test Suite\n- 198 tests across 22 test files, all passing","newString":"## Test Suite\n- 201 tests across 22 test files, all passing","originalFile":"# Agent Memory\n\n## Project\n- **Name**: Bookmate\n- **Repo**: https://github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate\n- **Project Board**: Bookmate (project #1 on TommyBrennan)\n- **Tech**: Next.js 15, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS v4, SQLite, iron-session\n- **Owner**: XanderZhu (human), TommyBrennan (GH account)\n\n## Current State\n- All PRD features implemented and merged (P0, P1, P2)\n- CI pipeline live: GitHub Actions runs lint + type check + build on push/PR to main\n- ESLint flat config with typescript-eslint, react-hooks, @next/eslint-plugin-next\n- Build passes clean, lint clean (no warnings)\n- Security hardening merged (PR #32, #36)\n- **Production deployed** via PM2 on port 3000 — http://89.167.127.85:3000\n- Health endpoint: `/api/health` (DB check, uptime, stats)\n- Custom 404 page (branded, with CTA)\n- OG + Twitter Card meta tags on listing pages (book cover, title, description)\n- Loading skeletons on all routes (root, listing detail, profile, notifications)\n- Error boundaries on all routes with retry functionality\n- Focus-visible styles for keyboard accessibility\n- **Accessibility improvements** (PR #49, merged + deployed):\n  - ARIA labels, roles, expanded states on all interactive elements\n  - Keyboard navigation on BookSearch dropdown (combobox pattern)\n  - autocomplete attributes on auth forms\n  - role=\"alert\" on error messages\n  - Skip-to-content link\n  - role=\"tablist\"/tab/tabpanel on profile tabs\n  - role=\"radiogroup\"/radio on format/platform buttons\n  - role=\"checkbox\" with keyboard support on approval toggle\n\n## Production\n- **URL**: http://89.167.127.85:3000\n- **Process manager**: PM2 (via npx)\n- **Config**: `app/ecosystem.config.js`\n- **Deploy script**: `scripts/deploy.sh`\n- **Logs**: `.claude-beat/logs/pm2-{out,error}.log`\n- **No reverse proxy** (nginx requires root — not available)\n- **No HTTPS** (no domain configured, no certbot)\n- PM2 process saved: `npx pm2 save` (restores on `pm2 resurrect`)\n\n## Open PRs\n- None\n\n## Closed Recently\n- #105: Code review round 15 — LIKE injection, auth bypass, unbounded queries, transporter cache — merged + deployed\n- #103: Code review round 14 — module-load freezing, stale sessions, TOCTOU races, type validation — merged + deployed\n- #101: Code review round 13 — PATCH is_full gap, transaction safety, orphaned applications — merged + deployed\n- #96: Code review round 11 — stale transaction data in approve, unmount cleanup, duplicate notifications — merged + deployed\n- #95: Code review round 10 — FK violations, stale notifications, input validation, error UX — merged + deployed\n\n## Open Issues\n- #21: Telegram bot token (needs-human) — multiple follow-up reminders sent, still waiting\n- #54: **PAT expires ~March 27** (needs-human) — GH_TOKEN renewal required, ~3 days left\n\n## Important Notes\n- GH_TOKEN loaded from `.claude-beat/.env`\n- Remote URL includes PAT: reset with `git remote set-url` if needed\n- Next.js 15 used (not 16) due to Turbopack /root permission issue\n- agent-browser WORKS if: export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp/runtime-$(id -u) && mkdir -p \"$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR\"\n- agent-browser crashes after extended use (memory pressure) — keep recording sessions short, restart between scenes\n- ffmpeg installed as static binary in ~/bin — add to PATH: export PATH=\"$HOME/bin:$PATH\"\n- ELEVENLABS_API_KEY not available — demo video has no voiceover\n- Demo video at .claude-beat/logs/demos/demo_2026-03-20.mp4 (5 scenes, ~1:51)\n- SQLite DB stored in `app/data/` (gitignored)\n- DB tables: users, listings, listing_members, listing_applications, user_genres, notifications, ratings, telegram_chats, pending_telegram_groups\n- Telegram bot integration requires TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN env var (see issue #21)\n- Discord bot integration requires DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN + DISCORD_CLIENT_ID + DISCORD_WEBHOOK_SECRET env vars\n- Listings have `platform_preference` field (telegram/discord)\n- Email notifications require SMTP env vars (SMTP_HOST, SMTP_PORT, SMTP_FROM, etc.)\n- nodemailer added as dependency for email sending\n- Session secret: lazy runtime check (not module-level) to avoid build failure\n- ESLint: use eslint v9 + @eslint/js v9 (not v10) to avoid peer dep conflicts\n- npm ci works cleanly (no --legacy-peer-deps needed)\n- No root/sudo access on server — can't install nginx, Docker, or system packages\n- PM2 available via npx (not globally installed)\n\n## Test Suite\n- 198 tests across 22 test files, all passing\n- Tested: auth (login, register), health, listings (browse, detail, create, join, edit, delete, telegram, discord, auto-telegram, auto-discord), notifications, profile, profile/genres, profile/reading, profile/reputation, ratings, rate-limit, telegram/setup, telegram/webhook, discord/setup, discord/webhook\n- All API routes now have test coverage\n- @vitest/coverage-v8 installed for coverage reporting\n\n## Components\n- `BookCover` — reusable book cover component with error fallback, uses `unoptimized` to bypass Next.js image proxy for Open Library covers that redirect through archive.org\n\n## Next Session Priority\n1. **URGENT**: PAT expires ~March 27 (#54) — ~3 days remaining, still needs-human\n2. Check issue #21 for Telegram token response\n3. Check production health\n4. All routes tested, 11 rounds of code review done — consider E2E testing, performance optimization, or new feature proposals\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":77,"oldLines":7,"newStart":77,"newLines":7,"lines":[" - PM2 available via npx (not globally installed)"," "," ## Test Suite","-- 198 tests across 22 test files, all passing","+- 201 tests across 22 test files, all passing"," - Tested: auth (login, register), health, listings (browse, detail, create, join, edit, delete, telegram, discord, auto-telegram, auto-discord), notifications, profile, profile/genres, profile/reading, profile/reputation, ratings, rate-limit, telegram/setup, telegram/webhook, discord/setup, discord/webhook"," - All API routes now have test coverage"," - @vitest/coverage-v8 installed for coverage reporting"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

Check issue #21 for Telegram token response\n3. Check production health\n4. All routes tested, 15 rounds of code review done — consider E2E testing, performance optimization, or new feature proposals\n5. ON DELETE CASCADE noted but deferred — manual deletion works, no user deletion feature exists","originalFile":"# Agent Memory\n\n## Project\n- **Name**: Bookmate\n- **Repo**: https://github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate\n- **Project Board**: Bookmate (project #1 on TommyBrennan)\n- **Tech**: Next.js 15, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS v4, SQLite, iron-session\n- **Owner**: XanderZhu (human), TommyBrennan (GH account)\n\n## Current State\n- All PRD features implemented and merged (P0, P1, P2)\n- CI pipeline live: GitHub Actions runs lint + type check + build on push/PR to main\n- ESLint flat config with typescript-eslint, react-hooks, @next/eslint-plugin-next\n- Build passes clean, lint clean (no warnings)\n- Security hardening merged (PR #32, #36)\n- **Production deployed** via PM2 on port 3000 — http://89.167.127.85:3000\n- Health endpoint: `/api/health` (DB check, uptime, stats)\n- Custom 404 page (branded, with CTA)\n- OG + Twitter Card meta tags on listing pages (book cover, title, description)\n- Loading skeletons on all routes (root, listing detail, profile, notifications)\n- Error boundaries on all routes with retry functionality\n- Focus-visible styles for keyboard accessibility\n- **Accessibility improvements** (PR #49, merged + deployed):\n  - ARIA labels, roles, expanded states on all interactive elements\n  - Keyboard navigation on BookSearch dropdown (combobox pattern)\n  - autocomplete attributes on auth forms\n  - role=\"alert\" on error messages\n  - Skip-to-content link\n  - role=\"tablist\"/tab/tabpanel on profile tabs\n  - role=\"radiogroup\"/radio on format/platform buttons\n  - role=\"checkbox\" with keyboard support on approval toggle\n\n## Production\n- **URL**: http://89.167.127.85:3000\n- **Process manager**: PM2 (via npx)\n- **Config**: `app/ecosystem.config.js`\n- **Deploy script**: `scripts/deploy.sh`\n- **Logs**: `.claude-beat/logs/pm2-{out,error}.log`\n- **No reverse proxy** (nginx requires root — not available)\n- **No HTTPS** (no domain configured, no certbot)\n- PM2 process saved: `npx pm2 save` (restores on `pm2 resurrect`)\n\n## Open PRs\n- None\n\n## Closed Recently\n- #105: Code review round 15 — LIKE injection, auth bypass, unbounded queries, transporter cache — merged + deployed\n- #103: Code review round 14 — module-load freezing, stale sessions, TOCTOU races, type validation — merged + deployed\n- #101: Code review round 13 — PATCH is_full gap, transaction safety, orphaned applications — merged + deployed\n- #96: Code review round 11 — stale transaction data in approve, unmount cleanup, duplicate notifications — merged + deployed\n- #95: Code review round 10 — FK violations, stale notifications, input validation, error UX — merged + deployed\n\n## Open Issues\n- #21: Telegram bot token (needs-human) — multiple follow-up reminders sent, still waiting\n- #54: **PAT expires ~March 27** (needs-human) — GH_TOKEN renewal required, ~3 days left\n\n## Important Notes\n- GH_TOKEN loaded from `.claude-beat/.env`\n- Remote URL includes PAT: reset with `git remote set-url` if needed\n- Next.js 15 used (not 16) due to Turbopack /root permission issue\n- agent-browser WORKS if: export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp/runtime-$(id -u) && mkdir -p \"$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR\"\n- agent-browser crashes after extended use (memory pressure) — keep recording sessions short, restart between scenes\n- ffmpeg installed as static binary in ~/bin — add to PATH: export PATH=\"$HOME/bin:$PATH\"\n- ELEVENLABS_API_KEY not available — demo video has no voiceover\n- Demo video at .claude-beat/logs/demos/demo_2026-03-20.mp4 (5 scenes, ~1:51)\n- SQLite DB stored in `app/data/` (gitignored)\n- DB tables: users, listings, listing_members, listing_applications, user_genres, notifications, ratings, telegram_chats, pending_telegram_groups\n- Telegram bot integration requires TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN env var (see issue #21)\n- Discord bot integration requires DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN + DISCORD_CLIENT_ID + DISCORD_WEBHOOK_SECRET env vars\n- Listings have `platform_preference` field (telegram/discord)\n- Email notifications require SMTP env vars (SMTP_HOST, SMTP_PORT, SMTP_FROM, etc.)\n- nodemailer added as dependency for email sending\n- Session secret: lazy runtime check (not module-level) to avoid build failure\n- ESLint: use eslint v9 + @eslint/js v9 (not v10) to avoid peer dep conflicts\n- npm ci works cleanly (no --legacy-peer-deps needed)\n- No root/sudo access on server — can't install nginx, Docker, or system packages\n- PM2 available via npx (not globally installed)\n\n## Test Suite\n- 201 tests across 22 test files, all passing\n- Tested: auth (login, register), health, listings (browse, detail, create, join, edit, delete, telegram, discord, auto-telegram, auto-discord), notifications, profile, profile/genres, profile/reading, profile/reputation, ratings, rate-limit, telegram/setup, telegram/webhook, discord/setup, discord/webhook\n- All API routes now have test coverage\n- @vitest/coverage-v8 installed for coverage reporting\n\n## Components\n- `BookCover` — reusable book cover component with error fallback, uses `unoptimized` to bypass Next.js image proxy for Open Library covers that redirect through archive.org\n\n## Next Session Priority\n1. **URGENT**: PAT expires ~March 27 (#54) — ~3 days remaining, still needs-human\n2. Check issue #21 for Telegram token response\n3. Check production health\n4. All routes tested, 11 rounds of code review done — consider E2E testing, performance optimization, or new feature proposals\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":89,"oldLines":4,"newStart":89,"newLines":5,"lines":[" 1. **URGENT**: PAT expires ~March 27 (#54) — ~3 days remaining, still needs-human"," 2. Check issue #21 for Telegram token response"," 3. Check production health","-4. All routes tested, 11 rounds of code review done — consider E2E testing, performance optimization, or new feature proposals","+4. All routes tested, 15 rounds of code review done — consider E2E testing, performance optimization, or new feature proposals","+5. ON DELETE CASCADE noted but deferred — manual deletion works, no user deletion feature exists"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_014WYVGk7g3FQjZ58dn3ZEte","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Session complete. Here's the summary:\n\n## Session 2026-03-24 12:00 UTC — Complete\n\n### What I did\n**Code review round 15** — found and fixed 6 bugs (issue #104, PR #105):\n\n1. **Auth bypass in auto-discord** — used `getSession()` instead of `requireAuth()`, bypassing DB user-existence verification\n2. **LIKE wildcard injection** — search terms containing `%` or `_` acted as SQL wildcards; `%` matched every listing\n3. **Unbounded mark-all-read** — bulk UPDATE with no limit could lock SQLite WAL; bounded to 1000 rows\n4. **Unbounded reading history** — no LIMIT on query; added LIMIT 200\n5. **ORDER BY hardening** — replaced if/else with explicit allowlist map\n6. **Transporter cache stale** — nodemailer transporter never rebuilt when SMTP env vars changed; added config hash invalidation\n\n### Status\n- ✅ All 201 tests pass, ESLint clean, build clean, CI green\n- ✅ Merged and deployed to production\n- ✅ Production healthy (HTTP 200, PM2 online)\n- ⚠️ PAT expiry #54 — ~3 days remaining, needs human action\n- ⚠️ Telegram token #21 — still awaiting human"}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":321,"cache_read_input_tokens":50243,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":321},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"2461f1b3-284a-46b0-accd-df8549bf2929","uuid":"e5ded15a-8cfb-4f17-b5ea-a4b087a551a5"}

Here's the summary:\n\n## Session 2026-03-24 12:00 UTC — Complete\n\n### What I did\n**Code review round 15** — found and fixed 6 bugs (issue #104, PR #105):\n\n1. **Auth bypass in auto-discord** — used `getSession()` instead of `requireAuth()`, bypassing DB user-existence verification\n2. **LIKE wildcard injection** — search terms containing `%` or `_` acted as SQL wildcards; `%` matched every listing\n3. **Unbounded mark-all-read** — bulk UPDATE with no limit could lock SQLite WAL; bounded to 1000 rows\n4. **Unbounded reading history** — no LIMIT on query; added LIMIT 200\n5. **ORDER BY hardening** — replaced if/else with explicit allowlist map\n6. **Transporter cache stale** — nodemailer transporter never rebuilt when SMTP env vars changed;
